sites.name: Remodeling & Renovation Services
sites.dataKeywords: faq_what_is_included_in_quote What information do I need to provide to get an accurate remodeling quote? To receive an accurate quote, we recommend providing: room type (e.g., kitchen, bathroom), approximate square footage or room count, current condition, desired scope of work (e.g., full remodel, cosmetic update), target budget, and preferred timeline. Our quote forms will guide you to enter these details, including fields like number of fixtures for bathrooms or square footage for flooring. quotes faq_is_there_minimum_budget Is there a minimum or maximum budget required to request a quote? No. You can request a quote for projects of various sizes. Our quote forms include a budget range so you can specify an upper limit, such as $9,000 for a bathroom remodel or $5,000 for roof repair. We’ll tailor recommendations and service options based on the budget you enter. faq_how_to_schedule_consultation How do I schedule a design consultation for my kitchen remodel? sites.name: Belchatow Memorial — Site Framework
sites.dataKeywords: he_1820_weaving_workshops Early Weaving Workshops Established in Bełchatów Small family-run weaving workshops mark the beginning of Bełchatów's industrial development. In the early 19th century, a number of family-run weaving workshops were established in Bełchatów. These enterprises laid the foundations for the town’s later transformation into a regional textile center. Archival records indicate that by 1820 several dozen looms operated in wooden houses around the market square. Bełchatów industry textiles economy 19th_century he_1853_textile_mill Opening of the First Larger Textile Mill in Bełchatów A small steam-powered textile mill begins operation, increasing production capacity in Bełchatów. In 1853 a small but modern textile mill was opened near the center of Bełchatów. Employing several dozen workers, the mill introduced more organized production methods and marked the town’s shift from cottage industry to factory-based textile production. This creamy nettles soup is one of the easiest ways to turn a basket of fresh spring nettles into a nourishing meal. In this recipe we'll cover how to safely handle nettles, how to blanch them to remove the sting, and how to build a rich flavour base with onions, garlic and potatoes.

Safety note: always wear gloves when harvesting and handling raw nettles. Once blanched, the sting disappears.

Serve this soup with crusty bread, a drizzle of olive oil, and a squeeze of lemon. nettles soup spring recipe vegetarian easy 200 g young nettle tops, rinsed 1 tbsp olive oil 1 onion, chopped 2 cloves garlic, sliced 2 medium potatoes, diced 1 L vegetable stock 100 ml cream or coconut milk Salt and pepper to taste Lemon wedges, to serve Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat. mpa_senate_outreach_2026 Protect Working Waterfronts in New Marine Protected Areas Tell your Senators to support balanced marine protected areas that protect both ecosystems and fishing communities. Federal agencies are considering new marine protected areas (MPAs) that could close large sections of productive fishing grounds. While conservation is essential, these proposals must account for the livelihoods of commercial fishermen and coastal communities.

This campaign urges Senators to push for science-based MPAs that:
- Clearly define conservation objectives
- Include fishermen in the planning process
- Allow sustainable commercial fishing where compatible with conservation goals
- Provide adequate transition support where access is reduced

Use the form on this page to send a tailored message to your Senators. You can customize the text to reflect your fishery, gear type, and community. marine_protected_areas active Support balanced marine protected areas that keep fishermen workin

event_3dp_beginner_evening_1 Intro to 3D Printing: Beginner Workshop (Evening) Get started with 3D printers after class Beginner-friendly evening workshop covering 3D printer basics, safety, and how to submit your first print. This beginner-friendly workshop introduces you to the 3D printers available in the library makerspace. We will cover basic safety, how to prepare simple models for printing, approved materials, and how to submit your first print job to the queue.

No prior experience is required. This session is designed especially for students who have never used a 3D printer before.

Topics include:
- 3D printer safety and policies
- Supported file formats and where to find starter models
- Basic slicing settings (layer height, infill, supports)
- How to submit a print request through the library system

You’ll leave with the knowledge needed to safely and successfully request your first 3D print. 3d_printing beginner students in_person free America/New_York Makerspace Lab 101

sites.name: Water Born - Web Series
sites.dataKeywords: mara Mara Vance Resourceful strategist who emerged as an unlikely leader after the blackout. Mara Vance grew up on the industrial edges of New Harbor, teaching herself systems engineering from salvaged tech. When the citywide blackout shattered the old order, she became the de facto strategist for a fracturing resistance, balancing moral lines against impossible choices. Her arc in Seasons 1 and 2 charts her evolution from quiet analyst to public symbol of the rebellion, with a growing rift between her and Ezra over what sacrifices are acceptable. ezra Ezra Kade Frontline tactician whose loyalty to Mara is tested by the uprising’s cost. Ezra Kade is a former city warden who defected after discovering the regime’s secret surveillance program. Charismatic and stubborn, he quickly became the face of the rebellion’s operations, leading dangerous raids into the core districts. Starting this month, the Riverton Transit Authority is increasing weekend bus frequency on several popular routes. Public transportation riders will see shorter wait times on Saturdays and Sundays, especially between downtown and the east side. Officials say the goal is to make it easier for workers, students, and families to rely on buses instead of cars. weekend at the retreat center introducing core temple practices. meals included shared lodging faq_wheelchair_access_01 Is your retreat center wheelchair accessible? Yes. Our main temple, dining hall, and most guest rooms are wheelchair accessible. We have step-free access to common areas, ramps at building entrances, and at least two fully accessible ground-floor rooms with roll-in showers. If you use a wheelchair or mobility aid, please let us know on your booking form so we can reserve an appropriate room and parking spot. pages.dataKeywords: art_irs_enf_20260225 IRS Enforcement Snapshot: High-Risk Schedules on 2025 Returns A practitioner-focused overview of current IRS enforcement campaigns targeting high-risk schedules filed for 2025 individual and business returns. The IRS has announced new enforcement campaigns focusing on high-risk schedules, including Schedule C losses, large Schedule A charitable deductions, and international information reporting forms. Practitioners should review their internal review procedures, ensure contemporaneous documentation is maintained, and proactively communicate with clients who have historically triggered correspondence exams... article irs_enforcement audit schedule_c individual_tax Dana McAllister, EA irs-enforcement-snapshot-high-risk-schedules-2025-returns published art_irs_enf_20260210 Field Examination Reboot: What Small Firms Need to Know in 2026 IRS field exam procedures are evolving with more data analytics and centralized case selection. Learn how to prepare your small firm. Beginning in 2026, the IRS is rolling out updated field examination procedures that rely more heavily on pre-contact analytics and centralized case building. This article summarizes the new IRM guidance, outlines likely triggers for field exams, and provides a preparation checklist you can adapt for your firm... field_exam small_firm practice_management Michael Tran, CPA field-examination-reboot-small-firms-2026 art_irs_enf_20260105 IRS Collections: Fresh Start Thresholds and Installment Agreements in 2026 Updated IRS collection thresholds affect when your clients qualify for streamlined installment agreements and other Fresh Start relief. The IRS has quietly updated several internal thresholds used to determine eligibility for streamlined installment agreements and other Fresh Start initiative programs. While the formal dollar limits have not changed in the Code, internal tolerances and documentation requirements have shifted. This enforcement update breaks down the new thresholds and provides sample client scenarios... collections installment_agreements representation Laura Chen, JD, LLM irs-collections-fresh-start-thresholds-2026 art_irs_enf_20251001 LB&I Campaigns Targeting Cross-Border Partnerships A deeper look at LB&I enforcement campaigns involving complex cross-border partnership structures. GroundCore delivered detailed basement excavation for a two-level underground car park on Clarendon Street, including temporary propping, shotcrete walls and dewatering. pages.dataKeywords: slope_stability_retaining_walls Slope Stability & Retaining Walls slope-stability-retaining-walls service_slope_stability.html geotechnical_engineering Advanced slope stability assessment, landslide remediation, and retaining wall design for transportation, infrastructure, and site development projects. Our slope stability and retaining wall team provides end‑to‑end support from preliminary screening through detailed numerical modeling and construction support. Services include limit‑equilibrium and finite element stability analyses, retaining wall type selection and design, landslide and embankment remediation concepts, seismic stability evaluations, and independent design reviews. subsurface_investigation Subsurface Investigation subsurface-investigation service_subsurface_investigation.html Drilling, in‑situ testing, and laboratory programs tailored to support foundation, earthworks, and infrastructure design. We scope and execute subsurface investigation programs ranging from small building sites to regional transportation corridors. Our capabilities include rotary wash drilling, hollow‑stem auger borings, CPT soundings, pressuremeter testing, geophysics, and comprehensive laboratory testing. We emphasize investigation programs targeted to specific design decisions and risk levels. deep_foundations Deep Foundations & Piling deep-foundations-piling service_deep_foundations.html Design and testing of pile foundations, drilled shafts, and ground anchors for heavy structures. We provide feasibility studies, preliminary sizing, and detailed design for driven piles, drilled shafts, micropiles, and ground anchors. Our engineers routinely support static and dynamic load testing, PDA monitoring, integrity testing, and construction phase troubleshooting for complex deep foundation systems. ground_improvement Ground Improvement & Earthworks ground-improvement-earthworks service_ground_improvement.html Ground improvement concepts, specification development, and performance verification for challenging sites. We develop ground improvement solutions such as stone columns, deep soil mixing, preload and wick drains, vibro‑compaction, and soil replacement to control settlement and liquefaction. Our involvement spans concept screening, performance‑based design, instrumentation planning, and construction monitoring. pkg_bldg_standard_borings Standard Building Foundation Borings standard-building-foundation-borings Baseline subsurface investigation for low‑ to mid‑rise building foundations using soil borings and SPT testing. building_foundations Up to 4 soil borings to 15 m depth Standard Penetration Test (SPT) at 1.5 m intervals Groundwater level observations Basic index testing (moisture content, Atterberg limits) Summary geotechnical recommendations memo Suitable for small commercial or light industrial buildings on relatively uniform soils. pkg_bldg_cpt_consol_compact Building Foundation CPT & Consolidation Package building-foundation-cpt-consolidation-packa

Good communication is less about saying the perfect words and more about having regular spaces to talk.

Try a weekly check-in where each person shares one win, one challenge, and one small request. Use a timer so each partner gets equal space. Focus on listening to understand, not to defend.

pages.dataKeywords: pause_membership_progress Can I pause my membership without losing my progress data? membership pause membership freeze account hold membership progress data freeze hold suspend keep progress streak vacation published cancel_membership How do I cancel my membership? You can cancel anytime from your account settings. Go to Settings → Membership → Manage plan and click “Cancel membership”. Your access will continue until the end of your current billing period. You will receive a confirmation email once the cancellation is processed. cancel membership end subscription cancel stop subscription end membership close account trial_billing Will I be charged at the end of my free trial? If you start a free trial on any paid plan, you won’t be charged until your trial ends. We’ll send you a reminder email 3 days before your trial expires. If you don’t cancel, your membership will automatically convert to a paid subscription at the plan’s standard price. billing free trial charges trial charge when am I billed subscription start refund_policy What is your refund policy? For monthly plans we do not offer prorated refunds, but you will keep access until the end of your billing period. For annual plans, we offer a 14-day money-back guarantee on your first purchase. If you’re not satisfied, contact support within 14 days and we’ll refund your payment in full. refund money back refund policy money back guarantee cancel annual prorated beginner_workouts Do you have beginner-friendly, no-equipment home workouts? Yes. You can filter our workout library by level (Beginner), equipment (No equipment), and location (Home) to find beginner-friendly home plans. Many of these offer 3 or more sessions per week with step-by-step video guidance. workouts beginner no equipment home workouts beginner workouts home training bodyweight live_class_recordings Are live classes recorded so I can watch them later? Most of our live classes are recorded and available for replay within 24 hours. Look for the “Replay available” badge on the class details page to confirm that a recording will be provided. live_classes live classes recordings replay recorded classes class replay on-demand live stream recording technical_requirements What are the technical requirements to use the app? Our web app works in modern browsers such as Chrome, Safari, Edge, and Firefox. For the best experience, we recommend a stable internet connection of at least 5 Mbps and enabling JavaScript and cookies. Mobile apps are available for iOS and Android 11 or newer. technical technical requirements devices browsers system requirements supported devices supported browsers internet speed weight_loss_guidance Can the app help me calculate calories for weight loss? Yes. Use the built-in Calorie & Macro Calculator to estimate your daily calorie target based on your age, gender, weight, height, activity level, and goal (for example, losing 0.5 kg per week). You can then email the plan to yourself and sync it with support

pages.dataKeywords: kit_water_purification_tablets Water purification tablets (50-count) water_beverages Tablets for disinfecting untreated water in emergencies. bottle Use according to instructions; wait time typically 30 minutes. kit_bottled_water_case_1gal Bottled water, 1-gallon jugs (3-pack) Sealed 1-gallon jugs of drinking water. pack Plan for at least 1 gallon per person per day. kit_electrolyte_powder_packets Electrolyte drink powder packets Single-serve electrolyte packets to mix with clean water. Helpful for heat-related emergencies and illness. kit_emergency_water_pouches_12pack Emergency drinking water pouches (12-pack) Long-shelf-life foil water pouches. Store in a cool, dark place; check expiration dates. kit_instant_oatmeal_packets Instant oatmeal packets (10-pack) food_nutrition Individual oatmeal packets that can be prepared with hot water. Choose low-sugar options when possible. kit_canned_beans_3pack Canned beans (3-pack) Ready-to-eat canned beans with easy-open lids. Include a manual can opener if cans are not easy-open. kit_peanut_butter_16oz Peanut butter (16 oz jar) High-calorie, shelf-stable protein source. Check for nut allergies in the household. kit_granola_bars_12count Granola bars (12-count) Individually wrapped snack bars for quick calories. Choose varieties that meet dietary needs (gluten-free, etc.). kit_ready_meals_3pack Ready-to-eat emergency meal pouches (3-pack) Shelf-stable meals that can be eaten hot or cold. Some kits include heating elements; follow instructions carefully. kit_pill_organizer_7day 7-day pill organizer health_medications Weekly pill organizer for managing daily medications. each Label clearly and update when medications change. kit_pain_reliever_acetaminophen Pain reliever (acetaminophen) Over-the-counter pain and fever reducer. Follow dosing instructions; keep out of reach of children. kit_n95_masks_10pack N95 respirator masks (10-pack) NIOSH-approved N95 respirators for smoke, dust, and illness protection. Fit must be tight to be effective; not sized for young children. kit_basic_first_aid_kit Basic first aid kit Includes bandages, antiseptic wipes, gauze, and basic supplies. Customize with personal medications and supplies. kit_emergency_whistle Emergency whistle safety_tools Loud safety whistle for signaling in emergencies. Attach to a lanyard or go-bag for quick access. kit_duct_tape_roll Duct tape (roll) Multi-purpose duct tape for temporary repairs and sealing. roll Useful for shelter-in-place sealing and basic repairs. kit_work_gloves_pair Work gloves (pair) Durable gloves to protect hands during cleanup. pair Choose appropriate sizes for each adult. kit_led_flashlight_batteries LED flashlight with batteries Battery-powered LED flashlight for power outages. Store extra batteries in original packaging. kit_wet_wipes_40count Wet wipes (40-count) hygiene_comfort Moist wipes for basic cleaning when water is limited. Avoid flushing; dispose of wipes in trash. kit_toilet_tissue_4roll Toilet tissue (4-roll pack

du_2024_11_early_access_announce Announcing Early Access Roadmap and Community Hub Overview of our early access plans, roadmap structure, and how you can participate through the new community hub. announcement roadmap community updates/early-access-roadmap-and-community-hub.html du_2025_01_15_alpha_combat_update Alpha Combat Update: New Abilities and Enemy Behaviors Details on the first major combat update in alpha, including new abilities, smarter enemies, and roadmap notes. patch_notes combat alpha updates/alpha-combat-update-abilities-enemies.html du_2025_03_02_crafting_deep_dive Crafting System Deep Dive and Upcoming Changes A look at how crafting works under the hood and what improvements are planned based on your feedback. crafting design_blog systems updates/crafting-system-deep-dive.html du_2025_04_10_performance_roadmap Performance Optimization Roadmap for PC and Consoles Outlining our performance optimization goals across PC, PS5, and Xbox Series X|S for 2025. performance xbox updates/performance-optimization-roadmap-2025.html du_2025_06_01_coop_trailer First Look: Co-op Gameplay Trailer and Feature Highlights Watch the first co-op gameplay trailer and learn what to expect from shared progression and matchmaking. Our first co-op gameplay trailer is live, showcasing four-player dungeon runs, shared loot rules, and synchronized boss mechanics. The trailer highlights how different roles can combine abilities to create powerful synergies, especially in late-game challenge modes.

We have also updated the Roadmap with new co-op features across PC, PS5, and Xbox Series X|S. These entries include improved matchmaking, cross-platform invites, and special co-op-only events arriving later in 2025. co-op trailer multiplayer updates/coop-gameplay-trailer-highlights.html du_2025_08_20_xbox_tech_preview Xbox Series X|S Technical Preview Details Information on how to join the Xbox Series X|S technical preview and what features are included. preview console updates/xbox-series-xs-technical-preview.html du_2025_10_05_crossplay_beta Cross-Play Beta and Account Linking Cross-play beta opens with support for PC, PS5, and Xbox Series X|S, along with new account linking options. cross-play beta updates/cross-play-beta-and-account-linking.html du_2025_12_18_holiday_patch Holiday Patch 0.8.9: Events, Rewards, and Stability Fixes Patch notes for the holiday update introducing limited-time events, reward tracks, and several stability improvements. holiday_event stability updates/patch-0-8-9-holiday-event-and-stability.html du_2026_01_10_hotfix_093_performance Hotfix 0.9.3: Stability and Performance Improvements A small hotfix addressing crashes and incremental performance improvements ahead of larger updates. hotfix updates/hotfix-0-9-3-stability-and-performance.html du_2026_01_25_lategame_weapon_balance Design Blog: Late-Game Weapon Balance Philosophy Explaining how we approach late-game weapon damage, scaling, and counterplay. gameplay_balance weapons updates/

pages.dataKeywords: role_novapay_product_designer Product Designer NovaPay fintech Remote · Berlin, Germany Led end-to-end product design for a mobile-first digital wallet used across the EU market. Owned UX and UI for NovaPay consumer and merchant apps Conducted generative research and usability testing with early adopters Collaborated with PMs and engineers to prioritize feature roadmap Defined and documented interaction patterns for core money flows Increased successful top-ups by 18% after redesigning the funding flow Reduced onboarding time from 7 minutes to under 3 minutes Contributed to Series A fundraising by providing product design demos role_flowbank_senior_ux_designer Senior UX Designer FlowBank Zurich, Switzerland Senior UX lead for a multi-platform banking experience, including web and native apps. Led UX strategy for consumer and SME banking dashboards Mapped complex financial journeys into clear, actionable flows Partnered with compliance and legal teams to design compliant experiences Mentored junior designers on research and interaction design best practices Increased monthly active users by 22% after dashboard simplification Reduced customer support tickets related to transfers by 35% Introduced a reusable pattern library adopted across 4 product squads role_luma_finance_lead_product_designer Lead Product Designer Luma Finance Remote · London, UK Leading product design for a B2B lending platform, from discovery to delivery. Set the product design vision and roadmap in partnership with the CPO Conducted discovery research with lenders and SMB borrowers Designed decisioning workflows and underwriting tools for internal teams Established a design system to align web app and internal tools Improved loan application completion rate by 27% after form redesign Shortened underwriting review time by 15% with optimized dashboards Built an experimentation framework for continuous UX improvements role_quicktap_ux_consultant UX Consultant QuickTap Pay Barcelona, Spain Short-term UX engagement to optimize in-store payment flows for a contactless POS startup. Audited existing POS flows and identified friction points Ran usability tests with retail staff and store managers Designed optimized tap-to-pay and refund experiences Delivered a prioritized UX improvement backlog to founders Reduced average checkout time by 11% for pilot merchants Improved task success rate for refunds from 63% to 92% role_clarify_saas_product_designer Clarify Analytics saas Remote · Austin, TX, USA Designed core analytics workflows for a B2B SaaS platform serving operations teams. Created dashboards and reporting UIs for non-technical users Collaborated with data science to visualize predictive insights Ran iterative usability tests on key reporting features Documented interaction patterns for complex filter controls Increased dashboard engagement (time-on-dashboard) by 19% Reduced time-to-first-report for new users from 2 weeks to 3 days role_orbit_agency_ux_ui_designer UX/UI Designer O

pages.dataKeywords: faq_what_is_included_in_quote What information do I need to provide to get an accurate remodeling quote? To receive an accurate quote, we recommend providing: room type (e.g., kitchen, bathroom), approximate square footage or room count, current condition, desired scope of work (e.g., full remodel, cosmetic update), target budget, and preferred timeline. Our quote forms will guide you to enter these details, including fields like number of fixtures for bathrooms or square footage for flooring. quotes faq_is_there_minimum_budget Is there a minimum or maximum budget required to request a quote? No. You can request a quote for projects of various sizes. Our quote forms include a budget range so you can specify an upper limit, such as $9,000 for a bathroom remodel or $5,000 for roof repair. We’ll tailor recommendations and service options based on the budget you enter. faq_how_to_schedule_consultation How do I schedule a design consultation for my kitchen remodel? From the Kitchens section, select a design consultation service and click the “Schedule Consultation” button. You’ll then choose an available date and time, including weekday evening slots, and fill in your contact information and project notes before confirming the appointment. bookings faq_change_or_cancel_booking Can I change or cancel a booked appointment? Yes. After booking, you’ll receive a confirmation email with a link to manage your appointment. You can reschedule to another available date and time or cancel, subject to our rescheduling and cancellation policies outlined in your confirmation. faq_project_timeline How long do typical remodeling projects take? Timelines vary by scope: many bathroom remodels take 2–4 weeks once work begins, kitchen remodels often take 4–8 weeks, and interior painting for several rooms usually takes 2–5 days. Our quote and booking forms allow you to indicate preferences such as completion within 3 months, and we’ll confirm a more precise schedule after an on-site visit. timelines faq_earliest_appointment_rules How far in advance can I schedule an appointment? You can typically schedule design consultations and service appointments several weeks in advance. Our online scheduler only shows dates that are at least a few days out, and in some cases we require bookings at least 7 days in advance to allow time for planning and material coordination. faq_warranty_coverage What kind of warranties do you offer on your services? Warranty coverage depends on the service. Many roof repair services include at least a 5-year workmanship warranty, while other services such as flooring or painting may have 1–3 year warranties. Warranty details are listed on each service page and summarized during checkout. warranties faq_free_inspection_roofing Do roof repair services include a free inspection? Several of our roof repair options include a free inspection, clearly labeled as such in the Roofing category filters. When you select the “Free inspection” option, the results

pages.dataKeywords:
dev_anxiety_casting_cares Casting Your Cares on Him casting-your-cares-on-him Name your worries and place them into the caring hands of Jesus, who invites you to cast every anxiety on Him. anxiety peace trust hope 1 Peter 5:6-7 Matthew 11:28-30 Sarah Thompson published dev_anxiety_be_still Be Still in the Middle of the Storm be-still-in-the-middle-of-the-storm In anxious moments, God invites you to stillness rooted in the unshakable truth of who He is. rest presence_of_god Psalm 46:1-11 Mark 4:35-41 Daniel Kim dev_anxiety_midnight_prayers Midnight Prayers for the Anxious Heart midnight-prayers-for-the-anxious-heart When anxiety keeps you awake, turn your "what ifs" into prayers and let God’s promises sing you back to rest. night prayer Psalm 4:8 Psalm 121:1-8 Job 35:10 Emily Rivera dev_anxiety_truth_over_feelings Choosing Truth Over Feelings choosing-truth-over-feelings Feelings can be loud, but God’s promises are louder. Let truth, not anxiety, have the final word. identity renewing_mind Romans 12:2 2 Corinthians 10:3-5 Michael Lee dev_anxiety_not_alone You Are Not Alone in Your Anxiety you-are-not-alone-in-your-anxiety Anxiety thrives in isolation, but God meets you through His Word and His people so you don’t have to walk alone. community encouragement 1 Kings 19:1-8 2 Corinthians 1:3-4 Galatians 6:2 Grace Mitchell dev_marriage_serving_spouse Serving Your Spouse Like Christ serving-your-spouse-like-christ Christlike love in marriage looks like daily, practical service. Jesus washed His disciples’ feet, demonstrating that greatness in His kingdom is found in humble love.

Ask the Lord to show you one specific way to serve your spouse today without expecting anything in return. As you do, you mirror the heart of Christ in your home. Humble, practical service is a powerful way to display Christ’s love in your marriage. marriage service love John 13:1-17 Philippians 2:3-5 Rachel Owens dev_forgiveness_roots Pulling Up the Roots of Bitterness pulling-up-the-roots-of-bitteress Unforgiveness grows like roots that choke out joy. God calls us to forgive as we have been forgiven in Christ. This does not minimize the pain we’ve experienced, but it releases our hearts from the prison of bitterness.

Begin by honestly naming the hurt before God. Ask Him for the grace to move toward forgiveness, even if your feelings are not there yet. Forgiveness pulls up the roots of bitterness so God’s grace can grow in their place. forgiveness bitterness healing Ephesians 4:31-32 Hebrews 12:14-15 Jonathan Perez dev_general_daily_bread Daily Bread for Today’s Needs daily-bread-for-todays-needs Jesus teaches us to pray for daily bread, not yearly storage. God’s provision often comes one day at a time, inviting us to trust Him in the present moment rather than hoarding for every possible future.

With a background in marriage and family therapy, she specializes in marriage counseling, communication, and relational healing. She also leads workshops for couples and mentors young families in the church. michelle.okafor@gracecommunitychurch.org (555) 201-4823 marriage_counseling marriage_and_family communication_issues conflict_resolution premarital_counseling pastor_david_chen David Chen David Chen Pastor David is the Lead Teaching Pastor, responsible for Sunday preaching, vision, and leadership development. pages.dataKeywords: natural-dyes-intro Slow Color: Introduction to Natural Dyes on Textiles slow-color-introduction-to-natural-dyes-on-textiles Natural dyes offer a slower, more considered approach to color on cloth. In this introduction, I cover the basics of cellulose vs. protein fibers, how to prepare fabric with simple mordants, and a few of my favorite starter plants for dyeing at home. We’ll walk through an easy first dye bath and discuss how to keep notes so you can reliably repeat your favorite hues. An introductory guide to setting up your first natural dye bath for textiles, from fabric preparation to simple plant-based colors. natural dyes technique beginner published indigo-iron-natural-blues Indigo and Iron: Deep Blues with Natural Dyes indigo-and-iron-deep-blues-with-natural-dyes Blue is one of the most sought-after colors in natural dyeing. This article explores working with indigo vats alongside iron modifiers to build depth and shadow into your textiles. I share my current vat recipe, safe handling practices, and a step-by-step process for creating layered shibori-inspired patterns on cotton and linen. Explore indigo vats, iron modifiers, and layered resist techniques for rich, complex blue textiles. indigo blue process botanical-neutrals-warm-browns Botanical Neutrals: Warm Browns from Natural Dye Baths botanical-neutrals-warm-browns-from-natural-dye-baths Warm neutrals are the quiet backbone of many of my wall hangings and cushions. In this post, I focus on extracting soft browns and caramels from walnut hulls, onion skins, and cutch. You’ll learn how to shift tones with pH changes, when to add iron for muted charcoals, and how I combine these colors in the studio for a cohesive palette. Learn to create a palette of warm, botanical browns using walnut, onion skins, and cutch on natural fibers. color palette warm tones natural-dye-troubleshooting Troubleshooting Natural Dyeing on Linen and Cotton troubleshooting-natural-dyeing-on-linen-and-cotton Uneven color, unexpected fading, or pale, washed-out hues are common challenges when dyeing cellulose fibers like linen and cotton. Here I break down the most frequent issues I see in student work, from skipped scouring to rushed mordanting. Each problem is paired with before-and-after photos and a checklist you can use the next time you set up a dye session. A practical checklist for resolving patchy color, fading, and other common natural dye problems on cellulose fibers. troubleshooting linen cotton studio-notes-january-2026 Studio Notes: January 2026 The start of the year is always a time for reorganizing the studio, reviewing last year’s collections, and sketching new work. In this entry I walk through my current loom setup, the yarns I’m most excited about, and how the Spring 2025 collection is informing a new series of smaller cushion covers. A behind-the-scenes look at the studio in January 2026, including loom setups and upcoming textile experiments. studio notes collections weaving-texture-loom-t

pages.dataKeywords:
prot_wheat_leaf_dna_96well_ht resources protocol High-throughput Wheat Leaf DNA Extraction in 96-well Plates Optimized wheat leaf tissue DNA extraction protocol for 96-well plate format, suitable for high-throughput genotyping labs.

High-throughput Wheat Leaf DNA Extraction (96-well)

This protocol describes a CTAB-based DNA extraction from young wheat leaf tissue using a 96-well plate format compatible with downstream SNP genotyping and sequencing workflows.

wheat global wheat leaf 96-well dna extraction high-throughput genotyping prot_maize_leaf_dna_384well Maize Leaf DNA Extraction in 384-well Plates High-density maize leaf DNA extraction workflow formatted for 384-well plates and robotics.

Maize Leaf DNA Extraction (384-well)

This protocol supports ultra-high-throughput DNA extraction for maize breeding pipelines using 384-well plates and automated liquid handling.

maize leaf tissue 384-well automation prot_wheat_ngs_library_prep Wheat Targeted Sequencing Library Preparation Step-by-step protocol for preparing wheat targeted sequencing libraries from plate-based DNA extractions.

Wheat Targeted Sequencing Library Prep

This protocol details multiplex PCR-based enrichment and library preparation for targeted sequencing panels in wheat.

targeted sequencing library prep prot_sorghum_seed_dna_simple Simple Sorghum Seed DNA Extraction for Marker-Assisted Selection Low-cost sorghum seed DNA extraction protocol for small breeding labs.

Sorghum Seed DNA Extraction

This protocol uses a simplified alkaline lysis method for sorghum seed DNA extraction suitable for MAS genotyping.

sorghum africa Ethiopia seed dna marker-assisted selection 24-tube rack prot_chickpea_leaf_dna_96well Chickpea Leaf DNA Extraction in 96-well Plates High-throughput DNA extraction protocol for chickpea leaf tissue using 96-well plates.

Chickpea 96-well Leaf DNA Extraction

Designed for custom SNP assay pipelines in chickpea, this protocol supports up to 10 plates per day per technician.

chickpea asia India article_mas_vs_gs_overview article Comparing Marker-Assisted Selection and Genomic Selection in Cereals A practical guide to choosing between MAS and GS in wheat, maize, and rice breeding programs.

MAS vs GS: Which Strategy Fits Your Program?

This article compares the cost, complexity, and ROI of MAS and GS in cereal breeding.

rice genomic selection roi calculator breeding strategy video_roi_calculator_tutorial video How to Use the MAS ROI Calculator Step-by-step walkthrough of the 5-year marker-assisted selection ROI calculator.

In this 15-minute video, we explain how to configure Scenario 1 and Scenario 2, interpret the ROI outputs, and save preferred scenarios.

tutorial cs_maize_kenya_gs_18pct_yield case_studies case_study Genomic Selection Delivers 18% Maize Yield Gain in Western Kenya A public-private maize breeding partnership in Kenya achieved an 18% yield increase using genomi

pages.dataKeywords: proj_asphalt_midtown_480 Midtown Asphalt Driveway Resurfacing – 2-Car Home Resurfaced a worn 2-car asphalt driveway with fresh overlay and clean edges. asphalt Midtown Atlanta, GA This 2-car driveway had extensive surface cracking and faded sealant. We milled high spots, repaired potholes, and applied a new 1.5" asphalt overlay with clean transitions to the sidewalk and garage slab. proj_asphalt_buckhead_720 Buckhead Hillside Asphalt Driveway Replacement Full-depth replacement of a sloped asphalt driveway with improved drainage. Buckhead, Atlanta, GA Removed the failing asphalt down to the base, regraded for better water runoff, compacted new aggregate, and installed a 3" asphalt driveway with crisp edging and a standard finish. proj_asphalt_decatur_650 Decatur Family Home Asphalt Driveway Upgrade New asphalt surface with widened parking pad for a growing family. Decatur, GA The original narrow driveway was replaced and widened to allow two vehicles side by side. Included base repairs, new asphalt installation, and standard finish suitable for everyday residential use. proj_asphalt_east_atlanta_350 East Atlanta Bungalow Asphalt Patch & Seal Smaller asphalt driveway with patch repairs and fresh sealcoat. East Atlanta, GA Performed localized patching of potholes and low spots, crack filling, and a two-coat seal over the existing asphalt surface to extend its life. proj_asphalt_sandy_springs_900 Sandy Springs Estate Asphalt Circle Drive Large circular asphalt driveway with guest parking area. Sandy Springs, GA Installed a new full-depth asphalt circle drive with integrated guest parking. Included under-drain installation and decorative border cuts. proj_concrete_suburban_600 Suburban Brushed Concrete Driveway – 600 sq ft Standard brushed concrete driveway with control joints and 4" slab. concrete Marietta, GA Removed old cracked concrete and installed a new 4" reinforced slab with broom finish, control joints, and a 3-year workmanship warranty. proj_concrete_inman_park_450 Inman Park Narrow Concrete Driveway Compact concrete driveway suited for a tight intown lot. Inman Park, Atlanta, GA Installed a reinforced concrete driveway with a slightly exposed aggregate border to match the historic character of the home. proj_concrete_vinings_900 Vinings Oversized Concrete Driveway Replacement Oversized driveway replacement with integrated walkway. Vinings, GA Complete tear-out and replacement of a large, cracked concrete driveway. Added a broom-finished walkway and improved apron at the street. proj_paver_brookhaven_550 Brookhaven Paver Driveway with Border Accent Concrete paver driveway with contrasting soldier-course border. paver Brookhaven, GA Excavated existing gravel, installed compacted base and bedding sand, and laid concrete pavers in a herringbone pattern with a darker border course. proj_paver_virginia_highland_800 Virginia-Highland Decorative Paver Drive Decorative paver driveway blending with historic brick home. Virginia-Highland, Atlanta, GA

Include data engineering, analytics, ML engineering, and MLOps experience. blog_ai_governance_playbook_2025_11 A 90-Day Playbook for Practical AI Governance 90-day-ai-governance-playbook A concise, action-oriented guide for standing up AI governance in under three months. This 90-day playbook outlines a pragmatic approach to AI governance that balances risk, innovation, and speed. We break the work into three 30-day phases: discovery, design, and rollout. Each phase includes specific deliverables such as a risk register, model inventory, and decision rights matrix.

By the end, you’ll have a lightweight AI governance framework in place that is anchored in real business decisions, not abstract policies. governance_ethics ai governance risk management compliance operating model published blog_ai_governance_board_2026_02 What Boards Need to Know About AI Governance in 2026 board-ai-governance-2026 Key questions directors should ask about AI risk, accountability, and metrics. Boards are being asked to sign off on increasingly ambitious AI roadmaps. Yet, many lack a clear view of AI risks and controls. This article offers a focused checklist of questions for directors: where AI is used, who owns the risk, what safeguards exist, and how performance is monitored.

We also outline how to embed AI governance into existing risk and audit committees rather than creating yet another siloed forum. board risk oversight strategy blog_responsible_genai_policies_2025_07 Designing Responsible GenAI Policies Without Slowing Innovation responsible-genai-policies How to craft practical guardrails for Generative AI that your teams will actually follow. Most GenAI policies fail because they are either too vague or too restrictive. In this piece we propose a middle path built around three tiers of use cases, simple approval workflows, and transparent risk ownership.

We share templates you can adapt, including an acceptable use policy, prompt hygiene checklist, and data classification guide tailored for GenAI. genai policy blog_ai_governance_operating_model_2024_09 Building an AI Governance Operating Model That Sca

pages.dataKeywords: cs_auto_de_stuttgart_paint_2026 case_studies Automotive Paint Shop Energy Optimization in Stuttgart automotive Germany Baden-Württemberg Stuttgart AutoWorks AG Comprehensive retrofit of paint shop ventilation and curing ovens delivering over 30% energy savings and improved uptime. Stuttgart AutoWorks AG partnered with our engineering team to modernize their paint shop. The project included high-efficiency variable-speed drives, heat recovery on curing ovens, and smart scheduling of ventilation systems. After a detailed audit and phased implementation, the plant achieved significant energy savings while reducing unplanned downtime and paint defects. 15% reduction in unplanned downtime 12% reduction in paint defects Improved operator working conditions automotive-paint-shop-energy-optimization-stuttgart-germany cs_auto_de_berlin_press_2025 Stamping Line Modernization at Berlin Components Plant Berlin Berlin Components GmbH Hydraulic press optimization and controls upgrade to reduce energy usage and extend equipment life. Berlin Components GmbH faced increasing maintenance costs and rising energy usage on their legacy stamping presses. Our team executed a phased controls retrofit, upgraded hydraulic power units with variable speed drives, and implemented condition-based monitoring on key components. The program yielded double-digit energy savings and fewer unplanned stoppages. 20% reduction in maintenance interventions Extended hydraulic component life Improved press availability stamping-line-modernization-berlin-automotive-plant cs_auto_de_munich_assembly_2024 Assembly Line Conveyor Optimization in Munich Bavaria Munich Mobility Systems Energy-efficient conveyor drives and smart controls deliver reduced power draw and smoother flow on final assembly. Munich Mobility Systems needed to cut operating costs on a high-throughput final assembly line. We replaced oversized motors with right-sized, high-efficiency units and introduced zone-based conveyor control. Operators gained better visibility into line performance while the plant reduced its overall energy intensity. Shorter restart times after stoppages Lower noise levels on assembly line Reduced component wear on conveyors assembly-line-conveyor-optimization-munich-automotive cs_mfg_us_oh_press_shop_2025 Hydraulic Press Reliability Program for Midwest Manufacturer manufacturing United States Ohio Midwest Metal Forming Inc. Condition monitoring and standardized maintenance procedures reduce unplanned press outages by nearly half. Midwest Metal Forming Inc. struggled with frequent unplanned stops on their hydraulic press lines. We implemented oil cleanliness monitoring, vibration analysis on critical components, and standardized preventive maintenance checklists. Downtime dropped significantly and maintenance planning improved. 45% reduction in unplanned outages Improved safety compliance Better spare parts planning hydraulic-press-reliability-program-ohio cs_logistics_us_il_warehouse_2024 Automated

pages.dataKeywords: US Dollar Euro British Pound faq_payment_options What payment options are available for buying this domain? You can purchase the domain using major credit and debit cards, PayPal, and in some cases bank transfer or wire. For Buy Now purchases, your payment is processed immediately. For lease-to-own plans, we set up an automatic monthly charge using your selected payment method. All payments are processed over an encrypted, secure checkout. payment_options payment-options faq_installments Can I pay for the domain in installments (lease-to-own)? Yes. If lease-to-own is available, you can choose a monthly payment plan during checkout. You will see several plan lengths with different monthly prices. After your final payment is completed, full ownership of the domain is transferred to you. You can also usually pay off the remaining balance early at any time. installment-plans faq_transfer_process How does the domain transfer process work after I pay? Once your payment is confirmed, we initiate the transfer to your preferred registrar. For most purchases, you will receive an email with either a transfer authorization code (EPP code) or push instructions for the seller’s registrar. After you follow those steps, the domain will appear in your account at the receiving registrar. Transfers typically complete within 1–5 business days, depending on the registrars involved. domain_transfer_process domain-transfer-process faq_transfer_timing How long will it take to receive the domain after I complete checkout? Most domain transfers are completed within a few hours to 1–2 business days after payment. In rare cases, such as cross-registrar transfers with additional verification requirements, it may take up to 5–7 business days. We will keep you updated by email until the domain appears in your registrar account. transfer-timing faq_refunds Do you offer refunds or cancellations after purchasing the domain? Because domain names are unique digital assets, completed sales are generally final and non-refundable once the transfer has started or been completed. In limited situations (for example, payment processing errors or if the seller cannot deliver the domain), we may issue a refund according to our Terms & Conditions. If you believe you qualify for a refund, please use the Contact Support form below to reach our team. refunds_cancellations refunds-and-cancellations faq_general_support I still have questions. How can I contact support? If you have any questions about payment options, lease plans, or the transfer process that are not answered here, you can contact our support team using the form in the Support section below. Provide as much detail as possible about the domain, your budget, and your timeline so we can assist you quickly. general contact-support prime_domain_broker Prime Domain Broker Independent domain brokerage specializing in premium brandable .com names and flexible lease-to-own options. sales@primedomainbroker.com +1-415-555-0123 elevate_digita

bacnet_integration_building_automation BACnet Integration Guide for Building Automation Dashboards bacnet-integration-building-automation-dashboards integration BACnet building_automation protocols Step-by-step instructions for connecting BACnet-compatible HVAC and lighting controllers to the building automation dashboard. modbus_integration_energy_meters Configuring Modbus Integration with Energy Meters modbus-integration-energy-meters Modbus energy_management Learn how to connect Modbus RTU/TCP energy meters to the platform for real-time consumption and demand monitoring. This article explains how to integrate Modbus-compatible energy meters with the energy management module. Topics covered include:

- Modbus RTU vs. Modbus TCP basics
- Wiring RS-485 networks and termination
- Adding a Modbus integration in the admin portal
- Importing device-specific register maps
- Scaling raw register values into engineering units (kWh, kW, Volts, Amps)
- Testing register reads and handling timeouts or CRC errors. bacnet_point_mapping_reference BACnet Point Mapping Reference bacnet-point-mapping-reference point_mapping Reference tables for mapping BACnet objects to logical points in the automation system. Use this reference when mapping BACnet objects into logical points. It includes recommended naming conventions, engineering units, and alarm thresholds for common HVAC and lighting objects. modbus_troubleshooting_checklist Modbus Communication Troubleshooting Checklist modbus-communication-troubleshooting-checklist troubleshooting A practical checklist for resolving common Modbus RTU and TCP communication errors. pages.dataKeywords: art_sexed_semen_dairy_heifers_beginner Using Sexed Semen in Dairy Heifers: A Beginner's Guide sexed-semen-in-dairy-heifers-beginner-guide Step-by-step guidance on when and how to use sexed semen in dairy heifers to increase replacement heifer numbers while managing conception risk. beginner sexed semen dairy heifers reproduction heifer management dairy ai_techniques article.html?articleId=art_sexed_semen_dairy_heifers_beginner art_sexed_semen_advanced_strategies Advanced Strategies for Using Sexed Semen in Dairy Herds advanced-strategies-sexed-semen-dairy-herds Optimize genetic gain and pregnancy rates when deploying sexed semen across heifers and cows. This article explores advanced sexed semen programs that combine genomic testing, strategic matings, and differential use of sexed versus conventional semen. We discuss economic modeling of replacement needs, conception risk by parity and days in milk, and how to allocate sexed semen to the top genomic animals while using beef semen on the bottom end of the herd. advanced genomic selection economic analysis genetics article.html?articleId=art_sexed_semen_advanced_strategies art_mastitis_control_scs Somatic Cell Score and Mastitis: What Your Bull Proofs Really Mean somatic-cell-score-and-mastitis Interpret somatic cell score (SCS) evaluations to reduce mastitis risk through sire selection. Somatic cell score is a key indicator trait for mastitis resistance. We explain how SCS is calculated, typical ranges for Holstein sires, and how much improvement you can expect by shifting the herd average SCS proof. Practical selection guidelines help you balance milk yield, udder health, and other traits without sacrificing profitability. intermediate somatic cell score mastitis holstein udder health health genetic_evaluations article.html?articleId=art_mastitis_control_scs art_understanding_bull_ebvs Understanding Bull EPDs and Indexes for Beef Operations understanding-bull-epds-and-indexes-beef A practical explanation of common beef bull EPDs and economic indexes for commercial producers. Bull selection is one of the highest-leverage decisions in a beef operation. This article breaks down birth weight, calving ease, weaning and yearling weight, carcass traits, and composite indexes. We illustrate how to read a bull proof and align trait emphasis with your marketing endpoint and environment. beef bulls weaning weight genetic indexes beef article.html?articleId=art_understanding_bull_ebvs art_beef_heifer_sync_protocols Choosing Synchronization Protocols for Beef Heifers choosing-sync-protocols-beef-heifers Compare common fixed-time AI protocols for beef heifers and how to pick the right one for your ranch. We outline popular synchronization protocols including 7-day CO-Synch + CIDR, 14-day CIDR-PG, and MGA-PG programs. For each protocol we discuss cost, labor requirements, expected conception rates, and heifer eligibility criteria. A decision chart helps you match protocols to facilities, labor, and breeding s

pages.dataKeywords: art_safety_beginner_lifejackets Beginner's Guide to Life Jackets and PFDs beginners-guide-life-jackets-pfds safety_basics other Learn how to choose, fit, and maintain a life jacket so every passenger on board stays safe. This beginner-focused guide explains the different types of life jackets (PFDs), how to read the labels, and how to choose the right size for adults and children. It also covers inspection checklists and when to replace your gear. beginner Beginner Safety Gear Life Jackets all_sizes art_safety_beginner_right_of_way 7 Essential Right-of-Way Rules for New Boaters essential-right-of-way-rules-new-boaters Understand who goes first on the water so you can avoid close calls and collisions. pages.dataKeywords: blog_battery_storage_guide_2026 Home Battery Storage 101: Is a Solar Battery Right for You? home-battery-storage-101 Learn how home battery storage works, typical costs, and when it makes sense to pair batteries with your solar system. Home battery storage allows you to capture excess solar energy during the day and use it at night or during grid outages. In this guide, we cover how lithium-ion batteries work, typical system sizes for residential homes, and how battery storage interacts with net metering and time-of-use rates. We also outline key questions to ask when deciding if battery storage is right for your home. battery storage home battery energy storage residential solar plus storage battery_storage Dana Lee blog_battery_sizing_guide_2025 How to Size a Home Battery for Your Solar System how-to-size-a-home-battery A step-by-step walkthrough for choosing the right battery capacity based on your energy usage and backup needs. Sizing a home battery for your solar system starts with understanding your critical loads and typical nightly usage. In this article, we explain how to read your electric bill, estimate kWh needs during an outage, and match that to common battery sizes. We also compare single-battery vs. multi-battery setups and discuss how battery storage can reduce time-of-use charges. battery sizing energy usage Alex Martinez blog_battery_vs_generator_2024 Battery Storage vs. Backup Generator: Which Is Better? battery-storage-vs-backup-generator Comparing battery storage and backup generators for home emergency power, including costs, noise, and maintenance. When homeowners compare backup options, battery storage often competes with traditional gas or propane generators. This article compares upfront costs, long-term operating expenses, noise, maintenance requirements, and environmental impact. We also explain scenarios where combining battery storage with a small generator can be effective. backup power generator resilience Jordan Kim blog_solar_basics_2026 Solar Basics: How Residential Solar Works in 2026 solar-basics-how-residential-solar-works-2026 A clear overview of how modern residential solar systems work, from panels and inverters to monitoring apps. Modern residential solar systems are more efficient and reliable than ever. This article walks through how sunlight becomes electricity, the role of inverters, and how your system connects to the grid. We also introduce key concepts like net metering, time-of-use billing, and typical system sizes for different home types. solar basics residential solar net metering education blog_incentives_guide_2026 2026 Guide to Solar Incentives, Tax Credits, and Rebates 2026-guide-to-solar-incentives-tax-credits-rebates See how federal, state, and utility incentives can lower the cost of going solar in 2026. Solar incentives can reduce your upfront cost by 20–40% or more. In 2026, many homeowners can still claim the federal investment tax credit, along with state-level rebates and utility p

pages.dataKeywords: art_advanced_anchors_overhung_2024 Redundant Anchor Systems for Overhung Indoor Walls redundant-anchor-systems-overhung-indoor-walls Design and inspection strategies for redundant, multidirectional anchors on steep lead walls in modern gyms. This article covers advanced principles for building and inspecting redundant anchor systems on overhung terrain. Topics include bolt spacing on arêtes, direction-of-load analysis, master point equalization techniques, backup strategies for fixed-top-rope anchors, and failure case studies from indoor environments... Dana Keller advanced anchor building redundancy lead climbing gym operations safety art_load_testing_anchors_2023 Load Testing Modern Indoor Anchors load-testing-modern-indoor-anchors Empirical data on shear and outward force performance for common indoor anchor configurations. Indoor anchor design has evolved alongside wall construction techniques. In this study we instrumented common anchor layouts and recorded forces during leader falls, top-rope loads, and dynamic lowers. We compare stainless glue-ins, mechanical bolts, and resin-bonded hardware, and provide recommendations for factor-of-safety and inspection intervals... pages.dataKeywords: individual_50min individual_counseling Individual Counseling (50 minutes) Standard individual therapy session focused on a wide range of concerns such as anxiety, depression, and stress. in_person online individual_80min_intake Individual Counseling Intake (80 minutes) Extended first session to review history, goals, and create an initial treatment plan. couples_75min couples_counseling Couples Counseling (75 minutes) Relationship-focused counseling for partners seeking to improve communication and connection. family_therapy_75min family_therapy Family Therapy (75 minutes) Therapy session for families working on communication, boundaries, and conflict. group_session_90min group_session Group Therapy Session (90 minutes) Facilitated group therapy for topics such as social anxiety, depression support, and trauma recovery. free_consult_15min free_consult_15 Free 15-Minute Consultation Brief telehealth consultation to discuss fit, availability, and answer questions before starting therapy. phq9 phq_9 Depression Self-Assessment (PHQ-9) A brief, evidence-based questionnaire to help screen for symptoms of depression over the last two weeks. This self-assessment is for educational purposes only and does not provide a diagnosis. If you are concerned about your results, please contact a licensed mental health professional. gad7 gad_7 Anxiety Self-Assessment (GAD-7) A short screening tool to assess the severity of generalized anxiety symptoms. Results from this tool are not a substitute for a professional evaluation. If anxiety is impacting your daily life, consider speaking with a therapist or healthcare provider. pts_screen Post-Traumatic Stress Screener A brief questionnaire to help identify common post-traumatic stress symptoms following difficult or traumatic experiences. This screener cannot confirm or rule out PTSD. For a full assessment and support, please consult with a qualified mental health clinician. wellbeing_checkin other General Wellbeing Check-In A short check-in covering mood, stress, sleep, and support to help you reflect on your current wellbeing. This check-in is informational only and does not replace a mental health evaluation or medical advice. sf_county_crisis_line San Francisco County Crisis Line 415-555-0100 local San Francisco County, CA all_ages 24/7 For anyone in San Francisco County experiencing an emotional or mental health crisis. sf_youth_crisis_line San Francisco Youth Crisis Line 415-555-0120 San Francisco, CA youth Confidential support for teens and young adults in crisis. sf_warm_line San Francisco Peer Warm Line 415-555-0135 San Francisco Bay Area, CA adults 7 days a week, 7:00 am–11:00 pm Peer support line for non-emergency emotional support. bay_area_domestic_violence Bay Area Domestic Violence Hotline 510-555-0180 Support, safety planning, and shelter referrals for people experiencing relationship violence. national_suicide_lifeline National Suicide & Crisis Lifeline national United States Call or text 988, or chat

pages.dataKeywords:
ux-telehealth-onboarding-2024 Designing Frictionless Onboarding for a Telehealth App telehealth-app-onboarding-ux-case-study How we reduced patient drop-off by 37% in the first session of a telehealth platform. Maya Ortiz user_experience case_study healthcare ux_case_study ux-fintech-saving-habits-2023 Nudging Better Saving Habits in a Fintech App fintech-saving-habits-ux-case-study Behavioral design techniques that increased weekly savings actions by 24%. This UX case study explores how we used behavioral science to encourage consistent saving in a consumer fintech app.

We introduced goal-based saving jars, progress visualizations, and just-in-time prompts based on payday predictions. The design emphasized clarity and reduced cognitive load by limiting choices at each step.

Post-launch analytics showed a 24% increase in weekly savings actions and a 15% reduction in users withdrawing from their savings jars prematurely. Jared Lin fintech ux-education-learning-paths-2024 Personalized Learning Paths for an EdTech Platform personalized-learning-paths-ux Reimagining course discovery and progress for 120K+ active students. For a fast-growing EdTech platform, we redesigned the course discovery and learning path experience.

Through diary studies and analytics, we learned that learners struggled to understand which course to take next and how close they were to their goals. We introduced guided paths, clear skill outcomes, and a new progress tracker spanning multiple courses.

The redesign led to a 19% increase in course completion rates and higher satisfaction scores in quarterly NPS surveys. education ux-microinteractions-2023 Microinteractions That Make Interfaces Feel Effortless microinteractions-that-delight A practical guide to designing subtle moments that guide and reassure users. Microinteractions are small design moments with an outsized impact on perceived usability.

In this article we break down four categories of microinteractions—input feedback, status and progress, navigational hints, and error recovery—showing real examples from recent client projects.

We also cover motion guidelines and accessibility considerations to ensure these moments feel helpful, not distracting. Ella Novak motion_design article ux-accessibility-2025 Leveling Up Accessibility in Product Teams accessibility-practices-for-product-teams Practical steps to embed accessibility into your design and development workflow. Accessibility is not a one-time checklist—it is an ongoing practice.

We share how we helped a SaaS team integrate accessibility into discovery, design, and QA. Topics include setting up shared color tokens, creating reusable accessible components, and running regular screen reader audits.

By the end of the project, critical accessibility issues dropped by 82% and the team felt confident shipping inclusive features. accessibility branding-restaurant-2024 From Pop-Up to Cult Favorite: Branding Ember Street Bistro branding-ember-street-bistro How a co

pages.dataKeywords:
primary_care Primary & preventive care Office visits with a primary care provider, annual checkups, vaccines, and routine screenings. pharmacy Pharmacy & prescriptions Generic and brand-name medications, mail-order options, and preferred pharmacy discounts. mental_health_counseling Mental health & counseling Therapy, counseling, and psychiatric services for adults, teens, and children. emergency_services Emergency & urgent services Emergency room visits, urgent care, and ambulance transportation. telehealth Telehealth & virtual visits Video and phone visits with doctors, nurses, and mental health professionals. labs_imaging Lab tests & imaging Blood work, X-rays, MRIs, CT scans, and other diagnostic tests. lost_phone_temp_card What if I lose my phone with my digital health card? account_security lost phone temporary card security device temporary_card_request_step1.html help_faqs.html#account_security what_is_digital_health_card What is the digital health card and how does it work?

Your digital health card is a secure ID you keep on your phone instead of a plastic card. Show it at check-in, share it with your provider, or use it online to confirm your eligibility and coverage.

It displays your member ID, plan name, and important copay information. You can access it anytime by signing in to your account or adding it to your phone wallet.

getting_started digital health card overview getting started index.html plans_pricing.html add_card_phone_wallet How do I add my digital health card to my phone wallet?

After you sign in, go to My card and select Add to wallet. Follow the prompts for Apple Wallet or Google Wallet.

If you change phones later, simply sign in on your new device and repeat these steps.

technical_support phone wallet apple wallet google wallet help_faqs.html#technical_support use_card_pharmacy How do I use my digital health card at the pharmacy?

At the pharmacy counter, open your digital health card on your phone. The pharmacist will scan the code or type in your member ID and BIN/PCN numbers.

For step-by-step examples, see our pharmacy usage guide in the How to use your card section.

using_your_card prescriptions how to use usage_scenarios.html coverage_overview.html?categoryId=pharmacy coverage_mental_health What services are covered for mental health & counseling?

Most plans include coverage for outpatient therapy, virtual counseling, and psychiatric evaluations. Your copay or coinsurance amount depends on your specific plan.

Review the Mental health & counseling coverage summary for details about visit limits, prior authorization, and telehealth options.

pages.dataKeywords: article_1 10 Practical Ways to Cope With Cravings in Early Recovery Step-by-step strategies you can use in the moment when a craving hits, including urge surfing and grounding techniques. Cravings are a normal part of early recovery, but they don’t have to control your actions. In this guide, we’ll walk through practical tools you can use the moment a craving appears... Dr. Lena Ortiz coping with cravings early recovery relapse prevention coping_with_cravings urge_surfing grounding_techniques early_recovery practical-ways-to-cope-with-cravings-early-recovery article_2 Coping With Nighttime Cravings: A 7-Step Checklist A quick checklist you can follow when cravings feel strongest at night. Many people notice that cravings get stronger in the evening, when they’re tired or alone. This article offers a simple 7-step checklist you can keep by your bed or on your phone... Marcus Lee, LCSW sleep evening routines sleep_hygiene nighttime_triggers coping-with-nighttime-cravings-7-step-checklist article_3 Urge Surfing: Riding Out Cravings Without Giving In Learn how to observe cravings like waves instead of fighting or acting on them. Urge surfing is a mindfulness-based technique that helps you experience cravings without reacting to them. Instead of trying to push cravings away, you learn to notice the sensations rise, peak, and fall... Jasmine Patel, PsyD mindfulness cbt_skills urge-surfing-riding-out-cravings-without-giving-in article_4 Handling Cravings at Work Without Anyone Noticing Discreet ways to manage cravings during the workday while staying focused and professional. Work can be a high-pressure environment, and for many people in recovery, it’s also full of triggers. This article focuses on subtle tools you can use at your desk, in meetings, or on breaks... Elena Brooks, CADC workplace stress management stress_management professional_life handling-cravings-at-work-without-anyone-noticing article_5 From Trigger to Choice: A Short Guide to Craving Logs How to track your cravings so you can predict and prevent them over time. Keeping a craving log is a simple but powerful way to understand your patterns. By writing down when, where, and how cravings show up, you gain data you can act on... Recovery Coach Team tracking tools self_monitoring worksheets from-trigger-to-choice-craving-logs-guide article_6 Staying Sober Through Social Events Practical boundaries and exit plans for parties, weddings, and work events. Social events can be full of triggers—alcohol, old using friends, and social pressure. Planning ahead dramatically reduces your risk of relapse... Taylor Johnson, LCDC social triggers alcohol boundaries social_events alcohol_recovery staying-sober-through-social-events article_7 Understanding Post-Acute Withdrawal Syndrome (PAWS) Why mood swings, sleep problems, and low energy can last for months—and what to do about it. Post-acute withdrawal syndrome (PAWS) can last weeks or months after you stop using. Recognizing the symptoms helps you p

1. Sign in and click **Profile** in the header.
2. Go to **Account Settings > Billing**.
3. Under **Payment Methods**, click **Edit** next to the card or service you want to change.
4. Update the details or click **Remove** to delete the method.
5. Confirm your changes when prompted.

pages.dataKeywords:
faq_30_day_money_back_guarantee What is your 30-day money-back guarantee? 30-day-money-back-guarantee billing_refunds refund money-back guarantee 30-day guarantee billing cancellation faq_how_to_get_started How do I get started with the work-from-home program? You can get started in three simple steps:

1. Choose a starter plan on the Plans & Pricing page that matches your budget and support needs.
2. Complete the short application so we can understand your goals and recommended track.
3. Check your email for login details and follow the "Week 1" onboarding checklist.

Most members complete onboarding in under 60 minutes and book their first coaching session within the first week. how-to-get-started getting_started getting started onboarding new members faq_billing_how_refunds_work How do refunds and cancellations work? how-refunds-and-cancellations-work subscription faq_technical_requirements What are the technical requirements to join? You’ll need a reliable internet connection, a modern web browser (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, or Edge), and a device with audio and video capability for live sessions (laptop, desktop, or tablet).

Our learning portal is mobile-friendly for viewing lessons, but we recommend a laptop or desktop for implementation work and coaching calls. technical-requirements technical technical requirements system requirements devices internet faq_program_differences What’s the difference between plans and programs? Plans determine how you access our platform (level of support, coaching, and tools), while programs are the specific business tracks you follow, such as Email Marketing or Affiliate Sales.

You can start with any plan and then choose one or more programs inside your dashboard. Higher-tier plans typically include more one-on-one coaching and additional live group sessions for your chosen program. difference-between-plans-and-programs programs plans coaching support levels plan_quickstart_89 Quickstart Coaching Lite quickstart-coaching-lite Quickstart Lite Entry-level work-from-home starter plan with focused training and a dedicated kick-off coaching call. pages.dataKeywords: photo_comp_2022_01 First 90cm Round at Spring Valley Star locking onto the first oxer of our 90cm round at Spring Valley. I was nervous, but she took me to the fence like a pro. competitions Spring Valley Showgrounds photo_comp_2022_02 Tight Turn to the Double Second fence of the tricky two-stride double at Spring Valley. We practiced this turn for weeks at home. photo_comp_2022_03 Clearing the Water Tray Our first clear jump over a spooky water tray at the county show. Star hesitated for a stride, then flew. Greenfield County Show photo_comp_2022_04 First Red Ribbon Finish Walking out of the arena after our first red ribbon in the 80cm class. I could not stop smiling. photo_comp_2022_05 Evening Jump-Off Round Our fastest round of the season under the evening lights. Star felt light and focused over every fence. Riverside Equestrian Centre photo_comp_2022_06 Waiting at the In-Gate One last pat and a deep breath while we wait for the bell. Star watches the last rider leave the arena. photo_comp_2022_07 Final Fence of the Season The last fence of our 2022 competition season. We finished on a clear round and a huge boost of confidence. Oakridge Arena photo_comp_2022_08 Prizegiving Lap of Honor Star cantering the lap of honor with her ribbon flapping on the bridle. She clearly enjoyed the applause. photo_comp_2023_01 First 1m Class Warm-Up Stretching over a warm-up oxer before our first 1m class. A big milestone for both of us. Brightside Showpark photo_comp_2023_02 Checking the Course Map Studying the jumpoff course with Star half-dozing beside me. The calm before the adrenaline. photo_training_2024_01 Gridwork in the Outdoor Arena A line of placing poles and small crosses to help Star find her rhythm and improve her technique over fences. training Home Arena photo_training_2024_02 Canter Poles for Suppleness Using raised canter poles on a circle to encourage Star to sit and balance in the corners. photo_tricks_2025_01 First Attempt at Bow Star learning to stretch one leg forward and lower her head for the bow trick, rewarded with lots of scratches. pages.dataKeywords:
faq_deposit_policy How much is the booking deposit and when is it due? We require a 25% deposit at the time of booking to reserve your party date and time. The remaining balance is due 3 days before your event. Your deposit is applied toward the total party cost and can be paid securely online by credit or debit card. If you need to make changes to your booking, you can adjust your package or add-ons up until 5 days before the event.

Ready to get started? Use the link below to open our booking form and check availability. deposit booking-deposit-policy Start a Booking booking.html?source=faq_deposit faq_payment_methods What payment methods do you accept? We accept all major credit cards (Visa, MasterCard, American Express, Discover), debit cards, and our own gift cards. Full payment can be completed online through our secure checkout. At this time, we do not accept cash or checks for deposits or final balances. payment payment-methods faq_change_cancel What is your cancellation and rescheduling policy? You may reschedule your party once at no additional charge up to 7 days before your event date, subject to availability. Cancellations made 14 or more days before the event are eligible for a full refund of the deposit. Cancellations made within 14 days of the event are non-refundable, but you may apply your deposit as credit toward a future party within 12 months. cancellation cancellation-and-rescheduling faq_group_size How many children can each party package accommodate? Each party package lists a recommended maximum number of children based on the activities and entertainer. Most of our standard packages are designed for 8–15 children, but we also offer options for larger groups. If you expect more children than the listed capacity, please contact us or add an extra helper or entertainer as an add-on so we can ensure a smooth experience. booking party-group-size faq_travel_fees Do you charge travel fees for entertainers? Travel within our core service area is included for many entertainers, while others may charge a small additional fee based on distance. When you enter your ZIP code on the Entertainers page, you will see which performers offer "no travel fee" in your area and any applicable travel charges before you book. general entertainer-travel-fees faq_when_to_book How far in advance should I book my party? We recommend booking at least 3–4 weeks in advance to secure your preferred date, time, and theme, especially during weekends and holiday periods. Last-minute bookings are sometimes available, so feel free to check our real-time availability through the booking form. when-to-book digital_email Digital Gift Card (Email Delivery) digital Send a digital gift card straight to their inbox. Perfect for birthdays, holidays, and surprise celebrations. The recipient can redeem it toward any party package, entertainer, or custom party. physical_mail Physical Gift Card (Mailed) physical A printed gift card mailed in a colorful envelope, great f

pages.dataKeywords: 2026_international_residential_award 2026 International Residential Award residential professional Global open august Flagship international award celebrating built and unbuilt residential projects of all scales, with categories for single-family, multi-family, and affordable housing. Open to licensed architecture firms worldwide. Early-bird entries receive a discounted fee and extended upload period. Built projects must be completed after January 1, 2020. 2026_emerging_residential_voices_student 2026 Emerging Residential Voices (Student) student upcoming november International student competition exploring experimental housing typologies that respond to climate, culture, and community. Open only to current architecture and urban design students (undergraduate or graduate). Team entries of up to 4 members are allowed. Digital submissions only. 2025_student_urban_courtyards_challenge 2025 Student Urban Courtyards Challenge urban_public_realm Europe closed october Student-only ideas competition for reimagining underused inner courtyards as inclusive public spaces in dense European cities. Open to enrolled students in architecture, landscape, or urbanism programs. Anonymous submissions required. Prize pool distributed among three jury-selected winners. 2025_campus_resilience_studio 2025 Campus Resilience Studio education North America Design competition for resilient and adaptable educational environments on university campuses, focusing on climate adaptation and community use. Individual or team student entries accepted. Entries must include site analysis, phasing strategy, and resilience measures. Jury includes campus planners and sustainability experts. 2025_global_cultural_landmark_award 2025 Global Cultural Landmark Award cultural june Award recognizing exemplary museums, galleries, and cultural centers that redefine public engagement with art and heritage. Open to built cultural projects completed between 2019 and 2024. Multiple project entries allowed per firm. Jury focuses on community impact and visitor experience. 2025_asia_pacific_hotel_innovation_awards 2025 Asia-Pacific Hotel Innovation Awards hospitality_hotel Asia-Pacific september Regional award spotlighting innovative hotel and resort architecture across the Asia-Pacific region, with an emphasis on guest experience and cultural integration. Open to hospitality projects completed after January 1, 2018, located in Asia-Pacific. Entries may be submitted by architects, interior designers, or hotel operators with owner consent. 2024_peoples_park_revitalization_award 2024 People's Park Revitalization Award public_space mixed Award celebrating built public space projects that have successfully revitalized urban parks and plazas through community-driven design.
