pages.dataKeywords: art_tour_diary_2024_eu_leg1 Tour Diary 2024: First Nights in Europe tour-diary-2024-first-nights-in-europe Kicking off the 2024 world tour in Berlin, late-night train rides, and the first time playing the new single live. Tour Diary Europe Tour Live Lena Park published art_tour_diary_2024_eu_leg2 Tour Diary 2024: Night Drives and Neon Streets tour-diary-2024-night-drives-and-neon-streets From Prague to Vienna, we chase sunrise on the highway and discover a new pre-show ritual. On the Road Noah Reyes art_tour_diary_2024_summer_festivals Tour Diary 2024: Summer Festivals and Sudden Storms tour-diary-2024-summer-festivals-and-sudden-storms Mud, lightning delays, and the thrill of hearing thousands sing your chorus back at a sunset festival slot. Summer festivals are chaos in the best way. One minute you’re backstage sharing a golf cart with a band you’ve idolized for years, the next you’re watching crews scramble to cover amps as storm clouds roll in. At one festival, a lightning delay pushed our set back by an hour, but the crowd stayed. When the sky finally cleared, the field turned into a choir. We cut the backing tracks for the last chorus of “City Lights” and let the crowd carry it. It was imperfect and absolutely unforgettable. Festivals Summer 2024 Mia Chen art_tour_diary_2024_fall_na Tour Diary 2024: Late-Night Diners Across North America tour-diary-2024-late-night-diners-north-america From Toronto to Los Angeles, we map the continent through coffee refills, jukeboxes, and corner booths. Fall on the road means hoodies, foggy windows, and the constant hunt for the best diner in every city. In Toronto we found a spot that served pancakes until 3 a.m., and the server slipped us a handwritten playlist of local bands. In Chicago, a cook recognized us from a festival livestream and requested “Aurora Dreams” while flipping burgers. By the time we reached Los Angeles, we had a full zine’s worth of polaroids, receipts, and scribbled lyrics born at sticky tables under neon lights. North America Food art_tour_diary_2023_world Tour Diary 2023: First Steps Onto Global Stages tour-diary-2023-first-global-stages Our first time playing outside our home country, learning to say thank you in five new languages. Nothing prepares you for the feeling of stepping onto a stage where no one shares your native language, but everyone knows your songs. In Tokyo, the crowd was shy at first, then exploded during the bridge of “Silver Lines.” In São Paulo, it was the opposite—loud from the first note. We learned how to order coffee, say thank you, and introduce ourselves in each country we visited. Every night, we left the stage a slightly different band than the night before. World Tour art_tour_diary_2022_first_run Tour Diary 2022: Our First Van, Our First Tour tour-diary-2022-first-van-first-tour Before buses and festivals, it was five friends, one van, and a map full of tiny clubs. pages.dataKeywords: art_under_10k_2026_overview How to Plan a Beautiful Wedding Under $10,000 (2026 Edition) plan-wedding-under-10000-2026 budget_money A step-by-step guide to planning a stylish wedding on a budget of $10,000 or less, including sample allocations and saving tips. Planning a wedding under $10,000 is absolutely possible with clear priorities and a realistic budget. In this guide, we break down venue, food, attire, decor, photography and more, showing sample allocations for different guest counts. You'll also find cost-saving strategies like off-peak dates, brunch receptions, and borrowing decor. Under $10,000 50–120 guests budget under_10000 planning_basics sample_budget art_under_10k_brunch_cityhall City Hall Ceremony & Brunch Reception: $10,000 All-In Sample Budget city-hall-brunch-10000-sample-budget See exactly how to allocate a $10,000 budget for a city hall ceremony followed by a brunch reception for 60–80 guests. This article walks through a sample $10,000 budget for a city hall ceremony and brunch reception. We cover permit fees, photography coverage, a brunch buffet, limited bar, simple florals, and a small dessert table. A detailed line-item budget shows how each dollar is allocated. 60–80 guests city_hall brunch art_under_10k_backyard_80_guests Backyard Wedding Budget: Under $10,000 for 80 Guests backyard-wedding-budget-under-10000-80-guests A realistic budget breakdown for hosting 70–80 guests in a backyard wedding for less than $10,000. Backyard weddings can save on venue fees but add costs for rentals, restrooms, and lighting. This article lays out a sample budget under $10,000, including tenting, basic rentals, buffet catering, and DIY decor. We also discuss where you can safely cut costs and where you shouldn’t. 70–80 guests backyard art_under_10k_micro_wedding Micro Wedding Budget Under $10,000: Intimate Celebration micro-wedding-budget-under-10000 Budget outline for a 20–30 person micro wedding with a $10,000 cap, focusing on great food and photography. For couples who value experience over a large guest list, a micro wedding can stretch a $10,000 budget incredibly far. This guide shows a sample budget focusing on a high-quality meal, stunning photography, and meaningful details, plus tips for trimming the guest list thoughtfully. 20–30 guests micro_wedding art_12k_15k_for_150 Stretching a $12,000–$15,000 Wedding Budget for 150 Guests stretch-12000-15000-budget-150-guests How to host around 150 guests on a $12k–$15k budget with venue, catering, and decor strategies. Hosting 150 guests on a mid-range budget requires trade-offs. We break down a sample $13,500 budget, highlight cost-per-guest targets, and share specific negotiation tips for venues and caterers. $12,000–$15,000 Around 150 guests (130–170) 150_guests art_save_vs_splurge Where to Save and Where to Splurge on Your Wedding Budget where-to-save-where-to-splurge-wedding-budget A practical guide to deciding which wedding elements deserve a splurge and where you can cut costs. pages.dataKeywords: article_index_funds_101 Index Funds 101: Simple Starter Guide index-funds-101-simple-starter-guide A plain-language introduction to what index funds are, how they work, and why many beginners start with them. Index funds are baskets of securities that track a specific market index, such as the S&P 500. Instead of trying to beat the market, index funds aim to match its performance at low cost... beginner index funds etfs passive investing article_choose_first_index_fund How to Pick Your First Index Fund how-to-pick-your-first-index-fund Step-by-step guidance on choosing a low-cost index fund that fits your goals and risk tolerance. When choosing your first index fund, focus on three key factors: the index it tracks, its total expense ratio, and how it fits your overall asset allocation... asset allocation fees article_index_fund_checklist Beginner's Checklist for Index Fund Investing beginners-checklist-for-index-fund-investing A quick pre-investing checklist to make sure you're ready to start with index funds. Before you place your first index fund order, review this checklist: emergency fund in place, high-interest debt under control, clear investment goal, and a basic understanding of market ups and downs... checklist financial planning article_index_funds_risks Are Index Funds Always Safe? Risks to Know are-index-funds-always-safe-risks-to-know Index funds are simple, but they still carry market and strategy-specific risks you should understand. Index funds can feel almost risk-free because of their diversification, but they still move with the market and can fall sharply during broad sell-offs... intermediate risk management volatility article_stop_loss_protect_portfolio Stop-Loss Orders: Intermediate Guide to Protecting Your Portfolio stop-loss-orders-intermediate-guide Learn how to size, place, and adjust stop-loss orders so they protect you without knocking you out of good trades. A stop-loss order is designed to cap your downside on a position, but where you place it dramatically changes your results. In this guide we compare percentage stops, volatility-based stops, and support-based levels... stop-loss order position sizing article_stop_loss_swing_trades Using Stop-Loss Orders to Protect Swing Trades using-stop-loss-orders-to-protect-swing-trades How to combine chart structure and volatility to place smarter stops on short-term trades. For swing traders, stop-loss placement is part art and part math. You want stops to sit beyond obvious noise but still limit losses if the setup fails... swing trading technical analysis article_trailing_vs_fixed_stop_loss Trailing Stop-Loss vs Fixed Stop: Pros and Cons trailing-stop-loss-vs-fixed-stop-pros-and-cons Compare fixed and trailing stop-loss orders, including when each style tends to work best. Fixed and trailing stops solve different problems. Fixed stops are simple and predictable, while trailing stops attempt to lock in gains as a trend develops... trailing stop article_options_trading_101 O

pages.dataKeywords: xr-museums-storytelling-2025 Reimagining Museum Storytelling with Immersive XR reimagining-museum-storytelling-with-immersive-xr How museums and galleries are using VR and AR to turn static exhibits into interactive, narrative-driven journeys. Museums around the world are embracing XR to extend their narratives beyond the glass case. From room-scale VR recreations of historical events to AR layers that reveal hidden details on artifacts, immersive technologies are transforming passive visits into participatory experiences. In this article we break down successful concepts, hardware considerations, visitor flow design, and accessibility best practices for curators planning their first XR pilot. Dana Morales museums_exhibitions museum exhibition curation storytelling ar-gallery-tours-2025 Designing AR Gallery Tours That Don’t Distract from the Art designing-ar-gallery-tours-that-dont-distract-from-the-art A practical guide for galleries using mobile AR and headsets to enhance, not overshadow, exhibitions. When introducing AR into gallery spaces, curators often worry that screens will pull attention away from the art itself. The key is to design a companion experience: light-touch overlays, context-aware prompts, and audio-first storytelling that supports the work on the wall. This guide explores optimal interaction patterns, device policies, and analytics for AR gallery tours, with examples from pilot programs across Europe and North America. Leah Anders gallery mobile visitor-experience immersive-exhibition-roadmap From Static Exhibit to Immersive Installation: A 6-Month Roadmap from-static-exhibit-to-immersive-installation-roadmap Timeline, roles, and budget checkpoints for turning a traditional exhibition into an immersive XR installation. Transitioning from traditional vitrines to immersive XR spaces doesn’t have to be overwhelming. In this roadmap we outline a practical six-month plan: discovery and stakeholder alignment, concept prototyping, content production, technical integration, on-site testing, and launch. We highlight decision points unique to museums, like conservation concerns, visitor safety, and staffing models. project-planning budgeting museums xr-for-open-air-festivals XR for Open-Air Festivals and Temporary Exhibitions xr-for-open-air-festivals-and-temporary-exhibitions Tech, logistics, and creative concepts for XR activations at festivals and pop-up exhibitions. Temporary exhibitions and festivals introduce unique XR challenges: weather, connectivity, variable light, and throughput. This article outlines deployment patterns for AR scavenger hunts, projection-mapped stages, and pop-up VR domes, with a focus on quick setup and teardown, rugged hardware, and staff training. Marcus Li festival pop-up logistics retail-vr-try-before-you-buy VR ‘Try Before You Buy’: What Actually Works in Retail vr-try-before-you-buy-what-works-in-retail Lessons from VR try-on and product visualization pilots across fashion, furniture, and automotive

Kenya lower_middle_income Hosts multiple neonatal sepsis and CPAP implementation RCTs in tertiary hospitals. Nigeria High-burden country for preterm birth and maternal hypertension trials. India Large volume of neonatal sepsis RCTs and KMC implementation studies. Bangladesh Frequent location for community and facility-based newborn sepsis trials. South Africa upper_middle_income Hosts high-volume NICUs used in benchmark comparisons and respiratory support trials. Brazil south_america Key setting for kangaroo mother care and antenatal corticosteroid research. Mexico north_america Upper-middle-income North American country included in regional analyses. China Large contributor of NICU cohort and respiratory support studies. United States high_income High-income North American country; many NEC and CPAP vs ventilation RCTs. Canada Included in North American NEC alerts and NICU benchmarking datasets. United Kingdom europe Site of major antenatal corticosteroid and preterm birth trials. Sweden High-quality registry data on extremely preterm infants used for NICU benchmarks. Australia oceania Important source of CPAP vs mechanical ventilation trials and KMC implementation studies. Egypt middle_east Representative Middle East/Africa setting for maternal hypertension and neonatal sepsis research. pages.dataKeywords: amanda_choi Amanda Choi Amanda Choi is a progressive policy analyst whose work focuses on climate governance, just transitions, and the intersection of environmental and economic justice. She has advised municipal governments on decarbonization strategies and frequently writes about how climate policy shapes democratic participation. amanda.choi@site-authors.example progressive david_morales David Morales David Morales is a civil rights lawyer and progressive commentator specializing in voting rights, ballot access, and the impact of redistricting on communities of color. His writing blends legal analysis with on-the-ground reporting from grassroots organizers. david.morales@site-authors.example rebecca_kyte Rebecca Kyte Rebecca Kyte is a conservative economist and columnist focusing on fiscal policy, regulation, and the long-term impacts of government spending. She previously worked in the private sector and now writes about market-based approaches to economic challenges. rebecca.kyte@site-authors.example conservative thomas_harrington Thomas Harrington Thomas Harrington is a conservative political writer whose work examines immigration, national identity, and social cohesion. Drawing on comparative history, he argues for stronger border controls while engaging with humanitarian concerns. thomas.harrington@site-authors.example lena_patel Lena Patel Lena Patel is a centrist legal scholar focusing on free speech, digital platforms, and institutional trust. pages.dataKeywords: gp_luma_base_apy_adjustment_mar_2026 Adjust LUMA Base Staking APY for Short-Term Pools (March 2026) Proposal to reduce volatility by narrowing the APY band on 7–30 day LUMA staking pools. active gp_treasury_luma_buyback_q2_2026 Authorize Q2 2026 LUMA Treasury Buyback and Burn Use a portion of protocol revenue to buy back and burn LUMA during Q2 2026. gp_add_short_term_luma_pools Introduce New 7 and 15 Day LUMA Staking Pools Add high-liquidity LUMA pools with 7 and 15 day lock durations and dynamic APY bands. gp_platform_fee_restructure_q2_2026 Restructure Platform Fees for Flexible and Fixed Pools (Q2 2026) Simplify fee tiers, reduce fees on flexible pools, and introduce loyalty discounts for long-term stakers. gp_security_audit_budget_2026 Allocate 2026 Security Audit and Bug Bounty Budget Set aside funds for third-party security audits and expand the bug bounty program. gp_luma_emissions_schedule_2026 Update LUMA Emissions Schedule for 2026 Retarget annual LUMA emissions to prioritize sustainable yields over aggressive growth. draft gp_stablecoin_risk_framework_update Revise Stablecoin Risk Framework and Collateral Limits Tighten collateral and exposure limits for algorithmic and undercollateralized stablecoins. gp_quarterly_financial_report_q4_2025 Approve Q4 2025 Financial Statements and Auditor Report Community ratification of the independently audited Q4 2025 financial statements. passed gp_listing_new_tokens_wave_4 List Additional Tokens for Staking Pools (Wave 4) Add support for four new tokens and their initial staking pool configurations. gp_reverse_dec_2025_fee_increase Reverse December 2025 Platform Fee Increase Attempt to roll back a previously approved increase in base platform fees. Following user feedback on the December 2025 fee adjustment, this proposal sought to reverse the 0.15 percentage point increase in base platform fees across most pools. The discussion period highlighted trade-offs between near-term user costs and the protocol’s ability to fund development and security. Ultimately, voters rejected the proposal, signaling support for maintaining the higher fee schedule and prioritizing long-term sustainability over immediate fee relief. rejected gp_luma_liquidity_mining_phase_2 Extend LUMA Liquidity Mining Program (Phase 2) Proposal to extend the LUMA liquidity mining incentives that did not reach quorum. This proposal asked the community to extend the existing LUMA liquidity mining incentives for an additional three months, with a modest reduction in weekly rewards and a reallocation toward high-utility pools. Despite broad support in the forums, on-chain participation was insufficient and the proposal failed to meet the minimum quorum requirement. As a result, the original liquidity mining program expired on schedule and no further emissions were authorized under this proposal. expired gp_governance_process_upgrade_v2 Upgrade Governance Process to Version 2 Introduce proposal templates, minimum review periods, and impro

pages.dataKeywords:
how-often-to-feed-a-10-week-old-puppy How Often to Feed a 10-Week-Old Puppy puppy puppy_feeding dog_nutrition feeding_schedule Learn how many meals per day a 10-week-old puppy needs, how much to feed at each meal, and how to adjust as they grow. puppy-feeding-schedule-chart Puppy Feeding Schedule by Age: Printable Chart printable_chart A simple chart that shows how many meals per day and how much to feed puppies from 8 weeks to 12 months. adult-dog-feeding-guide-25kg How Much Should a 25 kg Adult Dog Eat Per Day? how-much-should-a-25kg-adult-dog-eat feeding_tips feeding_calculator portion_control Learn how to estimate daily calories and portions for a moderately active 25 kg adult dog. understanding-grain-free-dog-food Understanding Grain-Free Dog Food: When Is It Right for Your Dog? grain_free dry_dog_food Explore what grain-free means, when it may be helpful, and how to choose a complete and balanced grain-free formula. indoor-cat-weight-control-guide Weight Control for Indoor Cats: A Practical Feeding Guide indoor_cats weight_control cat_nutrition How to feed your indoor cat for a healthy weight, including tips on portioning dry and wet foods. wet-vs-dry-cat-food Wet vs. Dry Cat Food: Which Is Better for Your Cat? wet_food dry_food mixed_feeding Compare the benefits of wet and dry cat food and learn how to create a mixed feeding plan. Both wet and dry cat foods can be complete and balanced. Dry foods are convenient, support dental crunching, and are easy to store, while wet foods boost moisture intake and can be more enticing to picky eaters.

Many veterinarians recommend a mixed feeding approach: a portion of dry food for grazing and a portion of wet food for hydration and variety. Our Feeding Plan tool helps you calculate the right combination based on your cat’s weight and activity level. indoor-hairball-mixed-feeding-plan Creating a Mixed Feeding Plan for Indoor Hairball Control Cats indoor_hairball How to combine Indoor Hairball dry and wet foods into a balanced daily plan for adult indoor cats. senior-large-dog-joint-support-nutrition Nutrition Tips for Senior Large-Breed Dogs with Joint Concerns senior_dogs joint_health large_breed Key nutrients and feeding strategies to support mobility in senior large-breed dogs. As large-breed dogs age, joint comfort becomes a top priority. Look for recipes formulated for senior or mobility support that include glucosamine, chondroitin, and omega-3 fatty acids from sources like fish oil.

Keep your dog at a lean body condition, as excess weight puts additional strain on joints. Use our Product Finder quiz to identify formulas tailored for senior large-breed dogs with joint concerns, and talk to your veterinarian about the right feeding plan. choosing-puppy-treats-for-training Choosing Puppy Treats for Training Without Overfeeding training puppy_training puppy_treats reward_based_training How to pick the right treats for puppies and keep treat calories within healthy limits. pages.dataKeywords: riverside_cultural_center Riverside Cultural Center A mid-sized cultural hub along the riverfront featuring a black-box theatre, rehearsal rooms, and flexible gallery spaces for community arts events. 120 Riverfront Ave Riverton Riverside District +1 (415) 555-2100 info@riversidecultural.org Step-free entrance from Riverfront Ave, ramps to all public levels, accessible restrooms on each floor, assistive listening devices available upon request. From downtown Riverton, take Main St north to Riverfront Ave. Turn right and continue 0.5 miles; the center is on the left. Underground parking garage is accessible from 2nd St. From Central Station, walk north on Main St for 8 minutes, turn right onto Riverfront Ave, and continue two blocks. The entrance is on the left, across from the river promenade. Served by Bus 12 (Riverfront), Bus 7 (Uptown–Riverside), and Light Rail Line B (Riverside stop two blocks away). downtown_community_arts_center Downtown Community Arts Center A renovated warehouse hosting exhibitions, music performances, and hands-on arts workshops for all ages. 415 Market Street Suite 100 Downtown +1 (415) 555-1122 hello@downtownarts.org Automatic doors at main entrance, elevator access to mezzanine classrooms, accessible seating in performance hall. Please contact us ahead of time for specific accommodation requests. Exit Hwy 80 at 7th St, continue north to Market St, then turn right. Limited street parking; recommended to use nearby public garages on 5th or 8th St. From Civic Plaza, walk three blocks east along Market St. The center is on the right, with a large mural on the facade. Located one block from Civic Plaza Station (Lines A, C, and D) and directly on Bus 3 and 5 routes. greenfield_park Greenfield Park A large urban park with open lawns, playgrounds, and an outdoor stage used for family concerts and festivals. 800 Greenfield Blvd Greenfield +1 (415) 555-3344 parks@cityofriverton.gov Paved paths connect parking areas to the playground and stage. Some lawn seating areas have uneven ground; a paved viewing area is reserved for wheelchair users. From Hwy 101, take the Greenfield Blvd exit and head west for 1 mile. Park entrances are on both sides of the boulevard with two main parking lots. From Greenfield Station, follow the signed pedestrian path north for 6 minutes to the south park entrance. Served by Bus 2 and 9 with stops along Greenfield Blvd; Greenfield light rail station is a 6-minute walk. harborview_senior_center Harborview Senior Center A welcoming community center offering programs, social events, and wellness activities tailored for older adults. 60 Harborview Lane Harborview +1 (415) 555-7788 programs@harborviewseniors.org Fully accessible single-level facility with grab bars in restrooms, wide corridors, and curb cuts at the main entrance. Staff can assist with mobility devices. Take the Harborview exit off Bayside Fwy and follow signs toward the marina. Turn left on Harborview Lane; the center is at the end of the c

pages.dataKeywords: birthday_balloons Birthday Balloons birthday spotlight_generic Spotlight Stage generic holiday_marquee Holiday Marquee holiday thanks_spotlight Thank You Applause thank_you dropin_3_pack 3-Class Drop-In Sampler Try three adult improv drop-in classes over 60 days. Perfect for first-timers or busy schedules, this sampler includes admission to any three adult improv drop-in classes. Valid for 60 days from first use at our Training Center locations. drop_in_pass Non-transferable. Good for adult drop-in classes only. Must be used within 60 days of first visit. dropin_weekend_trio Weekend Warrior Drop-In Trio Three Saturday or Sunday drop-ins at a special rate. Includes three credits good for any weekend (Saturday or Sunday) adult improv drop-in class. Ideal for busy professionals planning a few fun weekends. Valid only for designated weekend drop-in classes. Expires 45 days after first redemption. No refunds for unused visits. dropin_4_pack_evening Weeknight Improv 4-Pack Four evening drop-in classes, Monday–Thursday. Save on your practice with four credits for our Monday–Thursday evening adult drop-in improv classes at the Training Center. Good for any scheduled adult evening drop-in class. All four visits must be used within 90 days of first visit. dropin_5_pack 5-Class Drop-In Pass Five flexible drop-in visits for ongoing practice. Includes five visits to any adult improv drop-in class on the schedule. Great for performers looking to keep their skills sharp all season. Visits may be shared between family members but not resold. All visits must be used before the expiration date. show_date_night Date Night Show Pass Two general admission tickets for any Friday or Saturday show. Make it a night out with two general admission tickets for any regularly scheduled Friday or Saturday evening improv show in our Mainstage Theater. show_pass Subject to availability. Not valid for special events or festivals. Both tickets must be used for the same performance. show_family_matinee_4pack Family Matinee 4-Pack Four tickets to any family-friendly matinee. Bring the whole family to laugh together with four tickets good for any all-ages or family-friendly matinee show. Valid only for designated family-friendly matinee performances. Tickets may be used across multiple dates. class_level1_pass Level 1 Class Pass Covers tuition for one 8-week Level 1 improv class. Apply this pass to pay for any 8-week beginner (Level 1) adult improv class. Ideal as a gift when you don’t know which start date they’ll choose. class_pass Redeemable for one qualifying 8-week adult improv class. Registration is required; classes may fill up. class_plus_show_bundle Class + Show Starter Bundle One 8-week class plus two show tickets. pages.dataKeywords:
art_1 Step-by-Step Guide to Transitioning to Organic Vegetable Farming transitioning-to-organic-vegetable-farming A practical 3-season roadmap for small farms and backyard growers moving from synthetic inputs to fully certified-organic vegetable production. Dr. Elena Morales organic_transition vegetables small_scale_farms organic_farming_basics published art_2 Converting a Conventional Orchard to Organic: A 3-Year Transition Plan converting-orchard-to-organic-3-year-plan Learn how to phase out synthetic inputs and establish a resilient, organic orchard system over three seasons. Marcus Lee orchards tree_crops art_3 Beginner’s Guide to Organic Fertilizers for Home Gardens beginners-guide-organic-fertilizers-home-gardens Confused by compost, manure, and organic NPK labels? This guide breaks down what home gardeners really need. Home gardeners are often overwhelmed by fertilizer choices. Organic options can be grouped into three main categories: composts and manures, granular base fertilizers, and liquid or foliar feeds.

We explain where each type fits, how to read organic labels, and how to avoid over-fertilizing raised beds and small vegetable plots. Sarah Nguyen home_gardening organic_fertilizers small_vegetable_gardens art_4 How to Choose the Right Organic Tomato Fertilizer choose-right-organic-tomato-fertilizer Tomatoes are heavy feeders. Learn how to pick the most suitable organic fertilizer for your soil, budget, and growing system. Tomatoes require consistent nutrition throughout the growing season. In organic systems, this usually means combining a slow-release base fertilizer with periodic side-dressings or liquid feeds.

We compare different N-P-K ratios, discuss calcium for blossom-end rot, and outline how to interpret labels on tomato-specific organic fertilizers. tomatoes crop_fertilization art_5 Understanding NPK in Organic Form understanding-npk-in-organic-form A technical yet practical look at nitrogen, phosphorus, and potassium in organic fertilizers, and how they release over time. NPK in organic fertilizers behaves differently than in synthetic products. Nutrients are bound in complex organic molecules and require microbial activity to become plant-available.

We explore common organic nitrogen sources, rock phosphate, and sulfate of potash magnesia, and explain how to match release patterns to crop demand. Prof. Daniel Ortiz nutrient_management soil_health technical_guides art_6 Compost vs Manure: What’s Best for Your Soil? compost-vs-manure-whats-best Compare the benefits and risks of compost and raw or aged manures for improving soil fertility and structure. Both compost and manure can boost soil organic matter, but they behave differently in the field. Compost is usually more stable and weed-free, while manures can deliver a quick nutrient boost but carry higher risk of nutrient loss and pathogens.

We provide guidelines for safe application rates, timing, and how to combine compost with other organic fertilizers. composting

pages.dataKeywords: sheet_pan_tofu_veggies_teriyaki Sheet Pan Tofu & Veggie Teriyaki sheet-pan-tofu-veggie-teriyaki Crispy baked tofu with a medley of vegetables in a light teriyaki glaze, all roasted on one pan for an easy high-protein vegetarian dinner. dinner vegetarian extra-firm tofu broccoli florets red bell pepper carrot red onion olive oil low-sodium soy sauce maple syrup rice vinegar garlic fresh ginger cornstarch sesame seeds Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C) and line a large sheet pan with parchment paper. Press the tofu for 10–15 minutes, then cut into 1-inch cubes. Chop the broccoli, bell pepper, carrot, and red onion into bite-size pieces. In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, maple syrup, rice vinegar, minced garlic, grated ginger, and cornstarch. Toss tofu cubes with half of the sauce and spread on one side of the sheet pan. Toss vegetables with olive oil and the remaining sauce, then spread on the other side. Bake for 25–30 minutes, flipping tofu and stirring vegetables halfway, until tofu is golden and edges are crisp. Sprinkle with sesame seeds and serve hot over rice or quinoa if desired. lentil_spinach_curry_brown_rice Lentil & Spinach Curry with Brown Rice lentil-spinach-curry-brown-rice Creamy red lentil and spinach curry simmered in coconut-tomato sauce, served over nutty brown rice for a satisfying vegetarian dinner. dry red lentils baby spinach onion ginger curry powder ground cumin diced tomatoes light coconut milk vegetable broth brown rice, cooked Rinse lentils under cold water until the water runs clear. Heat olive oil in a pot over medium heat. Add diced onion and sauté 3–4 minutes until softened. Stir in minced garlic, grated ginger, curry powder, and cumin, and cook 1 minute until fragrant. Add lentils, diced tomatoes, vegetable broth, and coconut milk. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer 18–20 minutes until lentils are tender. Stir in spinach and cook 2–3 minutes until wilted. Adjust salt and pepper to taste. Serve curry over bowls of warm cooked brown rice. edamame_veggie_stir_fry High-Protein Veggie Stir-Fry with Edamame high-protein-veggie-stir-fry-edamame Colorful stir-fried vegetables with shelled edamame in a garlic-ginger sauce, ready in under 30 minutes. shelled edamame snap peas green onion sesame oil neutral oil Prep the vegetables: slice bell pepper and carrot, trim snap peas, and chop green onions. In a small bowl, whisk soy sauce, minced garlic, grated ginger, cornstarch, and 2 tablespoons of water. Heat neutral oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Add carrots and bell pepper, stir-frying 2–3 minutes, then add snap peas and edamame. Pour in the sauce and cook, stirring, for 3–4 minutes until thickened and vegetables are crisp-tender. Finish with sesame oil and half the green onions. Garnish with remaining green onions and serve over rice or noodles. mushroom_quinoa_pilaf Mushroom & Quinoa Protein Pilaf mushroom-quinoa-protein-pilaf Hearty quinoa pilaf with sautéed mushrooms, peas, and toast

pages.dataKeywords: c001 Schrödinger's Coffee Break The office cat is both attending and ignoring the stand-up until someone opens the treat drawer. multi_panel_strip c002 Open Office Banana Bandit Someone keeps leaving banana peels by the manager's standing desk. HR suspects the intern, who suspects the monkey plush. c003 Inbox of Holding An office worker discovers their unread emails are actually a low-level dungeon populated by out-of-office replies. multi_page c004 Morning Hedgehog Affirmations A hedgehog in a bathrobe gives wholesome pep talks to its reflection before rolling into the day. c005 Clouds Have Feelings Too A grumpy rain cloud gets cheered up by being told it waters future coffee plants. c006 Pigeon Self-Care Day A city pigeon cancels all meetings to take a luxurious breadcrumb bath in the park. c007 Grandma's Wi-Fi Password The whole family gathers to decode grandma's 48-character handwritten Wi-Fi password taped to the router. c008 Monday Mug Motivation A coffee mug cycles through increasingly desperate motivational quotes as the day goes on. c009 Midnight Fridge Negotiations A person and their fridge light haggle over whether cheese counts as a snack or a meal. c010 Cat on Important Keyboard A smug cat sits on a laptop and improves the quarterly report by typing only the letter 'm'. single_panel c011 Laser Pointer Performance Review A cat attends its performance review where the only KPI is 'chasing the red dot'. c012 Ceiling Cat's Annual Report Ceiling Cat presents a pie chart showing 'time spent judging humans' vs 'time spent napping'. c013 Cat Versus Productivity App Every time the productivity app sends a focus reminder, the cat walks across the screen. c014 Salad vs. Pizza Debate A salad and a slice of pizza argue over who is the 'better life choice' while the human orders fries. c015 Dark Roast, Darker Thoughts A sentient coffee mug gives uncomfortably honest motivational quotes before 7 a.m. c016 The Void Stares Back (And Schedules a Meeting) An employee accidentally invites the existential void to a recurring calendar event. c017 Infinite Email Thread A reply-all chain becomes self-aware and starts adding its own recipients. c018 Dungeon of Lost Socks A brave adventurer enters the dryer Please bring your order confirmation email. collection home_delivery Home delivery Have your items delivered to your home address 7–10 days before race day. shipping express_collection Express collection from info tent Priority pick-up from the information tent on race day from 8:30 AM onwards. dog_policy Can I bring my dog to the event? For the safety and comfort of all participants, only assistance dogs are allowed within the main event arena, race route and start/finish areas. Well-behaved pet dogs on short leads are welcome in the outer park and picnic areas but may not enter crowded spectator pens or indoor spaces.

If you have a registered assistance or therapy dog and are unsure whether they meet our criteria, please contact us in advance using the contact form so we can discuss your individual situation. event_policies dogs policies spectators accessibility can-i-bring-my-dog-to-the-event assistance_dogs Are assistance dogs allowed on the race route? Yes. Registered assistance dogs are welcome on the race route and in all spectator areas. We may ask to see supporting documentation on arrival. Please ensure your dog is wearing their assistance harness or jacket and that you bring water and waste bags. assistance-dogs-on-race-route registration_deadline What is the deadline to register a pram race team? Online registration for pram race teams closes at 6:00 PM on Friday 9 May, or earlier if a race category reaches capacity. A small number of on-the-day places may be available

pages.dataKeywords: winter_exterior_painting_guide Exterior Painting in Winter: Safe, Durable Results in Cold Weather exterior-painting-in-winter-cold-weather-guide exterior Yes, you can paint your home’s exterior in winter—if you follow the right temperature, moisture, and product guidelines. winter cold_weather exterior_painting seasonal home_exterior Jordan Lee prep_home_for_winter_paint How to Prepare Your Home for Winter Painting Projects prepare-home-for-winter-painting Simple steps you can take before your painters arrive to keep winter projects on schedule. Winter painting, both interior and exterior, requires a bit of extra planning. Indoors, we recommend clearing furniture away from walls, making sure heat can run consistently, and cracking windows briefly during and after painting for ventilation. Outdoors, trim back plants that block access, move patio furniture, and check that downspouts and gutters are flowing freely so surfaces stay dry. This article includes a printable checklist you can follow the week before your winter project starts. checklist interior_painting Alex Martinez low_odor_paints_guide Low-Odor and Zero-VOC Paints: What You Need to Know low-odor-zero-voc-paints-guide color_advice Curious about low-VOC and zero-VOC paints? We break down the labels and what they mean for your home. VOC stands for volatile organic compounds—chemicals that evaporate into the air as paint dries. In this article, we explain the difference between standard, low-VOC, and zero-VOC paints, including how regulations define each category. You’ll learn where low-VOC makes the biggest difference (nurseries, bedrooms, and high-use living spaces), how colorants can affect VOC levels, and how to balance washability and durability with eco-friendly formulas. zero_voc low_voc indoor_air_quality health Morgan Patel kitchen_color_trends_2025 Kitchen Color Trends 2025: From Soft Blues to Bold Greens kitchen-color-trends-2025 Explore the most requested kitchen paint colors, including soft blue-greys and grounded earth tones. Kitchens are evolving into softer, more calming spaces, and paint colors are leading the way. This article highlights our most requested kitchen palettes for 2025, from misty blue-greys for walls to deeper navy islands and warm white cabinets. We include real project photos, recommended sheen levels for walls, trim, and cabinets, and tips for pairing paint with existing countertops and flooring. kitchen color_trends blue green Jamie Rivera interior_painting_timeline How Long Does Interior Painting Take? Room-by-Room Timeline interior-painting-timeline-room-by-room interior Plan your project with realistic timeframes for bedrooms, living rooms, kitchens, and more. Interior painting timelines vary depending on room size, condition, and added services like furniture moving or wall repair. pages.dataKeywords:
art_team_tips_new_managers 12 Team Building Tips for New Managers team-building-tips-for-new-managers New to managing a team? Use these 12 practical team building tips to build trust, set norms, and create momentum in your first 90 days. Erica Daniels team building tips new managers team leads leadership first 90 days team_building resources/team-building-tips-for-new-managers.html art_hybrid_icebreakers 15 Quick Icebreakers for Hybrid Meetings quick-icebreakers-for-hybrid-meetings Keep both in-room and remote teammates engaged with these 15 easy hybrid-friendly icebreakers. Hybrid meetings can easily slide into a one-sided experience if you don’t intentionally design for inclusion. These 15 icebreakers are built to work whether people are in the room, remote, or a mix of both.

We’ll cover name games, visual prompts, word clouds, and quick polls that take under 5 minutes each. You’ll also find facilitator notes on how to avoid common pitfalls, like side conversations in the room or tech delays on video calls. Marcus Patel icebreakers hybrid teams meeting facilitation resources/quick-icebreakers-for-hybrid-meetings.html art_chicago_offsite_budget How to Plan a Chicago Offsite on a Budget plan-chicago-offsite-on-a-budget A step-by-step guide for HR and operations teams planning a cost-effective Chicago offsite without sacrificing impact. Chicago offers a huge range of venues and activities, but costs can add up quickly. In this guide, we break down how to choose a neighborhood, build a realistic budget, prioritize activities, and use half-day formats to stretch your dollars. Jordan Lee chicago offsite planning budget pricing offsite_planning HR teams resources/plan-chicago-offsite-on-a-budget.html art_virtual_activities_that_work Virtual Team Building Activities That Actually Work virtual-team-building-activities-that-work From quick games to deeper workshops, here are proven virtual formats that keep remote teams engaged. Zoom fatigue is real, but so is the need for connection. This article walks through different types of virtual team building—icebreakers, skill-building workshops, and recurring micro-rituals—plus when to use each. pages.dataKeywords: getting_started_overview Getting Started with Stablecoin Insights getting-started-with-stablecoin-insights This article walks you through the main sections of Stablecoin Insights, including the Stablecoin Directory, Learn, News, Tools, Risk Quiz, and Help Center. You’ll learn how to navigate from the homepage, understand the basic layout, and find resources relevant to your experience level. Use the top navigation bar to move between sections, and the footer for quick access to Saved Items, Newsletter, and Contact pages. getting_started getting started homepage site navigation stablecoin insights overview how to use this website account_and_saved_items Using Saved Items for Watchlists and Reading Lists using-saved-items-watchlists-reading-lists The Saved Items page lets you keep track of stablecoins you watchlist and articles you save for later. From the Stablecoin Directory, click the watchlist icon next to a coin to add it to Saved Items. From any article in Learn or News, click the bookmark icon to save it. Your Saved Items page groups content by stablecoins, educational articles, and news, and provides quick links back to their detail pages. accounts saved items watchlist reading list bookmark articles track stablecoins using_stablecoin_directory How to Use the Stablecoin Directory Filters and Comparison how-to-use-stablecoin-directory-filters-and-comparison The Stablecoin Directory lets you filter coins by peg currency, risk score, market cap, and more. Use the Peg Currency filter to focus on USD, EUR, or GBP stablecoins. The Risk Score slider narrows coins by our 1–10 risk rating, where lower scores indicate less risk. Market cap filters help you focus on larger or mid-cap coins. You can sort results by Market Cap – High to Low and select the comparison checkbox on up to three coins to open a side-by-side comparison view. stablecoin directory filter by peg currency risk score filter market cap filter compare stablecoins understanding_risk_scores Understanding Stablecoin Risk Scores understanding-stablecoin-risk-scores Each stablecoin in the directory has a numeric risk score from 1 (lowest risk) to 10 (highest risk). The score is based on reserve transparency, regulatory posture, peg mechanism, market liquidity, and historical incidents such as depegs. Use the Risk Score filter to find low-risk options, and combine it with peg currency and market cap to narrow your shortlist. Risk scores are updated periodically as new information becomes available. other risk score low risk stablecoins risk methodology peg stability depeg events redeem_to_bank How to Redeem Stablecoins for Cash and Withdraw to Your Bank how-to-redeem-stablecoins-for-cash-and-withdraw-to-your-bank conversions redeem stablecoins for cash cash out stablecoins withdraw to bank account convert stablecoins back to my bank redeem usdc redeem eur stablecoin conversion_tool_guide Using the Stablecoin Conversion Simulator using-the-stablecoin-conversion-simulator conversion tool sta

pages.dataKeywords: ind_50_new New Client - 50 minute Individual Session individual_50_new individual_therapy ind_50_return Return Client - 50 minute Individual Session individual_50_return ind_60_standard Individual Therapy - 60 minute Session individual_60_standard cpl_75_new New Couple - 75 minute Session couples_75_new couples_therapy cpl_75_return Return Couple - 75 minute Session couples_75_return grp_75_stress Stress Management Group - 75 minute Session group_75_stress_mgmt stress_management_group sleep_hygiene_basics Sleep Hygiene Basics for Anxious Minds sleep-hygiene-basics-for-anxious-minds Practical steps to create a calmer nighttime routine and improve sleep when you live with anxiety. Good sleep hygiene is a set of habits that support deeper, more restorative rest. For people who experience anxiety, winding down at night can be especially challenging. In this article, we cover how to create a consistent bedtime routine, set up a sleep-friendly environment, limit stimulants and screen time, and respond skillfully when your mind starts racing as you try to fall asleep. Dr. Elena Park, PhD Sleep Insomnia Anxiety Self-care cbt_for_insomnia Using CBT-I to Treat Chronic Insomnia using-cbt-i-to-treat-chronic-insomnia An overview of Cognitive Behavioral Therapy for Insomnia (CBT-I) and what to expect from treatment. Cognitive Behavioral Therapy for Insomnia (CBT-I) is an evidence-based treatment that targets the thoughts and behaviors that keep insomnia going. Sessions typically focus on understanding your sleep patterns, adjusting time in bed, and restructuring unhelpful beliefs about sleep. Unlike sleep medications, CBT-I aims to resolve the underlying problem so that improvements last long after treatment ends. Lisa Nguyen, LCSW CBT-I Evidence-based screen_time_and_sleep How Evening Screen Time Impacts Your Sleep how-evening-screen-time-impacts-your-sleep Learn how blue light and late-night scrolling affect your ability to fall and stay asleep. Phones, tablets, and laptops emit blue light that signals to your brain that it is still daytime. This suppresses melatonin, the hormone that tells your body it is time to sleep. In addition, late-night scrolling often exposes you to emotionally activating content, making it harder to unwind. We outline realistic strategies to create healthier boundaries with technology without requiring a complete digital detox. Dr. Maya Rodriguez, PsyD Screen time Habits racing_thoughts_at_night Taming Racing Thoughts at Night taming-racing-thoughts-at-night If your brain speeds up when your head hits the pillow, these strategies can help you slow it down. Racing thoughts at bedtime are a common experience for people dealing with anxiety and stress. Instead of trying to force your mind to be blank, it is often more effective to gently redirect your attention and give your brain a structured way to wind down. This article explores techniques like scheduled worry time, grounding exercises, and journaling prompts to help your mind shi

pages.dataKeywords: prog_msc_data_science_applied MSc in Applied Data Science masters data_science english A two-year program focused on practical data science skills for industry and research applications. Core modules in statistics, machine learning, data engineering, and visualization; electives in AI, natural language processing, and responsible data science; capstone industry project. Bachelor’s degree in a quantitative field, proficiency in programming (Python or R), and English language proficiency (IELTS 6.5 or equivalent). City Campus prog_msc_data_science_big_data MSc in Data Science and Big Data Analytics Specialized training in large-scale data processing, cloud computing, and advanced analytics. Courses in distributed systems, large-scale machine learning, database systems, and data ethics; integrated internship in tech or finance. Bachelor’s degree in computer science, engineering, mathematics, or related field; experience with programming and linear algebra. Tech Park Campus prog_msc_data_science_ai MSc in Data Science and Artificial Intelligence An interdisciplinary program at the intersection of data science and artificial intelligence. Machine learning theory, deep learning, reinforcement learning, causal inference, and AI ethics; research thesis in collaboration with an institute lab. Strong mathematical background, prior coursework in algorithms and probability, and demonstrated interest in AI. Main Campus prog_msc_data_science_german MSc in Data Science (German Track) german German-language track focusing on data science methods and applications in Europe. Core modules in statistical learning, databases, and data visualization with German-language seminars and projects. Bachelorabschluss in einem quantitativen Fach und Deutschkenntnisse auf Niveau C1. prog_msc_machine_learning MSc in Machine Learning machine_learning Advanced training in core and modern machine learning techniques with strong theoretical foundations. Statistical learning theory, deep learning, probabilistic modeling, optimization, and research seminars; independent research project. Bachelor’s degree in mathematics, computer science, or related; strong background in calculus, linear algebra, and programming. prog_msc_neuroscience MSc in Integrative Neuroscience neuroscience A research-focused program covering systems, cognitive, and computational neuroscience. Neuroanatomy, systems neuroscience, cognitive neuroscience, neuroimaging methods, and lab rotations in partner labs. Bachelor’s degree in neuroscience, biology, psychology, or related field; motivation letter and two references. Medical Campus prog_bsc_computer_science BSc in Computer Science bachelors computer_science Undergraduate program covering foundational topics in computer science. Programming, data structures, algorithms, databases, operating systems, and introductory AI. Secondary school diploma with strong performance in mathematics. prog_phd_computer_science PhD in Computer Science Doctoral program emphasizing

pages.dataKeywords: addon_battery_trickle battery_trickle_charger Battery Trickle Charger Keep your RV or boat battery maintained with a dedicated 110V outlet and low-amp trickle charger at your space. rv_storage boat_storage addon_exterior_wash exterior_wash_area_access Exterior Wash Area Access Access to our on-site wash bay with hose, sprayer, and drainage for rinsing off your RV or boat. addon_gate_24_7 gate_access_24_7 24/7 Gate Access Upgrade from standard gate hours to 24/7 keypad access for maximum flexibility. faq_security_deposit billing_payments Is there a security deposit? Yes. We require a $75 refundable security deposit per space at the time of move-in. The deposit is held to cover any unpaid rent, fees, or damages. As long as your account is paid in full and your space is left clean and undamaged, the full $75 security deposit is automatically refunded to your original payment method within 10 business days after you move out. faq_autopay How does autopay work? Autopay automatically charges your preferred payment method on your monthly due date so you never miss a payment. You can enable or disable autopay anytime from the My Account > Billing & Autopay section. We currently support credit and debit cards for autopay. Changes made at least one day before your due date will apply to the upcoming charge. faq_change_reservation reservations Can I change my move-in date or switch to a different space after reserving? Yes. You can modify most reservations online before your move-in date. From the My Account dashboard, go to My Reservations, select the reservation you want to change, and use the Edit or Change Unit & Date option. You will see available spaces and pricing for your new date. If the new space costs more, you will be shown the updated monthly rate before confirming the change. faq_gate_hours access What are your gate hours? Standard gate access hours are 6:00 AM to 10:00 PM, 7 days a week, including holidays. For customers who need additional flexibility, we offer an optional 24/7 Gate Access add-on at select locations. Office hours vary by facility, so please check the facility details page for specific office times. faq_vehicles_allowed general What types of vehicles can I store? Our facilities are designed to accommodate a wide range of vehicles, including Class A, B, and C motorhomes, travel trailers, fifth wheels, camper vans, toy haulers, boats on trailers, personal watercraft, and some commercial vehicles. Each space has a listed maximum length and type (RV or boat), so please choose a space that matches your vehicle and length, including the trailer and hitch. faq_security_features security What security features do your facilities have? Most of our facilities feature computerized gate access, perimeter fencing, bright LED lighting, and 24/7 digital video surveillance. Facility managers perform regular walkthroughs of the property. While no storage facility can guarantee complete security, we invest heavily in multiple layers of protect

pages.dataKeywords:
apt_new_patient_eval_60 New Patient Physical Therapy Evaluation new_patient_physical_therapy_evaluation A comprehensive 60-minute evaluation for new patients, including movement assessment, goal setting, and a personalized home exercise plan. apt_back_pain_eval_60 Back Pain Evaluation (60 min) back_pain_evaluation_60_min A focused 60-minute evaluation for patients with neck, mid-back, or low back pain, including posture and core strength assessment. apt_follow_up_30 Follow-Up Visit (30 min) follow_up_visit_30_min A 30-minute follow-up session to progress your exercise plan and address current symptoms. apt_telehealth_30 Telehealth Check-In (30 min) telehealth_check_in_30_min A virtual 30-minute session for exercise progressions, form checks, and questions. blog_stronger_shoulders_at_home_2025 3 Simple Shoulder Exercises You Can Do at Home simple-shoulder-exercises-at-home Build stronger, more stable shoulders with three no-equipment exercises you can safely do in your living room. Maya Patel, PT, DPT shoulder home_exercise rotator_cuff beginner blog_posture_for_shoulder_health_2024 Posture Tips for Happier Shoulders at Your Desk posture-tips-for-shoulder-health-at-your-desk Small changes to your workstation and posture can significantly reduce shoulder and neck tension. Long hours at the computer can leave your shoulders feeling tight, achy, or even burning. The good news is that improving your posture and workstation setup can dramatically reduce strain.

In this article we cover: ideal monitor height, how to position your mouse and keyboard to protect your shoulders, and three quick shoulder mobility drills you can do without leaving your chair.

Remember, the best posture is the next posture—aim to change positions every 30–45 minutes. Sophia Ross, PT, DPT posture ergonomics office_workers blog_return_to_sport_after_shoulder_sprain_2023 How to Safely Return to Sport After a Shoulder Injury return-to-sport-after-shoulder-injury A step-by-step guide to getting back to tennis, swimming, or overhead lifting after a shoulder sprain or strain. Returning to sport too quickly after a shoulder injury is one of the main reasons we see repeat issues in the clinic.

We break down a simple 4-phase progression: pain control, mobility restoration, strength and control, and sport-specific loading. You’ll also learn key ‘green light’ and ‘red flag’ signs to help you know when it’s time to progress or hold back.

If your shoulder pain persists beyond 2–3 weeks or interferes with sleep, schedule an in-person evaluation. Daniel Kim, PT, DPT, OCS sports_rehab return_to_sport blog_sleep_positions_for_shoulder_pain_2023 Best Sleep Positions for Shoulder Pain Relief sleep-positions-for-shoulder-pain-relief Learn how to position your arm, pillow, and body to reduce nighttime shoulder pain. Nighttime shoulder pain can make it nearly impossible to get quality rest, which slows down healing.

We share our favorite pillow setups for side-sleepers and back-sleepers, plus t

addon_video_call Video Call (Zoom/Meet) for Live Direction video_call Add a high-quality Zoom or Google Meet video call to your live directed voice session so producers and clients can listen in and give feedback in real time. voice_session addon_extra_revision Extra Revision Round extra_revision Includes one additional round of script or mix revisions beyond the standard package. service_bundle addon_transcription_basic Basic Transcription transcription Human-reviewed transcript of your finished audio up to 30 minutes. other addon_priority_delivery Priority 48-Hour Delivery priority_delivery Upgrade your project to 48-hour turnaround where available. addon_subscription_onboarding Subscription Onboarding Call One-time 45-minute onboarding call to plan workflows and templates for subscription clients. subscription_plan blog_home_recording_tips_1 10 Home Recording Tips for Pro-Sounding Voiceovers 10-home-recording-tips-for-pro-sounding-voiceovers Turn your spare room into a reliable home studio with these practical home recording tips. recording_tips home recording voiceover tips studio vs home beginners Chris Lane blog_studio_vs_home_recording Studio vs Home Recording: When to Book a Booth and When to DIY studio-vs-home-recording-when-to-book-a-booth-and-when-to-diy Not sure whether to record at home or in a pro studio? We compare costs, quality, and convenience to help you decide. budgeting Jamie Patel blog_podcast_editing_basics Podcast Editing Basics: From Raw Recording to Release podcast-editing-basics-from-raw-recording-to-release A step-by-step walkthrough of our podcast editing process, from noise cleanup to final loudness. Editing a podcast is about more than cutting out ums. In this article, we outline our standard podcast editing workflow: organizing tracks, removing distractions, repairing audio issues, balancing levels, and exporting to platform-ready formats.

We also explain why a 45-minute episode typically takes 3–4x its runtime to edit properly, and how our podcast editing service tiers work—especially the options that include 48-hour delivery. podcasting podcast editing workflow loudness post production Morgan Lee blog_remote_directed_sessions How Remote Directed Sessions Work for Commercial Voiceover how-remote-directed-sessions-work-for-commercial-voiceover Learn what to expect from a live directed commercial voiceover session with Zoom or Meet. Remote directed sessions let creatives and clients listen live while a voice actor records from our studio or their treated home booth. Using high-quality audio connections and an optional video call add-on, you can give real-time feedback on performance, pacing, and pronunciation.

In this guide we walk through scheduling, tech checks, script preparation, and best practices for keeping a 60-minute session efficient and focused. tutorials commercial voiceover remote session video call blog_elearing_voice_tips Voiceover Tips for Engaging eLearning Narration voiceover-tips-for-engaging-el

pages.dataKeywords: education_outreach_committee Education & Outreach Committee Oversees the society’s educational programs and outreach activities for students, educators, and the public. education_outreach To expand access to high-quality scientific education and foster public engagement with research. Design outreach initiatives, evaluate education grant proposals, coordinate with regional chapters, and develop teaching resources. public_engagement_task_force Public Engagement Task Force Leads short-term projects to improve public understanding of science through events, media, and online campaigns. To increase the visibility and impact of member research among non-specialist audiences. Organize public lectures, manage social media campaigns, collaborate with schools and community organizations. k12_curriculum_working_group K–12 Curriculum Working Group Develops and curates curriculum materials aligned with national science standards for primary and secondary education. To provide age-appropriate, evidence-based curriculum materials that highlight current research. Create lesson plans, review submitted materials, pilot resources with partner schools, and collect feedback. online_learning_advisory_group Online Learning Advisory Group Advises on the society’s webinars, online courses, and digital learning platforms. To enhance the accessibility and effectiveness of virtual education offerings. Review proposals for new courses, recommend learning technologies, and assess user feedback and analytics. education_policy_panel Education Policy Panel Monitors developments in science education policy and coordinates society responses. To represent the society in discussions about science education policy and funding. Draft position statements, respond to calls for comment, and collaborate with the policy committee. research_committee Research Committee Provides strategic guidance on the society’s research priorities and grant programs. research To support high-impact research across all disciplines represented in the society. Set review criteria, recruit peer reviewers, and evaluate outcomes of funded projects. policy_advocacy_committee Policy & Advocacy Committee Coordinates advocacy on issues affecting research funding and scientific integrity. policy_advocacy To promote evidence-based policy and protect the interests of the scientific community. Develop advocacy campaigns, prepare policy briefs, and train members in science communication to policymakers. membership_engagement_council Membership Engagement Council Focuses on recruitment, retention, and member experience. membership_engagement To build an engaged, diverse, and growing membership. Design member surveys, propose new benefits, and oversee onboarding initiatives. diversity_inclusion_commission Diversity & Inclusion Commission Works to improve equity, diversity, and inclusion across all society activities. diversity_inclusion To foster an inclusive culture that supports scientists from all backgrounds. pages.dataKeywords:
art_tender_strategy_foundations Practical Tender Strategy for Complex Public Procurements practical-tender-strategy-for-complex-public-procurements A step‑by‑step tender strategy framework for public buyers running complex, multi-lot procurements. Dr. Elaine Porter tender strategy public procurement evaluation models stakeholder management art_it_software_rfp_500k Writing IT Software RFPs Over $500k: A Complete Guide writing-it-software-rfps-over-500k-complete-guide Guidance for drafting high-value IT and software RFPs above $500,000, including requirements, scoring, and negotiation levers. High-value IT and software RFPs (>$500k) combine technical complexity with commercial risk. This guide covers how to structure your RFP, define mandatory vs. scored requirements, design evaluation criteria, and plan for clarifications and BAFO rounds.

We also provide a downloadable RFP template and checklist in our Resources section. At the end of the article you will find recommended reading on tender evaluation and risk allocation. Michael Zhang IT procurement software sourcing art_eprocurement_platforms E-Procurement Platforms: Making Tenders Truly Digital e-procurement-platforms-making-tenders-truly-digital How e-procurement platforms can streamline tender creation, publishing, and evaluation. Digital tendering tools can reduce cycle times by up to 30%. In this article we compare different e-procurement platform capabilities, from online questionnaires to automated scoring and audit trails.

We also outline a practical roadmap for migrating from email-based submissions to a fully digital process. Sara Lindholm e-procurement tender management digital transformation art_vendor_evaluation_models Designing Vendor Evaluation Models That Drive Value designing-vendor-evaluation-models-that-drive-value Best practices for weighting cost, quality, lead time, and risk in vendor evaluation matrices. A well-structured vendor evaluation matrix turns subjective preferences into transparent, auditable decisions. We explain how to choose criteria, assign weights that reflect strategic priorities, and avoid double-counting.

An accompanying interactive Vendor Evaluation Matrix tool is available in our Tools section. vendor evaluation scoring models risk management art_training_needs_procurement Assessing Procurement Training Needs Across Your Organization assessing-procurement-training-needs-across-your-organization A structured approach to identifying procurement training gaps and building a targeted training plan. Organizations often struggle to prioritize procurement training. This article introduces a capability matrix and survey approach to identify gaps in tendering, negotiation, category management, and contract management skills.

We also show how to map gaps to specific courses from our Training & Workshops catalogue. Lauren Davies training procurement capability tendering skills art_logistics_tendering Logistics Tendering: Balancing Cost, Service and Risk logisti

pages.dataKeywords: adult_general Adult adult General admission for adults ages 18–64. child_general Child child Admission for children ages 6–17. Children 5 and under are free. senior_general Senior senior Discounted admission for seniors ages 65+. student_general Student student Discounted admission with valid student ID. member_admission Member member Free general admission for current museum members. family_twilight_tour_game_night Family Twilight Tour & Game Night family-twilight-tour-game-night An evening guided tour of the house followed by historic parlor games for all ages. Bring the whole family for a special twilight visit to Oakwood Manor. Enjoy a shortened guided tour designed for younger visitors, then head to the parlor for historic board and card games, light refreshments, and a family photo station. family_program summer_porch_concert_series Summer Porch Concert Series summer-porch-concert-series Outdoor evening concerts on the front porch featuring local musicians. Relax on the lawn and enjoy live music from jazz quartets, folk duos, and classical ensembles performing from the historic front porch. Picnics and blankets are welcome. Limited chair seating provided. concert evening_preservation_lecture Evening Lecture: Preservation in Practice evening-lecture-preservation-in-practice A public lecture on current historic preservation projects at Oakwood Manor. Join our preservation architect for an in-depth look at recent and upcoming projects to stabilize, restore, and reinterpret Oakwood Manor. Learn about how decisions are made, how materials are sourced, and how the public can support ongoing work. lecture victorian_holiday_candlelight_tour Victorian Holiday Candlelight Tour victorian-holiday-candlelight-tour A special candlelit tour of the house decorated for the holidays. Experience Oakwood Manor by candlelight as costumed guides share stories of Victorian holiday traditions. Warm up with spiced cider in the kitchen and listen to carols in the music room. public_program garden_history_walk Garden History Walk garden-history-walk A guided stroll through the historic gardens and grounds. Explore the evolution of Oakwood’s gardens, from kitchen plots and orchards to ornamental landscaping. This outdoor program covers roughly one mile over gently sloping paths. members_only_archive_tour Members-Only Archive Tour members-only-archive-tour Behind-the-scenes visit to the archives exclusively for members. Go behind the scenes with our curator to view original letters, photographs, and artifacts rarely seen by the public. Limited capacity; registration required. other childrens_hands_on_history_morning Children's Hands-On History Morning childrens-hands-on-history-morning Drop-off program with crafts and activities for ages 7–11. Young historians investigate artifacts, play historic games, and create a take-home craft inspired by life at Oakwood Manor. Snack provided. Pre-registration required. historic_cooking_demo Crafting the Past: Historic Cooking Dem

pages.dataKeywords:
access_affordable_cf_medications Access to Affordable CF Medications access-to-affordable-cf-medications Urge Congress to protect and expand access to life-saving cystic fibrosis medications. access_to_medications active high us_senator us_representative protect_cf_insurance_coverage Protect Insurance Coverage for People with CF protect-insurance-coverage-for-people-with-cf Tell lawmakers to preserve strong insurance protections for people with cystic fibrosis. People with cystic fibrosis depend on comprehensive health coverage for medications, clinic visits, hospitalizations, and specialized therapies. Proposals that weaken protections for people with pre-existing conditions or allow skimpy plans can put individuals with CF at serious risk.

This campaign urges policymakers to preserve and strengthen insurance protections at both the federal and state levels, including bans on annual and lifetime limits and protections against coverage denials. insurance_coverage normal state_legislator cf_research_funding_2026 Increase Federal Funding for CF Research increase-federal-funding-for-cf-research-2026 Ask Congress to boost federal investment in CF research at NIH and other agencies. Life expectancy for people with cystic fibrosis has improved dramatically thanks to decades of research—but we are still far from a cure. Continued progress depends on sustained and increased funding for CF research at the National Institutes of Health and other federal agencies.

This campaign calls on Congress to prioritize CF research funding so that future generations can live longer, healthier lives. research_funding cf_awareness_month_2026 Support Cystic Fibrosis Awareness Month support-cf-awareness-month-2026 Encourage your local officials to recognize May as Cystic Fibrosis Awareness Month. Public awareness is critical to driving support for research, better policies, and inclusive communities for people with cystic fibrosis. By issuing proclamations or resolutions, local and state officials can shine a spotlight on CF and the challenges families face.

Use this tool to ask your mayor, governor, or other local officials to recognize May as Cystic Fibrosis Awareness Month. awareness archived mayor governor city_council adult_cf_nutrition_foundations_2024 Nutrition Foundations for Adults Living with Cystic Fibrosis nutrition-foundations-adults-cystic-fibrosis Key nutrition strategies to support lung health, weight, and energy levels in adults with CF. nutrition adults_with_cf adults pancreatic_enzymes high_calorie_diet published adult_cf_meal_planning_2023 Meal Planning Tips for Adults with CF on Busy Schedules meal-planning-adults-cf-busy-schedules Practical ways to fit high-calorie, high-protein meals into real life when you have CF. meal_planning time_management adult_cf_nutrition_label_reading How to Read Nutrition Labels When You Have CF reading-nutrition-labels-with-cf Learn which parts of the nutrition label matter most for people with cystic fibrosis. food_l

We are excited to announce an expansion of our teen group voice program at the East Branch Studio.

Beginning March 2025, we will:
- Add new Tuesday and Thursday evening group voice sections for ages 13–17.
- Keep class sizes small (6–8 students) for individual attention.
- Align curriculum with our recital and performance calendar.

pages.dataKeywords: he_1820_weaving_workshops Early Weaving Workshops Established in Bełchatów Small family-run weaving workshops mark the beginning of Bełchatów's industrial development. In the early 19th century, a number of family-run weaving workshops were established in Bełchatów. These enterprises laid the foundations for the town’s later transformation into a regional textile center. Archival records indicate that by 1820 several dozen looms operated in wooden houses around the market square. Bełchatów industry textiles economy 19th_century he_1853_textile_mill Opening of the First Larger Textile Mill in Bełchatów A small steam-powered textile mill begins operation, increasing production capacity in Bełchatów. In 1853 a small but modern textile mill was opened near the center of Bełchatów. Employing several dozen workers, the mill introduced more organized production methods and marked the town’s shift from cottage industry to factory-based textile production. Bełchatów textile district factory he_1881_steam_power Introduction of Steam Power in Local Factories Steam engines are installed in Bełchatów workshops, increasing output and altering working conditions. By 1881, several of Bełchatów’s larger workshops and mills had installed steam engines. Mechanization accelerated production and attracted a growing workforce from surrounding villages, reshaping the town’s social structure. Bełchatów industrial quarter steam_power labor he_1907_workers_coop Formation of the Bełchatów Workers’ Cooperative Textile workers establish a cooperative to improve wages and working conditions. In 1907, workers from several textile workshops in Bełchatów formed a cooperative to negotiate wages and mutual aid. The organization also created a small fund to support families during illness and strike actions. cooperative social_history he_1913_lignite_surveys Geological Surveys Identify Lignite Deposits near Bełchatów Engineers document rich lignite deposits, foreshadowing the region’s later mining complex. Geologists conducting surveys in 1913 confirmed extensive lignite deposits in the fields surrounding Bełchatów. Although large-scale extraction would not begin until after the Second World War, these findings shaped long-term plans for industrial development. Fields near Bełchatów mining lignite geology he_1924_electrification Municipal Electrification of Bełchatów Workshops Electric power reaches Bełchatów’s workshops, replacing many steam engines. By 1924, Bełchatów’s municipal authorities extended electric power lines to the town’s main workshops and industrial buildings. Electrification made production more efficient and attracted new entrepreneurs to the town. electricity modernization he_1929_crisis_industry Great Depression Reaches Bełchatów Textile Industry Falling orders and bankruptcies hit local factories during the global economic crisis. In 1929–1930, the global economic downturn sharply reduced demand for textiles. Several Bełchatów factories closed or laid off work

pages.dataKeywords: safety Safety Safety fundamentals, checklists, and precautions for DIY brain stimulation. side_effects Side effects Recognizing, preventing, and managing side effects such as skin irritation and headaches. protocols_step_by_step Protocols & step-by-step Detailed, step-by-step protocol examples for different targets and parameters. comparisons_overviews Comparisons & overviews Comparative articles that explain differences between tDCS, tACS, TMS, and related techniques. diy_build_guides DIY build guides Hardware-focused guides for building and testing DIY brain stimulation devices. basics_foundations Basics & foundations Beginner-friendly introductions to core DIY brain stimulation concepts and terminology. ethics Ethics & responsibility Ethical, legal, and societal questions related to DIY brain stimulation. research_updates Research summaries Plain-language summaries of recent peer-reviewed research on non-invasive brain stimulation. anodal_stimulation Anodal stimulation anodal-stimulation tDCS configuration where the anode (positively charged electrode) is placed over the target region, typically associated with increased excitability. In conventional tDCS, anodal stimulation is thought to depolarize neuronal membranes slightly, making neurons more likely to fire. For example, placing the anode over the motor cortex (M1) at 1–2 mA for 10–20 minutes can transiently enhance motor cortex excitability. Effects depend on montage, current density, session duration, and individual variability. Anodal stimulation does not always enhance performance and may interact with task difficulty and baseline performance. cathodal_stimulation current_density motor_cortex_m1 montage Cathodal stimulation cathodal-stimulation tDCS configuration where the cathode (negatively charged electrode) is placed over the target region, often associated with reduced excitability. Cathodal stimulation has historically been associated with slight hyperpolarization of neuronal membranes, making neurons less likely to fire. For example, cathodal tDCS over motor cortex at 1–2 mA can reduce corticomotor excitability. However, effects are not universally inhibitory and may reverse at higher currents or different montages. DIY users should be cautious about assuming simple 'on/off' effects and should prioritize safety over performance enhancement. Current density current-density Amount of electrical current per unit electrode area, often expressed in mA/cm². Current density is a critical safety parameter in tDCS and other direct current techniques. It is calculated by dividing the applied current (in mA) by the contact area of the electrode (in cm²). Higher current densities increase the risk of skin irritation or burns. Many human tDCS studies use current densities below about 0.05–0.08 mA/cm². DIY builders should compute current density for each montage and avoid exceeding values used in peer-reviewed research. current_intensity skin_irritation tdcs tDCS (transcranial direct current
