pages.dataKeywords: kyc_know_your_customer KYC (Know Your Customer) Know Your Customer (KYC) refers to the set of processes and controls that financial institutions and regulated entities use to verify the identity of their customers, assess their risk profiles, and comply with anti-money laundering (AML) and counter-terrorist financing (CTF) regulations. compliance customer_due_diligence_cdd enhanced_kyc kyc_remediation kyc_refresh_cycle aml_anti_money_laundering kyc-know-your-customer Enhanced KYC (EDD) Enhanced KYC, often referred to as Enhanced Due Diligence (EDD), is a deeper level of customer due diligence applied to higher-risk customers, products, or transactions, typically involving additional identity checks, source-of-funds verification, and ongoing monitoring. politically_exposed_person_pep enhanced-kyc-edd KYC remediation KYC remediation is the process of updating, correcting, or completing existing Know Your Customer records to ensure they meet current regulatory standards and internal policy requirements, often triggered by regulatory reviews or internal audits. transaction_monitoring kyc-remediation KYC refresh cycle The KYC refresh cycle is the defined schedule on which customer identity and risk information must be reviewed and updated, typically based on the risk rating of the customer (e.g., annually for high-risk, every 3–5 years for low-risk). kyc-refresh-cycle Anti-Money Laundering (AML) Anti-Money Laundering (AML) refers to laws, regulations, and procedures designed to prevent criminals from disguising illegally obtained funds as legitimate income, including requirements for customer due diligence, transaction monitoring, and suspicious activity reporting. beneficial_ownership anti-money-laundering-aml Beneficial ownership Beneficial ownership refers to the natural person or persons who ultimately own or control a customer or on whose behalf a transaction is being conducted, regardless of whose name the legal ownership is in. legal beneficial-ownership Customer Due Diligence (CDD) Customer Due Diligence (CDD) is the process of collecting and assessing information about a customer to verify their identity, understand the nature of the relationship, and evaluate the risk they may pose in terms of money laundering or terrorist financing. customer-due-diligence-cdd Politically Exposed Person (PEP) A Politically Exposed Person (PEP) is an individual who holds or has held a prominent public function, as well as their close associates and family members, and who may pose a higher risk of involvement in bribery or corruption. politically-exposed-person-pep open_banking Open banking Open banking is a regulatory and technology-driven approach that allows customers to securely share their financial data with pages.dataKeywords: dc_substation_frankfurt_110mw 110 MW Data Center Substation Design – Frankfurt, Germany 110mw-data-center-substation-frankfurt-germany data_centers hv_substation_design europe fifty_to_200_mw Turnkey high-voltage substation design and grid connection for a new hyperscale data center campus in Frankfurt. Our team delivered concept and detailed design for a 110 MW, 110/20 kV indoor GIS substation connecting a new hyperscale data center campus to the German transmission grid. Scope included load flow and short-circuit studies, protection & control schemes, grid code compliance, and coordination with the TSO and local DSO. The project met an aggressive schedule to align with staged data hall energization. Hyperscale data center operator dc_substation_dublin_80mw 80 MW Data Center Substation Expansion – Dublin, Ireland 80mw-data-center-substation-expansion-dublin-ireland Substation capacity upgrade to support expansion of an existing colocation data center. For a large colocation data center in Dublin, we engineered the expansion of an existing 60 MW substation to 80 MW, including transformer additions, busbar reconfiguration, and relay upgrades. The project was executed in live substation conditions with staged outages and detailed risk planning to maintain uptime commitments. Colocation data center provider dc_substation_ashburn_60mw 60 MW Data Center Greenfield Substation – Ashburn, USA 60mw-data-center-substation-ashburn-usa north_america Design of a radial-fed 230/34.5 kV substation for a greenfield data center campus in Virginia. We provided complete engineering design services for a 230/34.5 kV substation supplying a 60 MW IT load in Ashburn, Virginia. Deliverables included SLDs, protection coordination, SCADA integration, grounding design, and construction support. The design optimized reliability and future expandability while complying with local utility standards. Cloud services provider onshore_wind_grid_connection_150mw 150 MW Onshore Wind Farm Grid Connection Study – Spain 150mw-onshore-wind-grid-connection-study-spain renewables grid_connection_study Comprehensive grid connection study for a 150 MW wind project connecting to a 132 kV network. Our engineers performed power flow, short-circuit, and dynamic stability studies for a 150 MW onshore wind farm in Spain. The study assessed multiple grid connection points, optimized reactive power compensation, and verified grid code compliance under various contingency scenarios. Independent power producer (IPP) solar_rooftop_portfolio_25mw 25 MW Industrial Rooftop Solar Portfolio – Texas, USA 25mw-industrial-rooftop-solar-portfolio-texas-usa industrial solar_pv_engineering ten_to_50_mw Engineering design for a multi-site rooftop PV rollout across manufacturing facilities in Texas. pages.dataKeywords: gots GOTS Global Organic Textile Standard The leading worldwide standard for organic fibers, including ecological and social criteria, backed up by independent certification of the entire textile supply chain. Global Recycled Standard An international, voluntary, full product standard that sets requirements for third-party certification of recycled content, chain of custody, social and environmental practices, and chemical restrictions. bsci BSCI Business Social Compliance Initiative A leading supply chain management system that supports companies to drive social compliance and improvements within the factories and farms in their global supply chains. oeko_tex_standard_100 OEKO-TEX-100 OEKO-TEX Standard 100 A globally uniform, independent testing and certification system for raw, semi-finished, and finished textile products at all processing levels, as well as accessory materials. fair_wear FairWear Fair Wear Foundation An independent, non-profit organization that works with pages.dataKeywords: faq_parasailing_age_limits safety_and_requirements What are the age limits for parasailing? Our standard age limits for parasailing are 16–65 years old. All participants under 18 must have a parent or legal guardian sign the waiver on-site. Guests over 60 should be in good overall health and may be asked additional screening questions. For safety reasons, we are generally not able to accept participants younger than 16 or older than 65, but feel free to contact us if you have questions about specific situations. parasailing-age-limits parasailing faq_parasailing_weight_health Are there weight or health restrictions for parasailing? parasailing-weight-and-health-restrictions faq_reschedule_bookings booking_and_payments Can I reschedule or change my booking? reschedule-or-change-booking general faq_cancellation_policy policies What is your cancellation policy? cancellation-policy faq_payment_methods What payment methods do you accept? We accept all major credit and debit cards (Visa, Mastercard, American Express, Discover), as well as many mobile wallets such as Apple Pay and Google Pay for online bookings. You can also redeem our digital gift cards at checkout by entering your gift card code. Cash payments may be accepted on-site for walk-in bookings, but advance online reservations are strongly recommended to secure your preferred date and time. payment-methods gc_boat_rentals_only Boat Rentals Only Digital Gift Card boat_rentals_only This digital gift card can be redeemed for any of our boat rentals, including speedboats, pontoons, and private cruises. It cannot be used for parasailing tours or combo packages. active gc_all_activities All Activities Gift Card all_activities Our most flexible option. This gift card can be used toward any parasailing tour, boat rental, combo package, or add-on available on our website. gc_parasailing_only Parasailing Only Gift Card parasailing_only Perfect for thrill-seekers. This card is valid exclusively for parasailing tours and parasailing-related add-ons such as photo packages. gc_packages_only Packages & Combos Gift Card packages_only Give the gift of a full-day experience. This card can be redeemed for eligible combo packages, including parasailing + jet ski and other bundled adventures. avail_parasail_budget_morning_2026-07-18 exp_parasail_budget_morning America/New_York avail_parasail_standard_morning_2026-07-18 exp_parasail_standard_morning avail_parasail_budget_morning_2026-07-15 avail_parasail_weekday_morning_9am_2026-07-15 exp_parasail_weekday_morning_9am avail_parasail_afternoon_classic_2026-06-10 exp_parasail_afternoon_classic avail_parasail_afternoon_classic_2026-06-17 avail_parasail_hp_short_2026-08-10 exp_parasail_hp_short avail_parasail_hp_long_2026-08-10 exp_parasail_hp_long avail_speedboat_115hp_value_2026-08-02 exp_speedboat_115hp_value avail_speedboat_150hp_premium_2026-08-02 exp_speedboat_150hp_premium avail_pontoon_3hr_value_2026-07-18 exp_pontoon_3hr_value avail_pontoon_3hr_premium_2026-0

prepare-home-estate-sale-guide How to Prepare Your Home for an Estate Sale: Room-by-Room Checklist prepare-your-home-for-an-estate-sale Walk through every room with our step-by-step checklist to get your home ready for a successful estate sale, from decluttering and staging to safety and signage. prepare your home preparing home estate sale prep home staging checklist published preparing_home Get Help Preparing My Home prepare_home_consultation pre_sale_home_preparation_staging full-estate-liquidation-timeline Full Estate Liquidation Timeline: What to Expect Week by Week Understand the typical 4–6 week timeline for a full estate liquidation, from initial consultation and cataloging through sale days and post-sale cleanout. full estate liquidation selling process timeline planning selling_process Schedule a Full Estate Consultation schedule_consultation full_estate_liquidation downsizing-remote-owner-tips Downsizing When You Live Out of State: Managing an Estate from Afar downsizing-from-out-of-state If you’re managing a downsizing or estate sale from another state, learn how to structure the project, choose vendors, and minimize travel. downsizing remote owner out of state online estate sale Talk to Us About a Remote-Owner Project managed_online_estate_sale professional-partners-guide Estate Services for Real Estate Agents, Attorneys, and Fiduciaries estate-services-for-professionals See how estate liquidation services can support your clients, streamline transactions, and protect your brand when time and sensitivity matter most. Professionals who advise families—real estate agents, attorneys, fiduciaries, and financial planners—are often the first call when an estate situation arises.

This guide covers:
- Ways to simplify property transitions
- How to coordinate timelines with listing, closing, or probate milestones
- Options for co-branded marketing and white-label support

We offer a structured partner program with dedicated contacts, simple referral tracking, and custom marketing assets you can share with clients. for professionals real estate agents attorneys fiduciaries partner program for_professionals Download the Professional Partner Overview download_guide consultation_only estate-market-trends-2025 Estate Sale Market Trends: What Buyers Want This Year estate-sale-market-trends-2025 From mid-century furniture to high-quality tools, learn what’s in demand right now and how that can impact your estate sale strategy. Each year we analyze data from thousands of item listings and estate sales in our marketplace to understand what buyers are looking for.

In 2025, we’re seeing strong demand for:
- Quality mid-century and contemporary furniture
- Vintage audio equipment and vinyl
- Jewelry with clear provenance
- Well-maintained tools and outdoor equipment

Understanding demand trends helps us advise clients on pricing, timing, and whether to sell certain items separately or as part of an estate event. market trends pricing buyer demand estat

Alex Chen is a principal engineer at Nimbus Systems, where they lead platform teams focused on distributed systems, observability, and internal developer tooling. Alex frequently moderates and speaks on panels about technical decision-making, platform ownership, and building resilient engineering cultures. @alexchen_eng speaker_jordan_lee Jordan Lee Lead UX Researcher Brightline Studio UX researcher focused on making research approachable for cross-functional teams. Jordan Lee leads UX research at Brightline Studio. With a background in psychology and product strategy, Jordan has developed lightweight research frameworks that help teams of any size make evidence-based decisions. Jordan teaches beginner-friendly UX research workshops and mentors aspiring researchers. @jordanux speaker_taylor_morgan Taylor Morgan Product Manager Northwind Apps Product manager bridging design, data, and engineering teams. Taylor Morgan works as a product manager at Northwind Apps, focusing on collaboration between design, data, and engineering. Taylor has shipped features used by millions of users and is passionate about roadmapping, stakeholder communication, and outcome-focused product planning. @taylormorganpm speaker_samira_khan Samira Khan Director of AI Aurora Labs Director of AI building responsible machine learning products at scale. Samira Khan leads the AI organization at Aurora Labs, where her team delivers production ML systems for personalization, search, and recommendations. She regularly speaks about responsible AI practices, evaluation frameworks, and aligning AI investments with business outcomes. @samira_ai speaker_maria_garcia Maria Garcia Head of Product Design Flowline Design leader focused on systems thinking and inclusive design. pages.dataKeywords: ba_2024_video_funnels The 2024 Guide to Video Marketing Funnels 2024-guide-video-marketing-funnels In this comprehensive guide, we break down how to design full-funnel video marketing systems—from awareness to retention. We cover creative formats for each funnel stage, ideal video lengths, and how to map your content to measurable business outcomes. Topics include building a messaging hierarchy, planning retargeting sequences, and optimizing for different platforms such as Instagram, TikTok, YouTube, and LinkedIn. Design full-funnel video marketing systems that drive awareness, consideration, and conversions in 2024. This playbook outlines how to structure hooks, storytelling beats, and CTAs in under 30 seconds. We share real examples, scripting frameworks, and recommendations for testing creative variations across TikTok, Reels, and YouTube Shorts. A practical playbook for D2C marketers using TikTok, Reels, and Shorts to drive sales. Morgan Patel E-commerce short-form video ba_2023_video_roi How to Measure Video Marketing ROI Beyond Views how-to-measure-video-marketing-roi Views are a vanity metric. In this article we outline a practical framework for measuring video marketing ROI using metrics like engaged view rate, assisted conversions, and customer lifetime value. We also share how to set up tracking and experiments so your creative team and performance team speak the same language. Move beyond vanity metrics with a practical framework for video marketing ROI. Jordan Kim Analytics analytics ba_2025_vertical_trends Vertical Video Marketing Trends to Watch in 2025 vertical-video-marketing-trends-2025 Vertical video has become the default format on social platforms. We explore emerging trends for 2025, including shoppable live video, UGC-style ads, and platform-specific behaviors. Learn how to adapt your creative strategy and production workflow to stay ahead. Seven vertical video trends that should shape your 2025 content roadmap. Build a brand strategy that scales with your startup. pages.dataKeywords: director_maya_alvarez Maya Alvarez Maya Alvarez is a resident director known for contemporary reinterpretations of classic musicals. Since joining the company in 2014, she has directed multiple award-winning productions including several family-friendly musical revivals. director_james_okafor James Okafor James Okafor specializes in Shakespeare and poetic drama, with a focus on performances for student audiences. He regularly directs weekday matinee productions tailored for high school groups. director_lena_kapoor Lena Kapoor Lena Kapoor is an experimental theatre director whose work centers on new play development and intimate dramas. She collaborates closely with playwrights to bring new voices to the mainstage. director_samuel_choi Samuel Choi Samuel Choi directs large-scale musical productions featuring diverse casts and innovative choreography. Between 2015 and 2019 he helmed a series of hit musicals that remain audience favorites. director_claire_benson Claire Benson Claire Benson focuses on youth and family theatre, creating productions that are accessible, interactive, and engaging for audiences ages 8 and up. youth_education_fund Youth Education & Student Matinee Fund Supports youth education programs, student matinees, and in-school theatre workshops. The Youth Education & Student Matinee Fund underwrites subsidized tickets for school groups, transportation assistance for Title I schools, and teaching artist residencies that bring theatre into classrooms. Monthly gifts help sustain year-round education outreach and curriculum-aligned workshops. youth students education_outreach active general_operations_fund Annual Fund for General Operations Provides flexible support for productions, staffing, and day-to-day operations. Gifts to the Annual Fund sustain every aspect of the theatre’s work—from keeping the lights on and the stages running to supporting fair wages for artists and staff. Unrestricted support allows the company to respond quickly to emerging needs. general_operations new_work_development_fund New Work & Commissions Fund Invests in the development of new plays and musicals. The New Work & Commissions Fund supports playwright commissions, workshop productions, and staged readings. Your contribution helps nurture bold new voices and expand the canon of contemporary theatre. new_work_development accessibility_initiatives_fund Accessibility & Inclusion Fund Supports ASL-interpreted, captioned, and sensory-friendly performances. Gifts to the Accessibility & Inclusion Fund make the theatre more welcoming for everyone by underwriting ASL interpreters, open captioning, audio description, and sensory-friendly adaptations of select performances. accessibility community_engagement_fund Community & Family Engagement Fund Expands free and low-cost programs for families and community partners. This fund supports neighborhood partnerships, pay-what-you-can performances, and family-friendly events that invite new audiences into the theatre.

ba_sustainable_materials_urban_homes Choosing Sustainable Building Materials for Urban Homes sustainable-building-materials-urban-homes A practical guide to selecting sustainable materials for city infill projects, small lots, and multi-family renovations. Dana Schultz Sustainability Green Building Materials Urban Design ba_quick_guide_low_carbon_concrete A Quick Guide to Low-Carbon Concrete Mixes quick-guide-low-carbon-concrete-mixes Understand SCMs, optimized mix designs, and how to discuss low-carbon concrete with your structural engineer. Luis Moreno Structural Systems ba_healthy_interiors_materials Healthy Interiors: Material Choices That Actually Matter healthy-interiors-material-choices Not every eco-label is worth the premium. Here are the interior materials we prioritize for health and sustainability. Indoor air quality is shaped by a surprisingly short list of material decisions. In this article, we focus on paints, flooring, cabinetry, and sealants.

We explain VOC content vs. emissions, formaldehyde-free substrates, and how to interpret leading certifications.

Finally, we provide three prioritized upgrade paths—good, better, best—each with estimated cost impact per square foot. Priya Desai Healthy Homes Interiors ba_sustainable_materials_small_projects Sustainable Materials for Small Renovation Projects sustainable-materials-small-renovations Even on a modest kitchen or bath renovation, strategic material swaps can significantly cut environmental impact. Smaller projects often have tighter budgets, but they also offer concentrated opportunities for impact. By focusing on cabinets, countertops, fixtures, and lighting, you can meaningfully improve the sustainability of a renovation without blowing the budget.

We highlight durable, repairable materials, recycled-content products, and efficient fixtures that pay back quickly on energy and water bills. Renovation Kitchen ba_energy_codes_2024 What 2024 Energy Codes Mean for Your New Home 2024-energy-codes-new-home Stricter codes are arriving across the country. Here’s how they affect envelope design, HVAC sizing, and costs. As states adopt newer versions of the IECC and stretch codes, performance expectations rise for new single-family homes.

We summarize the most common changes, including higher insulation R-values, tighter air-sealing requirements, and mechanical ventilation mandates. The article includes example details and cost ranges for meeting vs. exceeding code in climate zones 3–6. Mark Chen Energy Codes New Construction ba_cost_drivers_kitchen_renovation Cost Drivers in a Kitchen Renovation: Where to Save and Where to Spend kitchen-renovation-cost-drivers Understand the five biggest cost drivers in kitchen renovations and how to keep a 120 sq ft project under budget. Cabinetry, appliances, countertops, and layout changes typically drive the majority of kitchen renovation costs.

We break down costs per square foot, show examples of 80, 120, and 180 sq ft kitchens, and provide s

Loading featured beginner programs... View all beginner classes Upcoming workshops & intensives Loading upcoming workshops... Next volunteer workday Checking for the next workday... Membership highlight Loading membership highlight... Learn more Join now About Minnesota NAVHDA Minnesota NAVHDA is a regional chapter of the North American Versatile Hunting Dog Association. We bring together handlers, trainers, and bird dogs of all backgrounds to develop reliable, ethical hunting companions using proven, test-based training methods. Through structured training programs, volunteer workdays, and formal testing opportunities, members gain access to training grounds, mentorship, and a community that understands both the hunting and dog-development sides of the work. Whether you are preparing for your first Natural Ability test or polishing an experienced dog for advanced work, you will find guidance and practice opportunities here. New to NAVHDA or versatile hunting breeds? Start by exploring beginner classes and workshops above, then consider a membership for regular access to grounds, events, and a network of experienced handlers. pages.dataKeywords: art_fall_prevention_home_safety_checklist Room-by-Room Fall Prevention Checklist for Home Safety room-by-room-fall-prevention-checklist-home-safety Walk through each area of the home with this checklist to reduce tripping hazards and prevent falls. home_safety fall prevention home safety checklist injury prevention Megan Holloway, RN art_fall_prevention_tips_seniors Fall Prevention Tips for Seniors at Home fall-prevention-tips-for-seniors-at-home Practical fall prevention tips for older adults living independently at home. for_seniors_at_home for seniors at home mobility safety Daniel Kim, PT art_fall_prevention_after_hospital Fall Prevention After a Hospital Stay: What Families Should Know fall-prevention-after-hospital-stay Recovery after hospitalization is a high-risk time for falls. Learn how to plan ahead. post-surgery recovery hospital discharge Lauren Patel, MSN art_home_safety_bathroom Making the Bathroom Safer for Aging in Place bathroom-safety-aging-in-place Simple upgrades can turn the bathroom from a high-risk space into a safer, more accessible room. Bathrooms are one of the most common locations for falls. This article explains grab bars, non-slip surfaces, and layout changes that lower risk and preserve dignity. bathroom safety aging in place grab bars art_memory_activities_dementia Engaging Memory Activities for Loved Ones with Dementia engaging-memory-activities-dementia Meaningful activities can reduce agitation and support quality of life for people living with dementia. Structured, familiar activities can help people with dementia feel calmer and more connected. Try music from their youth, sorting tasks, or simple art projects, and avoid overwhelming choices. dementia_care dementia care activities caregiver tips Carla Nguyen, OTR/L art_communicating_dementia How to Communicate with a Loved One Living with Dementia communicate-with-loved-one-dementia Learn simple communication techniques that reduce frustration for both you and your loved one. Use short sentences, yes/no questions, and a calm tone. Make eye contact, minimize background noise, and allow extra time for responses. Nonverbal cues like touch and facial expressions are often more important than words. communication family caregivers Elena Martinez, LCSW art_hiring_home_caregiver What to Ask When Hiring a Home Caregiver what-to-ask-when-hiring-a-home-caregiver Key questions to ask agencies and individual caregivers before inviting them into your home. Before hiring a caregiver, ask about training, background checks, supervision, backup coverage, and how care plans are updated. Clarify hourly rates, minimum shifts, and cancellation policies. caregiver_tips home care Michael Chen art_respite_care_benefits How Respite Care Helps Family Caregivers Recharge how-respite-care-helps-family-caregivers-recharge Respite care gives family caregivers time to rest, attend appointments, and prevent burnout. Short-term respite visits can be scheduled weekly or as needed. They provide

cybersecurity-basics-small-business Cybersecurity Basics for Small Businesses: 10 Quick Wins cybersecurity-basics-small-business-10-quick-wins Practical, low-cost cybersecurity steps that small businesses can take in under 30 days to reduce risk. cybersecurity small business security basics phishing Renee Carter smb-ransomware-defense-2026 SMB Ransomware Defense Checklist for 2026 smb-ransomware-defense-checklist-2026 A step-by-step ransomware defense checklist tailored for SMB environments with limited IT staff. Ransomware remains one of the most disruptive threats for SMBs. This checklist walks you through preparation, protection, detection, response, and recovery. We cover asset inventory, backup strategy, least-privilege access, endpoint protection, email filtering, user awareness training, tabletop exercises, and cyber insurance considerations.

Use this article alongside our downloadable checklist to validate your current posture and close the most critical gaps in your small business network. ransomware backup incident response Miguel Alvarez secure-remote-teams-small-business How Small Businesses Can Secure Remote Teams on a Budget secure-remote-teams-small-business-budget Guidance for small businesses securing remote and hybrid workers without enterprise-level budgets. remote work endpoint security policy Jordan Price cloud-migration-crm-files Planning a Cloud Migration for CRM and File Storage planning-cloud-migration-crm-file-storage Key steps, timelines, and risk considerations when migrating on‑prem CRM and file storage to the cloud. Migrating CRM and file storage to the cloud involves more than copying data. You must assess integration points, plan cutovers, map security requirements, and ensure adoption. We break the project into discovery, design, pilot, migration waves, and hypercare support, with a sample timeline for a 200–300 employee organization. cloud cloud migration file storage project planning Lena Hoffmann cloud-cost-optimization-2024 Cloud Cost Optimization: Avoiding Bill Shock cloud-cost-optimization-avoiding-bill-shock Techniques to control and forecast cloud spend as your workloads grow. Unplanned cloud spending can erode the financial benefits of migration. Learn how to tag resources, right-size workloads, set budgets and alerts, leverage reserved instances, and implement showback/chargeback for internal teams. cost optimization finops David Kim analytics-for-smb-forecasting Using Data Analytics to Improve SMB Sales Forecasting data-analytics-improve-smb-sales-forecasting How small and mid-sized businesses can use modern analytics tools to create more accurate sales forecasts. pages.dataKeywords: consulting Consulting Track CONS For students interested in management, strategy, and advisory consulting careers. The Consulting Track is designed for applicants who are interested in careers in management consulting, strategy, and advisory services. Applicants will complete a case-focused application, attend consulting-specific workshops, and interview with brothers who have consulting experience. The track emphasizes problem solving, client communication, and structured thinking. finance_banking Finance & Banking Track Focus on corporate finance, investment banking, and capital markets roles. The Finance & Banking Track prepares candidates for roles in corporate finance, investment banking, private equity, and related fields. Programming includes technical finance workshops, interview prep, and mentorship from brothers with finance experience. marketing_strategy Marketing & Strategy Track For students pursuing marketing, brand management, and growth strategy. The Marketing & Strategy Track is intended for students interested in brand management, growth marketing, product marketing, and related areas. Applicants will participate in brand case workshops, networking events, and resume reviews tailored to marketing roles. general_leadership General Business Leadership Track Broad track for applicants exploring multiple business paths. The General Business Leadership Track is ideal for applicants who are still exploring various areas of business or who are interested in interdisciplinary roles. It combines elements of professional development, leadership training, and networking across industries. professional_development Professional Development Plans resume workshops, mock interviews, and firm networking nights for brothers and potential new members. professional profdev@epsilonbusiness.org social Social Organizes mixers, retreats, and brotherhood bonding events throughout the semester. social@epsilonbusiness.org philanthropy Philanthropy Leads service initiatives and fundraising events in partnership with local nonprofits. philanthropy@epsilonbusiness.org recruitment Recruitment Coordinates recruitment marketing, information sessions, and bid day logistics. operations recruitment@epsilonbusiness.org Operations Manages chapter logistics, space reservations, and internal processes. operations@epsilonbusiness.org diversity_inclusion Diversity & Inclusion Hosts dialogues, workshops, and partnerships focused on equity and inclusion in business. other inclusion@epsilonbusiness.org faq_app_req_1 application_requirements What GPA is required to apply? We strongly recommend a minimum cumulative GPA of 3.0 to be a competitive applicant. The average GPA of admitted members is typically around 3.4–3.6, but we review applications holistically and consider leadership, involvement, and professional interest in addition to GPA. faq_app_req_2 Do I need to be a business major to apply? Use this step-by-step inspection checklist to know what your inspector will look at and what you should pay attention to before closing. As a first-time homebuyer, the inspection can feel overwhelming. This detailed checklist walks you through the exterior, roof, foundation, electrical, plumbing, HVAC, interior finishes, and safety items your inspector will evaluate. For each category, we explain what is considered normal wear versus a potential red flag that may require repair, negotiation, or further specialist evaluation... first-time homebuyer checklist home inspection basics buyers first_time_homebuyer_checklist_2025_06 First-Time Homebuyer Pre-Inspection Checklist: Be Ready Before Your Inspector Arrives first-time-homebuyer-pre-inspection-checklist blog/first-time-homebuyer-pre-inspection-checklist.html Prepare your home inspection like a pro with this first-time homebuyer checklist covering documents, utilities, and on-site questions to ask. Preparation is key to getting the most value from your home inspection. This checklist covers what to bring (notebook, tape measure, inspection contingency), how to confirm utilities are on, what questions to ask about major systems, and how to organize photos and notes for your records. We also include a printable PDF you can take to the inspection... pre-inspection radon_testing_guide_2024 Radon Testing 101: What Homebuyers in High-Risk Areas Need to Know radon-testing-101-homebuyers blog/radon-testing-101-homebuyers.html Radon is a colorless, odorless gas that can affect any home. Learn when radon testing is recommended, how it works, and what your results mean. Radon is the second leading cause of lung cancer after smoking, and many regions of the country are considered high risk. pages.dataKeywords: cs_auto_2019_brake_optimization Brake Module CNC Consolidation Cuts Line Changeovers automotive Tier-1 automotive supplier consolidated three brake module variants into a common CNC platform, improving OEE and achieving an 18% cost reduction on low-volume SKUs. A Tier-1 automotive supplier struggled with low utilization across three separate brake module machining lines. By redesigning fixtures and consolidating the product family into a single flexible CNC cell, they reduced changeover time from 90 minutes to 25 minutes. The project delivered an 18% piece-price cost reduction on low-volume SKUs, a 12% improvement in on-time delivery, and freed up 240 m² of floor space. brake-module-cnc-consolidation-automotive cs_auto_2021_line_consolidation Automotive Cross-Member Line Consolidation Delivers 21% Cost Reduction European OEM consolidated three stamped cross-member designs into a single modular platform and achieved a 21% cost reduction while maintaining crash performance. An automotive OEM needed to rationalize regional variants of a front subframe cross-member. Our engineering team standardized hole patterns, introduced modular brackets, and transferred manufacturing to a flexible robotic welding and machining cell. Finite element validation confirmed equivalent crash performance. The program achieved a verified 21% cost reduction, 15% weight reduction, and reduced part numbers from 9 to 3 across global platforms. automotive-cross-member-line-consolidation-21-percent cs_auto_2022_powertrain_localization Localized EV Powertrain Housing Machining Saves 24% Total Landed Cost North American EV start-up localized machining of aluminum powertrain housings and realized a 24% total landed cost reduction versus offshore sourcing. A fast-growing EV start-up imported machined powertrain housings from overseas, facing long lead times and volatile freight costs. By localizing aluminum 6061 housing machining to our automated cell network, they cut average lead time from 10 weeks to 3 weeks. Optimized toolpaths, high-pressure coolant, and in-line probing reduced scrap by 60%. The project delivered a 24% total landed cost reduction and enabled weekly design revisions during ramp-up. localized-ev-powertrain-housing-machining-24-percent-savings cs_auto_2023_ev_casting High-Pressure Die Casting for EV Motor Brackets Cuts Weight by 19% Conversion from machined steel to high-pressure die cast aluminum motor brackets reduced component weight by 19% and improved assembly ergonomics. An EV systems supplier wanted to reduce the mass of an electric motor mounting bracket. We converted the design from machined steel plate to high-pressure die cast aluminum with localized reinforcement. Process simulation eliminated porosity in critical clamp areas. While the primary KPI was weight (19% reduction), the new process also reduced machining time by 35% and improved assembly ergonomics on the final vehicle line. hpdc-ev-motor-brackets-weight-reduction cs_auto_2024_lightweight_s

article_piano_beginner_1 5 Short Daily Piano Routines for Absolute Beginners piano beginner practice_tips practice_routines beginners daily_habits Build a 10–15 minute daily piano habit with five simple beginner-friendly practice routines. article_piano_beginner_2 How to Practice Scales on Piano Without Getting Bored scales technique Turn beginner piano scales into a focused, game-like practice that actually feels rewarding. article_piano_beginner_3 Left Hand Made Easy: First Chord Patterns for Beginner Piano left_hand chords Strengthen your beginner piano left hand with three simple chord patterns you can use in real songs. article_piano_beginner_4 3 Common Beginner Piano Mistakes (and Easy Fixes) posture Identify and fix three tiny beginner habits that quietly hold your piano playing back. article_guitar_beginner_1 Your First 7 Days on Guitar: A Simple Practice Roadmap If you’ve just picked up a guitar, the first week sets the tone for your progress.

This roadmap gives you a day-by-day plan covering tuning, holding the guitar comfortably, your first two chords, and a basic strumming pattern. Each day includes a 10-minute routine and a quick self-check so you know when you’re ready to move on.

By day seven, you’ll be able to switch between two chords in time with a metronome and strum along to a simple song at a slow tempo. guitar roadmap A clear, day-by-day plan for your very first week of guitar practice. article_voice_pop_1 Pop Vocal Warm-Ups You Can Do in 5 Minutes Short, consistent vocal warm-ups keep your voice healthy and flexible.

We’ll cover three quick pop-focused warm-up sequences: lip trills for breath flow, gentle sirens to connect chest and head voice, and a simple 1–3–5–8 scale pattern on neutral syllables. Each warm-up includes recommended starting notes for different voice types.

Use these before practice, rehearsal, or gigs to feel more supported and confident when you sing. voice all_levels warmups A 5-minute warm-up routine to keep your pop singing voice flexible and healthy. article_news_1 New Online Lesson Studio Setup for 2026 The studio has a new online lesson setup for 2026, including upgraded cameras, studio lighting, and high-quality audio interfaces.

pages.dataKeywords: garden_chapel Garden Chapel garden-chapel outdoor_garden Romantic outdoor garden chapel with vine-covered arch and bistro lighting. Our Garden Chapel is an outdoor ceremony space nestled among mature trees, string lights, and seasonal blooms. A whitewashed arbor frames your vows, with built-in audio for your officiant and music. Ideal for sunset ceremonies and larger guest counts, the Garden Chapel includes white ceremony chairs, a dedicated aisle, and nearby access to indoor restrooms and a covered rain backup aisle. 101 Garden Walk, Riverbend, ST 12345 rose_garden_courtyard Rose Garden Courtyard rose-garden-courtyard Brick courtyard surrounded by rose beds and ivy walls. The Rose Garden Courtyard offers an intimate open-air ceremony setting enclosed by ivy-covered brick walls and manicured rose beds. A central fountain creates a soft ambient soundtrack as guests are seated under market lights. This location is perfect for medium to large garden ceremonies with a romantic European feel. 105 Garden Walk, Riverbend, ST 12345 indoor_chapel_classic Classic Indoor Chapel classic-indoor-chapel indoor_chapel_only Traditional white chapel with pew seating, stained glass, and climate control. Our Classic Indoor Chapel features wooden pews, soft uplighting, and stained glass accents for a timeless ceremony backdrop. The space is fully climate controlled and includes an integrated sound system, discrete photo vantage points, and a center aisle perfect for elopements, intimate ceremonies, and traditional weddings alike. 201 Chapel Lane, Riverbend, ST 12345 grand_cathedral_chapel Grand Cathedral Chapel grand-cathedral-chapel indoor_chapel Large vaulted chapel with balcony seating and grand center aisle. The Grand Cathedral Chapel is our largest indoor space, accommodating up to 200 guests beneath soaring ceilings and exposed beams. Floor-to-ceiling windows bring in natural light, while candlelit sconces and a raised altar create an unforgettable setting for formal ceremonies and large celebrations. 205 Chapel Lane, Riverbend, ST 12345 riverfront_gazebo Riverfront Gazebo riverfront-gazebo Intimate gazebo overlooking the river, perfect for small ceremonies and elopements. Exchange vows under a white gazebo with sweeping river views. The Riverfront Gazebo is ideal for elopements and micro-weddings, offering a tranquil setting a short walk from the main chapel. Simple seating, lantern accents, and flexible weekday availability make this a popular choice for smaller celebrations. 401 Riverfront Way, Riverbend, ST 12345 terrace_overlook Terrace Overlook terrace-overlook mixed Covered terrace with garden overlook and flexible indoor backup. The Terrace Overlook combines the best of indoor comfort and outdoor ambiance. Couples say 'I do' on a covered terrace framed by potted trees and skyline views, with a connected indoor lounge for weather backup. Ideal for mid-size weddings seeking a modern twist on a garden ceremony. 501 Terrace View, Riverbend, ST 12345 library

pages.dataKeywords: cs_ai_triage_stadtklinik_berlin AI-Assisted Triage Improved Emergency Throughput at StadtKlinik Berlin ai-assisted-triage-stadtklinik-berlin StadtKlinik Berlin healthcare StadtKlinik Berlin used an AI-assisted triage dashboard to prioritize emergency room patients and cut waiting times while increasing billable services. StadtKlinik Berlin partnered with our team to design and implement an AI-assisted triage workflow. We conducted in-context research with nurses and doctors, redesigned the intake screen, and deployed a prioritization model that surfaces high-risk cases. Within six months, the hospital saw shorter wait times, higher staff satisfaction, and a measurable uplift in revenue from better-coded procedures. 31.8% revenue uplift 22% reduction in average ER wait time 18% increase in accurate triage coding Berlin, Germany cs_telehealth_conversion_northriver Telehealth Conversion Optimization for NorthRiver Health telehealth-conversion-optimization-northriver-health NorthRiver Health NorthRiver Health streamlined its telehealth booking flow and increased completed appointments while reducing call-center load. NorthRiver Health engaged us to understand why patients were dropping off before completing telehealth bookings. Through remote usability testing and funnel analysis, we simplified the appointment flow, clarified insurance messaging, and introduced proactive support nudges. The redesigned experience drove more completed bookings and higher revenue per digital visit. 22.5% revenue uplift 29% increase in completed telehealth bookings 17% reduction in call-center volume Boston, USA cs_patient_portal_adoption_coastal Driving Patient Portal Adoption Across a Regional Health Network patient-portal-adoption-coastal-health Coastal Regional Health Network A patient-centric redesign of the portal experience brought more users online and reduced administrative overhead. For Coastal Regional Health Network, we mapped the end-to-end patient journey, with a focus on registration and lab results. By simplifying sign-up, personalizing reminders, and improving mobile accessibility, more patients began using digital channels for routine tasks, reducing pressure on front-desk teams. 18% revenue uplift 42% increase in portal sign-ups 25% reduction in in-person admin visits Portland, USA cs_hospital_supply_chain_metrocare Hospital Supply Chain Optimization for MetroCare hospital-supply-chain-optimization-metrocare MetroCare Hospitals MetroCare used predictive insights to balance inventory levels and reduce costly last-minute orders. MetroCare Hospitals struggled with frequent stockouts of critical supplies and overstock of low-use items. We integrated purchasing data with clinical utilization patterns and built dashboards for procurement teams. The result was a more predictable supply chain and fewer expensive emergency orders. 24.3% revenue uplift 31% reduction in emergency supply orders 15% decrease in inventory carrying costs Chicago, USA cs_digital_onboa

Youth athletes are at increased risk for heat illness during summer practices. This guide covers how to schedule sessions during cooler parts of the day, ensure adequate hydration, recognize early signs of heat exhaustion, and modify practices when humidity is high. It also includes a sample heat policy that families and coaches can review together. heat_safety summer hydration art_soccer_parent_guide Parent Guide to Youth Soccer Seasons parent-guide-youth-soccer-seasons An overview of what to expect in fall, winter, spring, and summer soccer. This guide explains the typical commitment, costs, and equipment needed for each season, plus questions to ask before registering your child. parent_resources soccer registration seasons art_choosing_program_on_budget Choosing Affordable Youth Sports Programs choosing-affordable-youth-sports-programs How to find quality programs that fit your family budget. pages.dataKeywords: article_chemo_side_effects_basics_2024 Chemotherapy Side Effects: What Adult Patients Can Expect chemotherapy-side-effects-what-adults-can-expect An overview of common chemotherapy side effects in adults, why they happen, and when to call your care team. Chemotherapy affects rapidly dividing cells throughout the body. This article explains common side effects such as fatigue, nausea, hair loss, and low blood counts, and offers practical tips for monitoring symptoms and staying in touch with your oncology team... all_cancers adult_patients article chemotherapy chemotherapy side effects fatigue nausea hair loss resources.html?articleId=chemotherapy-side-effects-what-adults-can-expect article_managing_chemo_side_effects_home_2023 Managing Chemotherapy Side Effects at Home: A Guide for Adults managing-chemotherapy-side-effects-at-home-adults Step-by-step strategies for adult patients to manage chemo side effects between clinic visits. Many chemotherapy side effects can be safely managed at home with the right tools and support. In this guide, we walk through setting up a symptom log, building a nausea management toolkit, staying hydrated, recognizing red-flag symptoms, and partnering with caregivers... guide self-care symptom management adult patients resources.html?articleId=managing-chemotherapy-side-effects-at-home-adults article_chemo_gi_side_effects_2022 Coping With Nausea, Vomiting, and Appetite Loss During Chemotherapy coping-with-nausea-and-appetite-loss-during-chemotherapy Practical tips for adult patients dealing with gastrointestinal side effects of chemotherapy. Gastrointestinal side effects like nausea, vomiting, and appetite loss can make it harder to complete treatment. This article reviews medication options, eating strategies, hydration tips, and when to seek urgent care... vomiting appetite loss nutrition resources.html?articleId=coping-with-nausea-and-appetite-loss-during-chemotherapy article_chemo_long_term_effects_2022 Understanding the Long-Term Side Effects of Chemotherapy in Adults long-term-side-effects-of-chemotherapy-in-adults Information on possible late and long-term effects of chemotherapy for adult survivors. While many chemotherapy side effects resolve after treatment ends, some can last months or years. This overview covers neuropathy, fertility changes, heart and lung effects, cognitive changes, and strategies for follow-up care... long-term effects survivorship neuropathy resources.html?articleId=long-term-side-effects-of-chemotherapy-in-adults article_chemo_fatigue_2023 Beating Cancer-Related Fatigue During Chemotherapy beating-cancer-related-fatigue-during-chemotherapy How adult patients can understand and manage fatigue throughout chemotherapy. Fatigue is one of the most common and distressing side effects of chemotherapy. pages.dataKeywords: art_startup_funding_paths How to Choose the Right Startup Funding Path startup-funding-paths An overview of bootstrapping, grants, angels, and venture capital for first-time founders. Choosing how to fund your startup is one of the earliest strategic decisions you’ll make. This guide breaks down the trade-offs between bootstrapping, equity-free grants, angel investment, and venture capital. We’ll look at how much capital each path typically offers, what expectations come with the money, and how to align your funding strategy with your long-term goals. Funding startup funding bootstrapping grants investment Dana Kim published art_equity_free_vs_equity_investment Equity-Free Grants vs Equity Investment: A Practical Guide equity-free-grants-vs-equity-investment Compare equity-free grants and equity investment programmes so you can pick the right funding track. Not all funding is created equal. Equity-free grants give you capital without dilution but are often milestone-driven and competitive. Equity investment programmes exchange capital and support for a stake in your company. In this guide, we break down typical cheque sizes, equity percentages, control implications, and how to assess the true cost of capital. equity-free grant equity investment funding tracks Jordan Lee art_mvp_validation_checklist MVP Validation Checklist: Test Before You Build mvp-validation-checklist A step-by-step checklist to validate your startup idea with an MVP before investing heavily in product development. Most startups fail because they build something nobody wants. Use this checklist to design a minimum viable product that actually tests demand. You’ll learn how to define your riskiest assumptions, select lean experiments, recruit test users, and measure traction using simple metrics like activation, retention, and willingness to pay. Validation mvp validation lean startup customer discovery Priya Desai art_customer_interviews_validation Customer Interviews that Actually Validate Demand customer-interviews-validate-demand Learn how to run customer interviews that surface real problems and buying intent instead of polite feedback. Good customer interviews focus on past behavior, not future promises. This article walks through how to recruit the right participants, craft non-leading questions, and analyze interview notes for patterns. We include scripts you can copy, plus red flags that indicate you’re collecting compliments instead of validation. customer interviews art_legal_basics_for_founders Legal Basics for Founders: Incorporation, IP, and Contracts legal-basics-for-founders A plain-language guide to the key legal decisions every founder should make in the first year. Legal work doesn’t have to be intimidating or expensive. In this guide, we cover when and where to incorporate, how to split equity among co-founders, the basics of intellectual property protection, and the essential contracts you should have in place with employees, contractors, and early customers

Pop Popular music with a focus on strong melodies, hooks, and accessible song structures across a wide range of substyles. jazz Jazz Improvisation-driven music rooted in swing, blues, and complex harmonies, ranging from classic standards to modern fusion. nova_echo Nova Echo Nova Echo is a synth-driven electronic project known for cinematic concept albums that blend spacious ambient passages with pulse-heavy rhythms. Since the late 2000s, Nova Echo has released a string of narrative-focused studio albums exploring science fiction themes and deep-space travel. Reykjavík, Iceland 2008–present solar_lines Solar Lines Solar Lines creates warm, melodic electronic music that sits between ambient and downtempo. Layered analog synths, gentle guitar lines, and field recordings give each album a sense of traveling through sunlit cityscapes and distant coastlines. Barcelona, Spain 2012–present midnight_parade Midnight Parade Midnight Parade is an indie rock band mixing driving guitars with shimmering synth textures. Their albums often revolve around nocturnal city life, blending danceable rhythms with introspective lyrics. Chicago, USA 2010–present crystal_harbor Crystal Harbor Crystal Harbor is an electronic duo known for lush, vocal-heavy productions that blend dream pop sensibilities with club-ready beats. Their work often features collaborations with vocalists and producers from across the globe. Sydney, Australia 2013–present harbor_lights Harbor Lights Harbor Lights is a jazz ensemble celebrated for intimate live recordings in small clubs and waterfront venues. Their performances focus on atmospheric standards, late-night improvisations, and stripped-back arrangements. New Orleans, USA 2001–present silver_atlas Silver Atlas Silver Atlas is a long-running indie rock project that has evolved from noisy 90s guitar anthems to layered, atmospheric records in the 2010s. The band is known for detailed discography spanning three decades. Manchester, UK 1992–present luna_vale Luna Vale Luna Vale is a vocalist and songwriter whose airy vocals bridge electronic, pop, and R&B. Alongside solo work, Luna is widely recognized for collaborations and duet projects with producers and bands across the electronic and pop spectrum. pages.dataKeywords: home_hospice_care Home Hospice Care hospice_care home Comprehensive hospice care delivered in the comfort of the patient’s home. Our Home Hospice Care program brings a multidisciplinary team directly to the patient’s home, focusing on comfort, dignity, and support for both patient and family. The team typically includes nurses, aides, social workers, chaplains, and volunteers who coordinate closely with the patient’s physician. Care plans are individualized and reviewed regularly to respond quickly to changing needs. 2–3 visits per week home_based family_support Regular nurse visits for assessment and symptom management 24/7 on-call nurse phone support Hospice aide visits for personal care Social work support for emotional and practical needs Spiritual care visits on request Home medical equipment and supplies related to the hospice diagnosis Medication coordination and delivery Care planning meetings with family service_detail.html?programId=home_hospice_care Hospice Care home_and_inpatient Full-spectrum hospice services available at home and in our inpatient hospice units. Our Hospice Care program combines home-based services with access to inpatient hospice units when symptoms become difficult to manage at home. The interdisciplinary team partners with patients and families to honor goals and preferences, manage complex symptoms, and provide emotional, spiritual, and practical support. Flexible levels of care allow transitions between home and inpatient settings as needs change. 3+ visits per week more_support_at_home advanced_symptom_management Frequent nurse visits for complex symptom management 24/7 nurse triage and urgent visit coordination Hospice aide support several times per week Access to inpatient hospice units for crisis or respite care Medical director oversight and coordination with primary physicians Social work, spiritual care, and volunteer support Medication management and delivery Durable medical equipment for home use service_detail.html?programId=hospice_care palliative_care_at_home Palliative Care at Home palliative_care Home-based palliative care for serious illness alongside ongoing treatments. The Palliative Care at Home program supports patients living with serious illness who may still be receiving curative or life-prolonging treatments. Our team focuses on relief of pain and distressing symptoms, advance care planning, and helping patients and families clarify goals of care. Visits are typically less frequent than hospice but still provide regular touchpoints and 24/7 access to nurse phone support for urgent questions. 2 visits per week symptom_management goal_setting_support Scheduled home visits from palliative care nurses 24/7 nurse phone support for urgent concerns Symptom assessment and medication recommendations Support with complex medical decision-making Social worker support for coping and resource navigation Advance care planning and documentation support Care coordination with specialists and primary care

pages.dataKeywords: downtown_schenectady BrightSteps Learning Center - Downtown 145 State St Schenectady 518-555-0123 America/New_York uptown_schenectady BrightSteps Learning Center - Uptown 780 Union St Suite 200 518-555-0456 clifton_park BrightSteps Learning Center - Clifton Park 22 Parkside Ave Clifton Park 518-555-0789 holiday_schedule_overview What is the center's holiday schedule? Our centers follow a consistent holiday schedule each year, with closures on major federal holidays and a short winter break in late December. A printable holiday schedule is posted on the Parent Resources and FAQs pages by September for the upcoming calendar year. Please note that exact dates, especially in December, may vary slightly year to year, so always refer to the current year calendar or contact the front desk for the most up-to-date schedule. holidays holiday schedule closure calendar december_holiday_closures Which days is the center closed for December holidays? Each December we close for a combination of winter holidays and staff training. Typically, the center is closed for at least Christmas Day and either the preceding or following weekday, as well as one to two additional days for winter break. Because specific closure dates change slightly each year, please check the current Holiday Calendar under Parent Resources or contact us directly to receive the full list of December closure dates for this year. december winter_break holiday_tuition_adjustments Are tuition payments adjusted for holiday closures? Tuition is billed as a flat monthly rate that already accounts for scheduled holiday closures and staff training days. For this reason, we do not prorate tuition for holidays listed on our annual holiday schedule. In the rare case of an unscheduled closure (such as severe weather), credits or make-up options may be considered on a case-by-case basis. tuition billing center_hours What are your regular hours of operation? Our standard hours of operation are Monday through Friday from 7:00 AM to 6:30 PM at most locations. Some programs, including part-day preschool and certain after-school options, may have different start or end times. Please refer to the specific program description or contact your center for exact hours. hours full_day extended_hours age_groups_served What age groups do you serve? We currently serve children from 6 weeks through 12 years of age. Our core programs include Infant (6 weeks–18 months), Toddler (18–36 months), Preschool (3–4 years), Pre-K (4–5 years), and After-School (Kindergarten–5th grade). Availability may vary by center location. curriculum age_groups infant toddler preschool after_school student_teacher_ratios What are your typical student-to-teacher ratios? Our ratios are designed to meet or exceed New York State licensing standards. Typical maximum ratios are 4:1 for Infants, 5:1 for Young Toddlers, 7:1 for Older Toddlers, 10:1 for Preschool, and 12:1 for School-Age programs. Specific classroom ratios are listed on each classroom de

pages.dataKeywords: birth_pref_epidural Epidural Pain relief through an epidural placed in the lower back, typically available in most hospital settings. pain_management birth_pref_breathing_techniques Breathing techniques Use patterned breathing, visualization, and relaxation exercises to cope with contractions. birth_pref_nitrous_oxide Nitrous oxide Inhaled nitrous oxide gas used during contractions for short-term pain relief. birth_pref_iv_pain_meds IV pain medication Systemic pain medicines given through an IV, often used before or instead of an epidural. birth_pref_unmedicated Unmedicated birth Plan to labor and give birth without medical pain medications whenever possible. birth_pref_water_immersion Water immersion Use a tub or shower during labor for comfort and natural pain relief. birth_pref_tens_unit TENS unit Use a transcutaneous electrical nerve stimulation (TENS) device for back labor and contractions. birth_pref_movement_positions Freedom to move and change positions Walk, use a birth ball, and choose comfortable labor positions whenever medically safe. birth_pref_dim_lighting Dim lighting Keep lights low or use natural light to create a calm environment. labor_environment birth_pref_music Music Play a personal playlist or calming music during labor and birth. birth_pref_partner_present Partner present Have a partner or chosen support person present throughout labor and delivery when allowed. birth_pref_doula_support Doula support Continuous support from a trained birth doula during labor. birth_pref_minimal_staff Minimal interruptions from staff Request that only essential staff enter the room to maintain privacy and calm. birth_pref_immediate_skin_to_skin Immediate skin-to-skin contact Place baby directly on the birthing parent’s chest right after birth, if medically safe. postpartum_care birth_pref_delayed_bath Delay baby’s first bath Postpone baby’s first bath for at least 24 hours unless there is a medical reason to bathe sooner. birth_pref_rooming_in Rooming-in with baby Keep baby in the same room as the birthing parent instead of using the nursery, when possible. birth_pref_breastfeeding_support Lactation or breastfeeding support Request a lactation consultant or nurse with breastfeeding training to assist after birth. birth_pref_delayed_cord_clamping Delayed cord clamping Wait at least 30–60 seconds before clamping and cutting the umbilical cord, if baby is stable. newborn_care birth_pref_vitamin_k_injection Vitamin K injection Administer the standard vitamin K injection to baby shortly after birth to prevent bleeding disorders. birth_pref_pacifier_on_request Pacifier only on request Avoid giving baby a pacifier unless the parent specifically requests it. forum_cat_first_trimester First Trimester first_trimester Discussions about early pregnancy symptoms, appointments, and emotional changes in weeks 1–13. forum_cat_second_trimester Second Trimester second_trimester Talk about the middle months of pregnancy, anatomy scans, and feeling baby move.

pages.dataKeywords: bookings_cancellations Bookings & cancellations bookings-cancellations Questions about reservations, changes, cancellations, refunds, and waitlists for workshops and retreats. payments_pricing Payments & pricing payments-pricing Information about payment methods, discounts, vouchers, and currency. workshop_experience Workshops & festival experience workshops-festival-experience Practical details about what to bring, dress code, photography, and class etiquette. accessibility_support Accessibility & support accessibility-support Accessibility features at venues, support for youth participants, and special accommodation requests. instructor_akiko_sato Akiko Sato Akiko Sato is a Tokyo-based butoh artist whose work explores the tension between stillness and eruption. After training in contemporary dance and Noh theater, she turned to butoh as a way to investigate memory, grief, and transformation. Akiko has performed across Japan and Europe and is known for her precise, spacious teaching style that supports both beginners and experienced movers. Contemporary butoh with Noh influences Slow storms, quiet bodies, unexpected memories. instructor_ren_takahashi Ren Takahashi Ren Takahashi is a senior butoh practitioner who began dancing in the underground scene of Osaka in the late 1990s. His workshops emphasize the grotesque and the tender living side by side, drawing on ankoku butoh traditions and improvisation scores. Ren mentors younger artists and regularly leads intensives focused on long-form performance and ensemble work. Ankoku butoh, improvisation-based Where shadows learn to breathe. instructor_mika_kobayashi Mika Kobayashi Mika works at the intersection of butoh, somatics, and trauma-informed practice. She facilitates gentle entry points for newcomers and youth, with a focus on consent, choice, and curiosity. Mika leads community projects in Tokyo and Yokohama, often collaborating with live musicians and visual artists. Somatic butoh, trauma-informed Listening to the body beneath the body. instructor_daichi_mori Daichi Mori With a background in physical theater and martial arts, Daichi approaches butoh as a laboratory for weight, gravity, and resistance. His intermediate and advanced workshops often include partner work, floorwork, and stamina training to support larger-scale performance projects. Physical theater butoh fusion Falling, flying, and everything in between. instructor_hana_yamamoto Hana Yamamoto Hana is a choreographer and educator focused on inclusive dance spaces. She designs workshops that are accessible to different bodies, ages, and movement histories, with an emphasis on imagery, voice, and group scores. Hana also facilitates youth labs and parent–teen classes. Inclusive butoh practice Everyone has a dance, even the shy parts. instructor_kazuo_nishida Kazuo Nishida Kazuo is a visiting instructor from Berlin whose work with soundscapes and minimal gesture invites participants into deep states of attention. He often collaborate

pages.dataKeywords:
bp_vr_ar_2024_1 Immersive Showrooms: Using VR/AR in Residential Marketing immersive-showrooms-vr-ar-residential-marketing How VR and AR showrooms help developers pre-sell residential units long before construction is complete. VR/AR Residential Marketing Laura Chen bp_vr_ar_2024_2 Real-Time AR for On-Site Design Reviews real-time-ar-for-on-site-design-reviews See how architects overlay 3D concept models onto the actual site using AR to speed up decisions. Workflow On-Site Review David Morales bp_vr_ar_2024_3 Choosing Between VR Tours and Cinematic Animations choosing-between-vr-tours-and-cinematic-animations VR tours and camera animations solve different problems—here’s how to decide which format fits your project. Animation Client Communication bp_vr_ar_2023_1 Five Ways Developers Use VR Tours to Pre-Lease Offices five-ways-developers-use-vr-tours-to-pre-lease-offices Pre-leasing commercial offices is easier when tenants can walk the space virtually months before handover. Commercial developers increasingly rely on VR tours to pre-lease office space before construction is finished. From showcasing lobby experiences to demonstrating flexible floorplates, VR helps non-technical tenants make confident decisions.

This post outlines five proven scenarios, including virtual test fits, executive presentations, and remote leasing roadshows. Commercial Leasing bp_interior_2024_1 Lighting Strategies for Minimalist Interior Renderings lighting-strategies-for-minimalist-interior-renderings Minimalist interiors rely on subtle lighting. Here’s how we approach natural and artificial light in 3D. In minimalist spaces, every shadow counts. We walk through how to balance natural light, artificial sources, and material reflectivity to avoid flat, lifeless imagery. You’ll see examples of how small changes in color temperature and contrast can significantly affect the perceived warmth of a scene. Interior Lighting Minimalist Sara Lindholm bp_exterior_2023_1 Rendering Snow: Crafting Convincing Winter Exteriors rendering-snow-crafting-convincing-winter-exteriors Winter exteriors require special attention to snow, fog, and daylight balance. Snowy exteriors are more than just white ground planes. From tire marks to roof buildup and window condensation, the details create realism. We describe our step-by-step process for crafting believable winter scenes while keeping materials and lighting flexible for late design changes. Exterior Winter Environment Marko Petrov bp_process_2022_1 Our 3D Visualization Workflow From Brief to Final Renders our-3d-visualization-workflow-from-brief-to-final-renders A transparent look at each step of our visualization process, including where revisions fit in. Understanding the production workflow helps teams gather the right inputs at the right time. We break down our process into seven stages: discovery, modeling, clay previews, look development, lighting and mood, high-resolution output, and final delivery.

At each step we explain what

article_urban_heat_island_cities_2025 How Street Trees Cool the Urban Heat Island Effect street-trees-cool-urban-heat-island-effect Discover how planting trees along streets, in schoolyards, and on rooftops can dramatically reduce the urban heat island effect and protect vulnerable communities. cities Dr. Maya Chen urban heat island street trees climate adaptation environmental justice article_urban_heat_island_schools_2025 Beating the Heat Island at Schools: Shade, Trees, and Playgrounds heat-island-schools-shade-trees-playgrounds Why schoolyards are urban heat islands—and how tree planting and nature-based design can cool them down. urban_forestry Luis Martinez schools children urban forestry article_urban_forest_basics Urban Forests 101: Why Cities Need Trees More Than Ever urban-forests-101-why-cities-need-trees An introduction to the benefits of urban forests, from cooling streets to boosting mental health. Elena Rossi species diversity reforestation metrics article_community_story_brazil_2024 From Pasture to Rainforest: A Community Story from Brazil from-pasture-to-rainforest-community-story-brazil Farmers in Brazil’s Atlantic Forest region share how restoring trees reshaped their land and livelihoods. community_stories João Pereira community stories Brazil agroforestry Atlantic Forest article_policy_urban_tree_equity Urban Tree Equity: Policies That Close the Canopy Gap urban-tree-equity-policies-close-canopy-gap A look at city policies that are helping distribute tree canopy more fairly across neighborhoods. In many cities, low-income and historically marginalized neighborhoods have fewer trees and higher temperatures. Urban tree equity policies aim to correct this by prioritizing public investment in these areas, updating zoning codes, and establishing long-term maintenance funding.

Examples include heat risk maps that guide tree planting, ordinances that require developers to preserve mature trees, and participatory budgeting that lets residents choose greening projects. policy Alicia Gomez urban equity heat risk tree canopy article_kids_family_planting Tree Planting with Kids: How to Make Fa

pages.dataKeywords: bts-commercial-times-square-2025 Behind the Scenes: Shooting a 45-Second Brand Spot in Times Square behind-the-scenes-45-second-brand-spot-times-square cinematography behind the scenes commercials lighting times square location shoot A look at how we lit and moved quickly for a high-pressure 45-second brand commercial in the middle of Times Square. For this 45-second brand commercial, we had just four hours of permitted time in Times Square. In this article we break down our pre-light, lens choices, and how we managed reflections in LED billboards while keeping skin tones natural. We also share our camera movement plan and how we coordinated with NYPD to keep the crew nimble and safe. bts-music-video-led-volume-2025 Behind the Scenes: LED Volume Cinematography for a Music Video behind-the-scenes-led-volume-music-video music video virtual production led volume How we used an LED volume to create five distinct worlds for a single-day music video shoot. Shooting in an LED volume gave us complete control over environments and reflections. In this breakdown, we cover our Unreal Engine workflows, color pipeline from on-set monitors to final grade, and practical tips for minimizing moiré on wardrobe. We also compare this approach to traditional green screen in terms of cost and schedule for indie artists. natural-light-doc-strategies-2024 Natural Light Strategies for Documentary Shoots natural-light-strategies-documentary documentary natural light run and gun location scouting Practical natural light techniques for documentaries when time, budget, and crew are limited. Most documentary shoots don’t allow for big lighting setups. This article walks through our process for scouting with the sun, using negative fill and bounce in cramped spaces, and making small LED units feel invisible to non-actors. We include frame grabs from three recent docs and diagrams you can adapt to your own shoots. directing-narrative-short-nyc Directing Performances in a 12-Minute New York Narrative Short directing-performances-new-york-narrative-short directing narrative short directing actors rehearsal new york Lessons learned from working with first-time actors on our award-winning New York short film. Casting first-time actors for a narrative short can bring freshness and authenticity, but it also requires a different directing approach. In this case study, we share our rehearsal process, how we blocked naturally in cramped apartments, and the communication tricks that kept performances grounded during a tight three-day shoot. project-calculator-deep-dive How Our Branded Content Project Calculator Works how-our-branded-content-project-calculator-works production_process project calculator branded content budgeting timeline A walkthrough of the assumptions behind our branded content estimator and how to read your results. Globex Pay partnered with our team to launch a cross-border mobile wallet focused on emerging markets. We delivered native iOS and Android apps with secure onboarding, automated KYC flows, multi-currency wallets, and real-time FX rates. The backend integrates with multiple payment gateways and AML providers, and the system is fully audited for PCI and GDPR compliance. NovaLend Capital needed a modern mobile-first experience for their SME lending product. We designed and implemented an onboarding flow, document upload and verification, automated risk scoring, and real-time loan status tracking. The app integrates with open banking APIs to analyze cash flow and uses a rules engine to provide instant approvals for low-risk applicants. pages.dataKeywords: art_shamanic_journeying_introduction What Is Shamanic Journeying? A Gentle Introduction what-is-shamanic-journeying-introduction Learn what shamanic journeying is, how it works, and what to expect from your first journeys. Mira Tal beginner shamanic_journeying foundations art_shamanic_journeying_preparing Preparing for Your First Shamanic Journey preparing-for-your-first-shamanic-journey Step-by-step preparation for your first shamanic journey, including intention-setting, safety, and basic ritual. Preparation is one of the most important parts of a safe and meaningful shamanic journey. This article walks you through choosing a clear and simple intention, creating a quiet and protected space, and the basics of calling in your guides. We explore how to work with a drum recording or rattle, what to do if you feel nervous, and how to gently close the space afterward. These beginner-friendly guidelines help you build trust with your own intuitive process. introduction ritual intention_setting art_shamanic_journeying_safe_return Safe Returning: How to Close a Shamanic Journey safe-returning-how-to-close-a-shamanic-journey Learn simple techniques to safely return, ground, and integrate after journeying. Every shamanic journey needs a clear opening and closing. In this guide, we explore how to follow the sound of the callback beats, thank your helping spirits, and intentionally step back into ordinary reality. We cover grounding practices such as touching the floor, drinking water, and naming five things you see in the room. This article also explains how to record your experience in a journal and how to discern what is symbolic, literal, or simply noise from the nervous system. Lena Crow intermediate grounding integration art_shamanic_journeying_meet_spirit_guide Meeting Your First Spirit Guide in a Shamanic Journey meeting-your-first-spirit-guide-in-a-shamanic-journey A beginner-friendly journey script and tips for meeting a compassionate helping spirit. One of the most beloved beginner journeys is meeting a primary helping spirit or power animal. Here we offer a simple drum journey script you can record for yourself, along with guidance on recognizing a true helping spirit versus projections of fear or fantasy. We speak about consent, boundaries, and how to ask your guide for clear signs that they are here to support you. You will also learn how to begin an ongoing relationship with your guide after the journey ends. spirit_guides power_animals art_shamanic_journeying_common_mistakes 7 Common Mistakes in Shamanic Journeying (and How to Avoid Them) common-shamanic-journeying-mistakes From forcing visions to skipping grounding, these are the pitfalls new journeyers can sidestep. Many beginners worry that they are "doing it wrong" when they begin shamanic journeying.
