Learn design basics, clearance rules, and ROI drivers. commercial_solar solar_carport parking_lot home_battery_sizing_guide How to Size a Home Battery for Backup and Savings how-to-size-a-home-battery-for-backup-and-savings Not sure if you need a 10 kWh or 20 kWh battery? This guide walks through backup priorities and bill savings. Battery sizing starts with your goals. For short-duration outage backup of essential loads, a 10–13 kWh battery is often sufficient. For extended outages or whole-home backup, multiple batteries totaling 20–40 kWh may be appropriate. Review your critical loads—refrigeration, lighting, networking, and medical equipment—and estimate their hourly consumption. Pairing batteries with solar maximizes bill savings by storing midday excess for evening use and supporting time-of-use rate arbitrage. home_battery energy_storage backup_power roof_and_solar_maintenance_checklist Annual Roof and Solar Maintenance Checklist annual-roof-and-solar-maintenance-checklist Extend the life of your roof and solar array with a simple annual maintenance routine. A combined roof and solar maintenance plan should include: visual inspection of shingles or membrane, checking for loose flashing, cleaning debris from gutters, thermal scanning for hidden moisture where applicable, visual inspection of solar modules, testing of open-circuit voltage and string current, verification of inverter performance, and cleaning of accessible panels where soiling is significant. Document findings with photos and track issues over time to avoid surprises. maintenance roof_inspection solar_panel_cleaning emergency_roof_leak_triage What to Do If Your Roof Starts Leaking During a Storm what-to-do-if-your-roof-starts-leaking-during-a-storm Quick steps to limit damage from an active roof leak before our emergency crew arrives. If you notice an active roof leak during a storm, move valuables away from the leak, place a bucket or container under the drip, and carefully relieve bulging ceiling paint by puncturing a small hole to let water drain in a controlled way. Do not climb on the roof while it is wet or windy. Take photos of the leak and any visible damage for insurance documentation. Our 24/7 emer

pages.dataKeywords: art_hybrid_focus_hours Hybrid Work, Focus Hours, and the Modern Office hybrid-work-focus-hours-modern-office Hybrid work has changed how teams use their office days. In this article, we explore how focus hours, collaboration rituals, and quiet zones can coexist in a modern hybrid office. We break down scheduling patterns, zoning strategies, and acoustic tips that help teams stay productive when only part of the team is on-site. Learn how to blend focus hours and collaboration rituals in a hybrid office without sacrificing productivity. Hybrid work Focus Remote work Maya Patel art_hybrid_neighborhood_refresh A One-Week Refresh Plan for Hybrid Neighborhoods one-week-refresh-plan-for-hybrid-neighborhoods Not every office update needs to be a full renovation. This one-week refresh plan focuses on small, high-impact changes across hybrid neighborhoods: updating task chairs, adding mobile whiteboards, and re-zoning underused corners into call booths or touchdown areas. Use this 7-day checklist to quickly refresh hybrid workspace neighborhoods without a major renovation. Office refresh Change management Daniel Kim art_hybrid_onboarding_checklist Hybrid Work Onboarding Checklist for New Hires hybrid-work-onboarding-checklist-for-new-hires Onboarding in a hybrid environment requires clarity around where work happens. This checklist covers technology, workspace norms, desk booking, and etiquette for using quiet rooms and collaboration zones. Give new hybrid employees a clear roadmap for using your office effectively from day one. People & culture Alex Rivera art_neurodiversity_open_plan Designing for Neurodiversity in Open-Plan Offices designing-for-neurodiversity-in-open-plan-offices Open-plan offices can be challenging for neurodivergent employees. We explore zoning, acoustic partitions, lighting control, and retreat spaces that support a wider range of sensory needs. Practical design moves to make open-plan offices more inclusive for neurodivergent teammates. Inclusive design Open-plan Ergonomics art_ergonomic_chair_basics Ergonomic Chair Basics: 5 Adjustments That Matter Most ergonomic-chair-basics-5-adjustments-that-matter-most Seat height, depth, lumbar support, armrests, and recline tension are the five adjustments that drive most day-to-day comfort. We explain how to set up each one for different body types. Before you invest in new chairs, understand the five adjustments that deliver the biggest comfort gains. Furniture Wellbeing Laura Chen art_workspace_trends_2026 Workspace Trends 2026: Flexible, Measured, and Human workspace-trends-2026-flexible-measured-and-human From sensor-informed utilization to softer, more residential finishes, 2026 continues the shift toward spaces that flex with teams and prioritize human experience. Our annual look at the workspace trends reshaping offices in 2026. art_language_rev_anishinaabe_intro Introduction to Language Revitalization in Anishinaabe Communities intro-language-revitalization-anishinaabe A beginner-friendly overview of how Anishinaabe communities in North America are reclaiming and revitalizing their languages through immersion camps, digital tools, and community leadership. north_america language revitalization north america community education beginner Marissa Eagle Jordan Whitecrow art_language_nests_pacific_northwest Language Nests in the Pacific Northwest language-nests-pacific-northwest How Indigenous families in the Pacific Northwest are using language nests to create immersive spaces for young children. language nest early childhood Talia James art_language_rev_community_radio Community Radio and Language Revitalization in North America community-radio-language-revitalization A look at how Indigenous community radio stations broadcast language lessons, storytelling, and local news. From northern Canada to the U.S.–Mexico borderlands, Indigenous community radio stations have become hubs for language revitalization. Stations air daily phrase-of-the-day segments, talk shows in Indigenous languages, and live coverage of community events...

This article surveys several stations, explores how they train youth as broadcasters, and considers how radio connects rural and urban community members. media community radio Carlos Running Bear art_language_rev_digital_tools Digital Tools for Indigenous Language Revitalization digital-tools-indigenous-language-revitalization An introduction to apps, online dictionaries, and social media campaigns supporting Indigenous language learning. Mobile apps, keyboard layouts, and online dictionaries have become important tools for Indigenous language learners. This article explains core concepts for getting started, including choosing a writing system, finding trusted resources, and setting realistic learning goals...

We highlight examples from North American communities, while also noting that technology is a tool, not a replacement for relationships with speakers and land. digital media education Naomi Littlefeather art_language_rev_intermediate_curricula Designing Community-Led Language Curriculum designing-community-led-language-curriculum Guidance for intermediate practitioners involved in creating language curricula rooted in Indigenous knowledge systems. Curriculum development for Indigenous languages must be grounded in community priorities... curriculum design intermediate Evelyn Yazzie art_governance_basics Indigenous Governance: Basic Concepts indigenous-governance-basic-concepts A beginner guide to core ideas in Indigenous governance, including sovereignty, self-determination, and nation-to-nation relationships. Indigenous governance refers to the ways Indigenous peoples organize political, social, and economic life according to their own laws and values. This article introduces key terms such as sovereignty, self-determination, and jurisdict

pages.dataKeywords:
ba_nettles_soup_30min Creamy Nettles Soup in 30 Minutes creamy-nettles-soup-30-minutes A simple, warming nettles soup you can make in half an hour from freshly foraged greens. This creamy nettles soup is one of the easiest ways to turn a basket of fresh spring nettles into a nourishing meal. In this recipe we'll cover how to safely handle nettles, how to blanch them to remove the sting, and how to build a rich flavour base with onions, garlic and potatoes.

Safety note: always wear gloves when harvesting and handling raw nettles. Once blanched, the sting disappears.

Serve this soup with crusty bread, a drizzle of olive oil, and a squeeze of lemon. nettles soup spring recipe vegetarian easy 200 g young nettle tops, rinsed 1 tbsp olive oil 1 onion, chopped 2 cloves garlic, sliced 2 medium potatoes, diced 1 L vegetable stock 100 ml cream or coconut milk Salt and pepper to taste Lemon wedges, to serve Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat. Add the onion and cook for 5–7 minutes until translucent. Stir in the garlic and potatoes and cook for 2 minutes. Pour in the vegetable stock, bring to a boil, then reduce to a simmer for 10 minutes. While the potatoes simmer, blanch the nettles in boiling water for 1 minute, then drain and squeeze out excess water. Roughly chop the nettles and add them to the pan. Simmer for 5 minutes. Blend the soup until smooth, then stir in the cream or coconut milk. Season with salt, pepper, and a squeeze of lemon to taste. Serve hot. published ba_nettles_pesto_20min Quick Nettles Pesto for Pasta or Toast quick-nettles-pesto-20-min Turn a handful of young nettles into a vibrant green pesto in under 20 minutes. Nettles make an earthy, mineral-rich alternative to basil in classic pesto. This recipe shows you how to safely prepare nettles and blitz them into a versatile sauce for pasta, grains, or toast.

Once blanched, nettles lose their sting but retain their deep green colour and flavour. Toasted nuts and good olive oil round everything out. pesto quick vegan very_easy 2 packed cups young nettle leaves 1/3 cup toasted sunflower seeds or pine nuts 1 small garlic clove 1/3 cup extra virgin olive oil 2 tbsp nutritional yeast or grated Parmesan Juice of 1/2 lemon Salt to taste Bring a pot of water to a boil. Wearing gloves, add nettles and blanch for 60–90 seconds. Drain nettles and rinse under cold water, then squeeze out excess moisture. Add nettles, toasted seeds, garlic, lemon juice, and nutritional yeast or Parmesan to a food processor. Pulse while drizzling in olive oil until a thick, spoonable paste forms. Season with salt and adjust lemon to taste. Serve immediately or store in the fridge in a jar topped with a thin layer of oil. ba_nettles_frittata_40min Oven-Baked Nettles and Wild Garlic Frittata nettles-wild-garlic-frittata A protein-rich brunch frittata featuring spring nettles and wild garlic, baked in the oven. This oven-baked frittata packs early spring greens into a simple one-pan meal. Nettles,

pages.dataKeywords: tracheal_gill Tracheal gill A thin-walled external extension of the body that contains tracheae and allows aquatic insects to exchange gases with the surrounding water. adaptations gill_filament Gill filament A slender, thread-like gill structure that increases surface area for oxygen uptake in aquatic insect larvae. morphology anal_gills Anal gills Gills located near the tip of the abdomen that allow some aquatic larvae to absorb dissolved oxygen from the water. gill_tuft Gill tuft A cluster of fine gill branches that functions as a compact respiratory organ in some aquatic insects. stone_case Stone case A portable shelter built from small stones, sand, or plant material and silk by some caddisfly larvae for protection and camouflage. clinging_claws Clinging claws Enlarged, curved tarsal claws that help aquatic insects cling to rocks in fast-flowing streams. flattened_body Flattened body A dorsoventrally flattened body shape that reduces drag and helps insects stay attached to substrates in swift currents. burrowing_legs Burrowing legs Short, stout legs often equipped with spines that enable larvae to dig into sand or fine sediment. nymph Nymph The immature stage of insects with incomplete metamorphosis that generally resembles the adult but lacks fully developed wings and reproductive structures. taxonomy larva Larva The immature stage of insects with complete metamorphosis that differs markedly from the adult form. riffle Riffle A shallow, fast-flowing section of a stream where water tumbles over coarse substrate, often rich in dissolved oxygen and macroinvertebrates. habitat lentic Lentic Describes standing or still water habitats such as ponds and lakes. kick_sampling Kick sampling A macroinvertebrate sampling method in which the streambed is disturbed by kicking while a net captures dislodged organisms. sampling_methods surber_sampler Surber sampler A quantitative sampling device that encloses a known area of streambed and captures organisms dislodged within that frame. ephemeroptera Ephemeroptera Mayflies incomplete_hemimetabolous Aquatic nymphs with leaf-like or filamentous abdominal gills; adults are short-lived and often emerge in large synchronized swarms. Many species are highly sensitive to pollution and are key indicators of good water quality. odonata Odonata Dragonflies and Damselflies Predatory nymphs with extendable labia used to capture prey; adults are agile aerial predators. Larvae occupy a range of habitats from streams to ponds and wetlands. plecoptera Plecoptera Stoneflies Flattened nymphs typically with two long cerci and well-developed thoracic legs adapted for clinging to rocks in cold streams. Many species require high dissolved oxygen and are among the most pollution-sensitive insects. trichoptera Trichoptera Caddisflies complete_holometabolous Larvae often build protective cases or nets; adults resemble small moths and are important food for fish. pages.dataKeywords: parental_leave Parental leave Up to 18 weeks of fully paid parental leave for primary caregivers. Global baseline policy with local adaptations; exact duration and pay structure may vary based on country-specific regulations. health_insurance Health insurance Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision coverage in most locations. We provide market-competitive health insurance plans that typically include medical, dental, and vision coverage for employees and their eligible dependents. Coverage details, providers, and cost-sharing arrangements (such as premiums and co-pays) vary by country and, in some cases, by region. Where private insurance is not customary, we supplement public healthcare with additional wellness benefits. Offered in all employing countries with plan design tailored to local market standards and regulations. remote_work_stipend Remote work & home office stipend Annual stipend for remote setup and monthly support for internet and utilities. To help you do your best work from anywhere, we offer an annual home office stipend that can be used toward ergonomic furniture, monitors, and other productivity equipment. Many locations also provide a recurring monthly allowance to offset internet, phone, or utility costs associated with remote work. Stipend amounts and eligible expenses may vary depending on your country of employment. Available to remote-eligible roles in most countries; specific stipend amounts are set locally. learning_development Learning & development budget Annual budget for courses, conferences, and certifications. We invest in continuous learning through an annual development budget that employees can use for conferences, online courses, books, professional memberships, and certifications. In addition, we run internal training programs, mentorship opportunities, and leadership development tracks. Budget amounts are benchmarked by country to reflect local pricing and currency differences. Global program with budget levels and eligible vendors tailored by region. retirement_plan Retirement & pension plans Company-sponsored retirement savings or pension programs. Depending on your country of employment, we offer employer-matched retirement savings plans (such as 401(k) in the US) or participate in statutory pension schemes with additional company contributions. Eligibility, vesting schedules, and contribution limits differ by country but are designed to help you build long-term financial security. pages.dataKeywords:
mpa_senate_outreach_2026 Protect Working Waterfronts in New Marine Protected Areas Tell your Senators to support balanced marine protected areas that protect both ecosystems and fishing communities. Federal agencies are considering new marine protected areas (MPAs) that could close large sections of productive fishing grounds. While conservation is essential, these proposals must account for the livelihoods of commercial fishermen and coastal communities.

This campaign urges Senators to push for science-based MPAs that:
- Clearly define conservation objectives
- Include fishermen in the planning process
- Allow sustainable commercial fishing where compatible with conservation goals
- Provide adequate transition support where access is reduced

Use the form on this page to send a tailored message to your Senators. You can customize the text to reflect your fishery, gear type, and community. marine_protected_areas active Support balanced marine protected areas that keep fishermen working quota_flexibility_2026 Improve Quota Management Flexibility for Small-Scale Fleets Ask Congress to support quota management reforms that work for small boats. Catch share and quota systems can stabilize fisheries, but poorly designed programs can concentrate access and harm small-boat operators. This campaign supports legislation that would require periodic reviews of quota systems, set guardrails on consolidation, and create community quota banks. quotas_management Support quota management reforms for small-scale fleets I am writing to urge you to support reforms to federal quota management programs that protect small-boat and family-owned fishing businesses. Concentration of quota into fewer hands threatens working waterfronts and new entrants.

Please support policies that set common-sense limits on consolidation, promote community quota banks, and require regular reviews of quota programs with input from working fishermen. bycatch_innovation_grants Fund Bycatch Reduction Innovation Grants Call for robust funding of gear innovation and bycatch reduction programs. Modernizing fishing gear and practices is one of the most effective ways to reduce bycatch. This campaign advocates for expanded federal funding for bycatch innovation grants, including support for gear trials led by fishermen. bycatch_policy upcoming Invest in bycatch reduction innovation Please support robust funding for bycatch reduction innovation grants in the upcoming appropriations bills. These programs help fishermen and scientists work together to test and adopt gear that reduces bycatch while keeping fisheries economically viable. crew_safety_training_standard Establish a National Fishing Crew Safety Training Standard Promote uniform, practical safety training requirements for commercial crew. Fishing remains one of the most dangerous jobs in America. This campaign calls on regulators to adopt a national baseline standard for crew safety training that is practical, accessible, and informed by

pages.dataKeywords:
event_3dp_beginner_evening_1 Intro to 3D Printing: Beginner Workshop (Evening) Get started with 3D printers after class Beginner-friendly evening workshop covering 3D printer basics, safety, and how to submit your first print. This beginner-friendly workshop introduces you to the 3D printers available in the library makerspace. We will cover basic safety, how to prepare simple models for printing, approved materials, and how to submit your first print job to the queue.

No prior experience is required. This session is designed especially for students who have never used a 3D printer before.

Topics include:
- 3D printer safety and policies
- Supported file formats and where to find starter models
- Basic slicing settings (layer height, infill, supports)
- How to submit a print request through the library system

You’ll leave with the knowledge needed to safely and successfully request your first 3D print. 3d_printing beginner students in_person free America/New_York Makerspace Lab 101 Library Lower Level, Room 101 (Makerspace) 3d printing 3d printer workshop fabrication event_3dp_beginner_evening_2 Intro to 3D Printing: Beginner Workshop (Late Session) Second-chance evening 3D printing intro A repeat beginner 3D printing workshop for students who can’t attend earlier in the month. Missed the first intro? This late-session beginner 3D printing workshop covers the same core content: safety, file preparation, and how to submit prints through the makerspace.

We’ll walk through:
- Makerspace 3D printing policies
- Finding and downloading simple models
- Basic slicing settings using our supported software
- Submitting and tracking your print jobs

This session is ideal for students who want a refresher or couldn’t attend the first evening workshop. evening event_skill_building_morning_1 Skill-Building Lab: Intro to Vector Design Morning design skills for makers Learn basic vector design skills for use with laser cutters, vinyl cutters, and other makerspace tools. Develop core digital design skills in this hands-on skill-building lab focused on vector graphics. We’ll introduce the basics of working with paths, shapes, and text using free software tools.

This session is ideal preparation for working with our laser cutter, vinyl cutter, and CNC tools, and is appropriate for beginners.

You’ll practice:
- Setting up documents for fabrication
- Creating simple shapes and logos
- Preparing designs for export to makerspace equipment

All software is provided on lab computers; you may optionally bring your own laptop. skill_building Design Studio 201 Library 2nd Floor, Room 201 (Design Studio) skill-building vector design laser cutter graphics event_skill_building_midday_1 Skill-Building Lab: Rapid Prototyping Techniques Turn ideas into quick physical prototypes Hands-on skill-building session exploring simple rapid prototyping methods using cardboard, foam, and 3D-printed components. In this mid-day skill-building lab, you’ll learn rapid prototyping techniq

pages.dataKeywords: understanding_1031_exchanges_multifamily_investors Understanding 1031 Exchanges for Multifamily Investors understanding-1031-exchanges-for-multifamily-investors An in-depth guide to how 1031 exchanges work, with a focus on multifamily properties and common timelines, rules, and pitfalls. 1031_exchange 1031 exchange multifamily tax deferral replacement property qualified intermediary David Chen, CPA step_by_step_1031_exchange_timeline Step-by-Step 1031 Exchange Timeline Checklist step-by-step-1031-exchange-timeline-checklist A practical checklist covering the 45-day identification and 180-day closing requirements in a 1031 exchange. Timing is one of the most common failure points in a 1031 exchange. Investors have 45 days from the sale of their relinquished property to identify replacement assets, and 180 days to close. This article provides a chronological checklist, from engaging a qualified intermediary before closing, to drafting identification letters, coordinating with lenders and sponsors, and documenting any failed or partial exchanges. We include downloadable templates you can adapt for your own exchange plan. timeline checklist tax strategies Laura Martinez, Esq. 1031_exchange_risks_and_pitfalls 1031 Exchange Risks and Pitfalls Every Investor Should Know 1031-exchange-risks-and-pitfalls Key legal, tax, and execution risks that can cause a 1031 exchange to fail, and how to mitigate them. While 1031 exchanges offer powerful tax benefits, they also introduce additional complexity and risk. Common pitfalls include missing critical deadlines, selecting ineligible replacement properties, commingling funds, and misunderstanding boot. In this article we outline the most frequent issues we see investors encounter, how sponsors typically structure 1031-friendly syndications or DSTs, and the questions you should ask before wiring exchange proceeds into a new deal. tax_strategies risk management due diligence Karen O'Neill, JD, LLM evaluate_multifamily_deal_15_minutes How to Evaluate a Multifamily Deal in 15 Minutes how-to-evaluate-a-multifamily-deal-in-15-minutes A quick framework for screening multifamily offerings using five key metrics and qualitative checks. Busy investors need a repeatable way to screen multifamily offerings. In this guide, we walk through five key areas: sponsorship, location, business plan, leverage, and projected returns. We explain how to use metrics like DSCR, debt yield, cash-on-cash, and IRR to quickly categorize a deal as core, value-add, or opportunistic, and when a fast "no" is the right answer. underwriting deal screening cash yield Mark Rivera office_investing_hybrid_work_world Office Investing in a Hybrid Work World office-investing-in-a-hybrid-work-world How hybrid work trends are reshaping office demand, lease structures, and value-add strategies. pages.dataKeywords: mara Mara Vance Resourceful strategist who emerged as an unlikely leader after the blackout. Mara Vance grew up on the industrial edges of New Harbor, teaching herself systems engineering from salvaged tech. When the citywide blackout shattered the old order, she became the de facto strategist for a fracturing resistance, balancing moral lines against impossible choices. Her arc in Seasons 1 and 2 charts her evolution from quiet analyst to public symbol of the rebellion, with a growing rift between her and Ezra over what sacrifices are acceptable. ezra Ezra Kade Frontline tactician whose loyalty to Mara is tested by the uprising’s cost. Ezra Kade is a former city warden who defected after discovering the regime’s secret surveillance program. Charismatic and stubborn, he quickly became the face of the rebellion’s operations, leading dangerous raids into the core districts. His partnership with Mara is both the rebellion’s greatest strength and its most volatile fault line, as his instinct for decisive action clashes with her long-game strategies. kaito Kaito Ren Underground broadcaster who turns hacked airwaves into a weapon. Kaito Ren runs the pirate channel ‘Night Signal,’ hijacking abandoned transmitters to beam uncensored news and coded calls-to-action across New Harbor. Once a shy sound engineer, Kaito now crafts the rebellion’s sonic identity, layering rallying speeches with forbidden music. His broadcasts have made him a folk hero to some and a top target to the regime’s trackers. lena Lena Ortiz Former regime scientist trying to undo the damage she helped create. Dr. Lena Ortiz once led the city’s predictive policing lab, designing algorithms that decided who was ‘high risk’ before any crime was committed. Haunted by how her work was weaponized, she defected and now helps Mara and Ezra decode the regime’s systems from the inside. Brilliant but guarded, Lena’s knowledge is invaluable—but her past makes it hard for others to fully trust her. Dr. Sol Anders Idealistic historian archiving the uprising in real time. Dr. Sol Anders believes stories outlive regimes. As the city’s unofficial archivist, he records testimonies, captures secret footage, and curates the ‘shadow archive’ that documents the uprising as it unfolds. Sol’s episodes provide a reflective counterpoint to the action, revealing how everyday people experience the fallout from Mara and Ezra’s decisions. ev_liveqa_2026_03_02 Live Q&A: Season 2 Deep Dive Join the showrunner and cast, including Mara’s and Ezra’s actors, for an in-depth live Q&A about Season 2 twists, character arcs, and what’s ahead in Season 3. live_qa online America/New_York ev_liveqa_2026_03_05 Live Q&A: Writing the Uprising The writers’ room breaks down how they built the rebellion storyline, from Mara and Ezra’s dynamic to the moral gray areas of Season 2. ev_watchparty_2026_03_01 Season 2 Finale Global Watch Party Sync up with fans worldwide to rewatch the explosive Season 2 finale with live chat commentary. w

pages.dataKeywords: chicago_blackhawks Chicago Blackhawks Chicago toronto_maple_leafs Toronto Maple Leafs Toronto Canada pittsburgh_penguins Pittsburgh Penguins Pittsburgh rockford_icehogs Rockford IceHogs Rockford ska_st_petersburg SKA St. Petersburg Saint Petersburg Russia boston_university_terriers Boston University Terriers ncaa Boston guide_spotting_fakes_2023_modern_tells OldBarnCollector The section about fight strap stitching was spot on. I caught a fake 1992 Hawks jersey last month using exactly this tip. BlueLineBob I’d add that many modern fakes still mess up the size tag fonts on 2010s Reebok edge jerseys. GameWornNewbie Thanks, this is the first 2023 guide I’ve seen that explains photo-matching steps in plain language. PatchHunter Great call-out on incorrect anniversary patches. I’ve seen tons of fake Blackhawks 75th season patches lately. LockerRoomFind Do you think this applies the same way to AHL gamers, or are there different fake tells there? VintageThreads For 1980s jerseys the hem stitching patterns can be a dead giveaway too. Might be worth adding a section on that. 90sGoalie Loved the example photos of fake vs real puck marks. Helps a ton for 1990s goalie jerseys. TagInspector The font comparison chart for CCM tag variations alone makes this guide a must-bookmark. StickMarks One more tip: check for repeating wear patterns in seller photos recycled across multiple listings. HawksHistorian For Chicago Blackhawks gamers, compare the sleeve number angle to Getty Images. Fakes often get that angle wrong. SignedOnly Could you expand the part on authentication COAs for signed game-worn jerseys? That’d help a lot. ThirdJerseyFan This 2023 fake-spotting guide should be required reading for anyone buying third jerseys online. guide_spotting_fakes_2024_tag_stitching LabelCheck The macro photos of neck labels are super helpful. I finally see the difference in the NHL shield embossing. EUCollector Any chance you can add a section on KHL game-worn fakes? Lots popping up on European sites lately. care_ultimate_storage_guide ClosetCurator The reminder not to use wire hangers on heavy goalie jerseys probably saved a few collars here. BasementBuyer Dehumidifier tip is gold. My 1990s Leafs gamers finally stopped smelling like the old basement. buying_guide_nhl_goalie_2024 CreaseCollector Helpful breakdown of price tiers. Surprised how affordable some backup NHL goalie gamers still are. PadStack Would love a follow-up covering AHL and KHL goalie jerseys, especially under the $400 range. Spotting Fake Game-Worn Jerseys: Modern Tells (2023 Guide) spotting-fake-game-worn-jerseys-2023-modern-tells guides guide Authentication Spotting Fakes other A deep-dive 2023 guide to spotting fake game-worn jerseys using stitch patterns, tagging inconsistencies, photo-matching, and wear analysis across NHL, AHL, and European leagues. game-worn fake-spotting authentication article.html?id=guide_spotting_fakes_2023_modern_tells Advanced Tag & Stitching Checks for Fake Game-Worn Jers

BABY10 10% off pastel baby shower decor for eligible baby shower theme items. percentage theme baby_shower WELCOME15 15% off your first booking on cart totals over $300. cart_total SAVE50 $50 off selected wedding decor categories on orders over $800. fixed_amount category wedding_decor SPRING20 20% off selected birthday themes during the 2025 spring season. birthday_spring wedding_cancellation_policy What is your wedding cancellation policy? You may cancel your wedding decor booking in writing at any time. Cancellations made 60 or more days before the event date receive a full refund of payments made, minus a non‑refundable 10% design retainer. Cancellations between 30–59 days receive a 50% refund of the decor package total. Cancellations made less than 30 days before the event date are non‑refundable, but you may request a one‑time date change subject to availability and any applicable price differences. wedding-cancellation-policy wedding_reschedule_policy Can I reschedule my wedding decor booking? Yes. You can request one complimentary date change up to 30 days before your original wedding date, subject to availability. Additional changes or changes requested within 30 days may incur a rescheduling fee, and package pricing will be updated to current rates if the new date falls in a higher‑demand season. wedding-reschedule-policy wedding_deposit_amount How much is the deposit for wedding decoration packages? For wedding decoration packages, a 30% non‑refundable design retainer is required to secure your date. The remaining balance is due 14 days before the event. For last‑minute bookings within 30 days, the full amount is due at the time of booking. wedding-deposit-amount wedding_guest_count_changes Can I adjust my guest count after booking a wedding package? You may increase or decrease your guest count up to 10 days before the wedding, as long as the new count is within the supported range of your selected package. Price adjustments will be applied per‑guest or per‑table where applicable, and an updated invoice will be issued for your approval. wedding-guest-count-changes wedding_includes_floral Do your wedding packages include fresh floral centerpieces? Several of our standard and premium wedding packages include fresh floral centerpieces, while budget packages may feature high‑quality faux florals or simple greenery. Each package clearly lists whether fresh floral centerpieces are included and how many are provided based on your guest count. wedding-floral-centerpieces wedding_setup_teardown_times What are your setup and teardown times for wedding decorations? pages.dataKeywords: cs_tech_launch_led_01 Global Tech Product Launch with Immersive LED Wall technology over_500 Product Launch High-impact global product launch for a technology client featuring a 60-foot curved LED video wall and interactive demos. Our team produced a flagship product launch for a cloud software company, transforming a downtown venue into an immersive brand environment. A 60-foot curved LED video wall served as the stage backdrop, displaying dynamic motion graphics, live camera feeds, and real-time social media content. The event hosted over 800 in-person attendees and thousands of virtual viewers. We managed all stage design, content programming, live streaming, and presenter coaching. cs_tech_launch_led_02 Software Suite Launch with Multi-Segment LED Canvas Regional launch event for a software suite using a segmented LED wall to highlight product stories. For a major SaaS provider, we designed a regional launch experience that combined a segmented LED video wall with kinetic lighting and synchronized audio. The LED canvas displayed animated UI walkthroughs, customer testimonials, and live data visualizations, helping the audience connect with the new product suite. Over 550 attendees participated in-person, with simultaneous streaming to three additional markets. cs_finance_summit_01 Annual Investor Day Hybrid Summit finance between_100_499 Investor Summit Hybrid investor day with synchronized in-room and remote experiences for a global asset manager. We produced a hybrid investor summit with 320 in-room attendees and over 2,000 remote participants. The event featured multi-camera live switching, secure webcast distribution, and branded motion graphics. Our team handled agenda design, speaker rehearsals, and on-site production, ensuring compliance-friendly messaging and flawless transitions between panels, keynotes, and Q&A sessions. cs_healthcare_townhall_01 Healthcare System Leadership Town Hall healthcare Internal Town Hall System-wide hybrid town hall for a healthcare network with live Q&A and remote clinic participation. A regional healthcare system engaged us to produce a leadership town hall connecting the main campus with eight satellite clinics. We implemented secure live streaming, moderated Q&A, and real-time polling to give frontline staff a voice. The production emphasized clear audio, accessibility, and simple remote participation, with detailed run-of-show planning to keep clinical operations uninterrupted. cs_retail_kickoff_01 National Retail Sales Kickoff Experience retail Sales Kickoff High-energy sales kickoff with LED stage, live entertainment, and interactive product zones. For a national retailer, we produced a multi-day sales kickoff featuring main-stage general sessions, breakouts, and a branded expo floor. A wide LED wall and intelligent lighting created dynamic main-stage looks. We coordinated entertainment, scripting, and content development, plus gamified learning stations in the expo to reinforce new product prioriti

pages.dataKeywords:
cs_hair_scalp_detox_quick Fast-Track Scalp Detox Shampoo Line for Indie Brand hair_care An indie clean-beauty brand launched a sulfate-free scalp detox shampoo line in just three months using our stock base platform. • Time-to-launch: 3 months from project kickoff to first shipment
• 97% batch acceptance rate in the first production run
• 40% lower development cost versus a fully custom formulation
• Gained nationwide placement with two specialty retail chains cs_hair_clean_shampoo_line Clean Shampoo Collection with Global Retailer Listing A mid-size brand expanded into a clean shampoo collection and met a strict retailer launch window in four months. • Time-to-launch: 4 months
• Approved in the first retailer quality audit
• 25% above forecast sell-through in the first 8 weeks
• Platform now used for seasonal fragrance extensions cs_hair_salon_partner Salon Partner Clarifying Shampoo Refresh A professional salon brand refreshed its hero clarifying shampoo with a gentler, sulfate-free base in just eight weeks. • Time-to-launch: 2 months
• 20% reduction in reported scalp irritation
• Maintained 95% of original clarifying performance in lab tests
• Positive adoption from over 2,000 partner salons cs_hair_premium_color_line Premium Color Care Range with Bond-Repair Technology A prestige brand built a full color care regimen with bond-repair shampoo, conditioner, and mask over a nine-month R&D program. A prestige hair care brand commissioned a fully custom color care system with bond-repair technology. The project included shampoo, conditioner, and a deep treatment mask across three fragrance directions.

We developed a new base system, performed accelerated and real-time stability, conducted bond-strength testing, and navigated complex claims substantiation across the EU and North America. • Time-to-launch: 9 months
• 3-product regimen with aligned fragrance profiles
• Clinically supported bond-repair claims
• Launch sell-out in several key prestige retailers cs_face_antiaging_serum_launch Anti-Aging Serum Line for Direct-to-Consumer Brand face_care A DTC brand launched a trio of anti-aging serums with graded price tiers using shared base technology. We helped a digitally native brand rapidly build a good-better-best anti-aging serum ladder, using one shared base system and different active complexes at each price tier. • Time-to-launch: 5 months
• 3 coordinated SKUs with unified sensorial profile
• 30% overall margin improvement versus prior contract manufacturer cs_bath_body_bar_soap_sustainable Plastic-Free Bar Soap Program for Retail Chain bath_body A regional retailer transitioned its private label bar soaps into plastic-free, recycled carton formats. We redesigned a five-SKU bar soap range to remove plastic film and move into FSC-certified recycled cartons, while maintaining price points and improving perceived quality. • 100% plastic film removed from the program
• 60% post-consumer recycled fiber in cartons

pages.dataKeywords: riley-kingston Riley Kingston Riley Kingston is a modern electric blues guitarist and vocalist known for gritty Chicago-style riffs and soulful songwriting. After years on the club circuit, he founded South Side Ember Records to highlight contemporary blues voices. maria-lopez-band Maria Lopez Band Fronted by powerhouse vocalist Maria Lopez, the band blends West Coast jump blues with gospel-tinged harmonies. Their live sets are driven by horn stabs, call-and-response choruses, and extended vocal improvisations. the-delta-runners The Delta Runners The Delta Runners are a quartet that channels raw Mississippi delta blues with contemporary arrangements, featuring slide guitar, harmonica, and minimalist percussion. chicago-fire-blues-band Chicago Fire Blues Band Chicago Fire Blues Band is a hard-hitting electric blues outfit rooted firmly in the tradition of the Windy City, with searing guitar leads and a tight rhythm section. lena-hart Lena Hart Lena Hart is a singer-songwriter whose smoky contralto voice and piano-led arrangements draw from classic torch songs, urban blues, and soul ballads. the-midnight-ramblers The Midnight Ramblers The Midnight Ramblers are a five-piece band specializing in moody, late-night blues with touches of psychedelia and roots rock, often recorded live to tape. hazel-james-trio Hazel James Trio Led by guitarist and vocalist Hazel James, this trio strips blues back to its essentials with raw, intimate performances and extended improvisations. smokehouse-johnson Smokehouse Johnson Smokehouse Johnson is a veteran harmonica player and storyteller who mixes front-porch acoustic blues with electric band arrangements. northside-electric Northside Electric Northside Electric is a Chicago-based collective that fuses blues grooves with modern production, mixing analog tape warmth and subtle electronic textures. bones-and-brass-band Bones & Brass Band Bones & Brass Band blends New Orleans second-line rhythms with brass-heavy blues arrangements, delivering high-energy festival sets. analog-tape-in-a-digital-world-2024 Analog Tape in a Digital World: How We Track Modern Blues Records analog-tape-in-a-digital-world Engineer Carla Nguyen explains our hybrid analog tape and digital workflow for modern blues sessions. pages.dataKeywords: tumor_seg_v1 Tumor Segmentation v1 tumor_segmentation colon First-generation tumor segmentation model optimized for colorectal adenocarcinoma whole-slide images. Outputs tumor probability maps and total tumor area (mm²). tumor_seg_v2 Tumor Segmentation v2 Improved tumor segmentation model with refined boundaries and better handling of mucinous components. Provides tumor area (mm²) and confidence metrics. nuclei_det_v2 Nuclei Detection v2 nuclei_detection other Second-generation nuclei detection and segmentation algorithm for H&E slides across multiple organs. Reports per-slide nuclei count and density metrics. til_density_map TIL Density Map breast Generates a tumor-infiltrating lymphocyte (TIL) density heatmap on breast cancer H&E slides, reporting local and global TIL densities in cells/mm². pdl1_quant PD-L1 Quantification biomarker_quantification lung Automated PD-L1 biomarker quantification for NSCLC cases on IHC-stained slides, providing percentage of PD-L1 positive tumor and immune cells. focus_qc_score Focus Quality Scoring Quality control algorithm computing a per-slide focus score between 0.0 and 1.0 to identify out-of-focus or low-quality scans. case_crc_001 CRC-2023-001 colorectal cancer Adenocarcinoma stage_ii Baseline resection specimen, moderate differentiation. case_crc_002 CRC-2023-002 stage_iii Segmental colectomy with lymph node involvement. case_crc_003 CRC-2022-015 Mucinous adenocarcinoma High mucin content; pages.dataKeywords:
hazardous_scrap_metal_guide Hazardous Scrap Metal Items That Need Special Handling hazardous-scrap-metal-items-special-handling Not all metal can go straight onto the scale. Learn which hazardous and restricted metal items need special handling or cannot be accepted at our yards. published hazardous restricted safety compliance lead-acid batteries mercury-containing switches PCB ballasts radioactive scrap (including contaminated metals) pressurized gas cylinders residential_pickup_guide How Residential Scrap Metal Pickup Works how-residential-scrap-metal-pickup-works Step-by-step guide to scheduling a home pickup for appliances and other household metal items. residential_pickup appliances how_to business_recycling_manufacturing Recycling Programs for Small Manufacturing Shops recycling-programs-small-manufacturing-shops Compare plan options designed for machine shops and small manufacturers, including scheduled monthly pickups. Small and mid-sized manufacturers generate valuable metal scrap, but managing it shouldn’t be a distraction from production.

We offer several business recycling plans tailored to manufacturing, including options with:
- Monthly scheduled pickup routes
- On-demand roll-off or gaylord swaps
- Container placement at no or low cost

Our Starter Manufacturing plan is specifically designed for shops with light to moderate scrap volume and a budget under $200 per month. business manufacturing programs materials_guide_non_ferrous Non-Ferrous Metals: Copper, Aluminum, and More non-ferrous-metals-copper-aluminum-guide Overview of our most common non-ferrous metals, including high-value copper grades. Non-ferrous metals like copper and aluminum are among the most valuable materials we buy.

### Common copper items
Examples include:
- Bare bright copper wire
- #1 insulated copper wire
- #2 insulated copper wire
- Copper pipe and tubing

Prices vary by yard and market conditions, and many clean copper items pay more than $2.00 per pound. materials copper non_ferrous dumpster_rental_overview Choosing the Right Scrap Metal Dumpster Size choosing-scrap-metal-dumpster-size A quick comparison of 10-yard, 20-yard, and larger metal dumpsters for projects and cleanouts. We offer 10-yard, 20-yard, 30-yard, and 40-yard scrap metal dumpsters in select ZIP codes. For many small projects, a 20-yard box offers the best balance between capacity and placement space.

Use the Container Rental page to enter your ZIP code, compare daily rates, and select a rental period that fits your schedule. dumpster_rental projects contractors pricing_and_payout_methods Understanding Scrap Metal Pricing and Payout Options understanding-scrap-metal-pricing-and-payout-options Learn how prices are set, how to compare pickup vs. drop-off, and which payment methods are available. pages.dataKeywords: art_private_credit_yield_landscape_2025 Navigating the Private Credit Yield Landscape in 2025 private_credit An overview of yield opportunities and risk premia across private credit segments in the current rate environment. In 2025, private credit continues to offer an attractive yield premium over broadly syndicated loans and high yield bonds. Direct lending strategies, in particular, benefit from tighter covenants and bespoke structuring. This article analyzes current spreads by borrower size, sector, and seniority in the capital structure, and examines how manager selection and structuring discipline influence realized returns. Emily Carter Michael Ross navigating-private-credit-yield-landscape-2025 art_private_credit_structured_solutions_2025 Structured Solutions in Private Credit: Unitranche and Beyond How unitranche, delayed-draw, and PIK structures are evolving in sponsor-backed deals. Sponsor-backed borrowers increasingly seek flexibility in their capital structures. Unitranche facilities, delayed-draw term loans, and PIK toggles can align incentives between sponsors and lenders while preserving downside protection. We review recent deal structures, key terms, and case studies where bespoke private credit solutions unlocked complex transactions. Daniel Nguyen structured-solutions-in-private-credit-unitranche-and-beyond art_private_credit_risk_management_2025 Three Pillars of Risk Management in Private Credit Portfolios A framework for underwriting, monitoring, and workout planning in private credit. Effective risk management in private credit starts with disciplined underwriting, but it does not end there. Managers must actively monitor performance covenants, sector fundamentals, and sponsor behavior. This article outlines three pillars of risk management: upfront structuring, proactive portfolio monitoring, and robust workout planning, supported by real-world examples from recent cycles. Laura Becker three-pillars-of-risk-management-in-private-credit-portfolios art_private_credit_covenant_trends_2024 Covenant Trends in Middle-Market Private Credit How covenant-light dynamics in syndicated markets influence middle-market private deals. While covenant-lite structures dominate broadly syndicated markets, middle-market private credit still relies on maintenance covenants to manage downside risk. We analyze recent covenant packages across sectors, discuss sponsor pushback on leverage and coverage tests, and highlight where lenders are holding the line. covenant-trends-in-middle-market-private-credit art_growth_equity_saas_scaling Scaling SaaS with Growth Equity: Lessons from 50+ Investments growth_equity Key drivers of value creation in growth-stage SaaS backed by growth equity capital. Growth equity investors in SaaS focus on efficient growth, cohort economics, and capital allocation discipline. Drawing on more than 50 historical investments, we examine which metrics most strongly correlate with outsized outcomes and how investors partner wi

pages.dataKeywords:
blog_small_apartment_layout_2025 Smart Layout Ideas for Small Apartments smart-layout-ideas-small-apartments Make every square foot count with flexible furniture, smart storage, and light-first layouts tailored to compact apartments. small apartment space saving apartment layout blog_small_apartment_storage_2025 Hidden Storage Tricks for Tiny City Apartments hidden-storage-tricks-tiny-city-apartments Turn awkward corners and dead zones into beautiful, hard-working storage in your studio or one-bedroom. Storage is the number one challenge our small-apartment clients mention. Instead of bulky wardrobes, we favor shallow built-ins, over-door cabinets, and under-seat storage that blends into the design. In this article we cover measurement guidelines, ideal depths for different types of storage, and how to choose finishes that visually recede.

We share three real-life case studies, including a 340 sq ft rental where we added 90 cubic feet of storage without blocking a single window. storage city living blog_small_apartment_color_2024 Using Color to Open Up a Small Apartment using-color-to-open-up-a-small-apartment The right palette can make a 350 sq ft apartment feel airy instead of cramped. Color impacts how spacious your home feels. We explore light-reflective neutrals, accent walls, and tone-on-tone schemes that keep visual noise low while still feeling warm and personal. We also share paint and fabric combinations that work especially well in small city apartments. color paint blog_kitchen_renovation_budget_guide How to Plan a Realistic Budget for Your Kitchen Renovation kitchen-renovation-budget-guide From cabinetry to countertops, we break down where your kitchen budget really goes. Kitchen renovations are one of the most expensive projects in a home. In this guide, we benchmark costs for cabinetry, appliances, countertops, lighting, and labor at three budget levels. We also outline where it makes sense to splurge, where to save, and how our studio structures fixed-fee vs per-room pricing for kitchen design packages. kitchen budget renovation blog_home_office_industrial Designing an Industrial-Style Home Office that Still Feels Warm industrial-style-home-office-warm Concrete, metal, and exposed brick can feel cozy with the right balance of texture and light. Industrial spaces are known for raw materials and generous ceilings, but without careful layering they can feel cold. We break down how to bring warmth through wood tones, textiles, and accent lighting while keeping the industrial character intact. We include a detailed breakdown of one of our recent industrial office projects, from early concept sketches to final styling. office industrial workspace blog_scandinavian_living_rooms Scandinavian Living Rooms: 5 Client Before-and-Afters scandinavian-living-rooms-before-after See how we transformed dark, cramped living rooms into light-filled Scandinavian spaces. pages.dataKeywords: art_anxiety_micro_reset 3 Micro-Resets for Anxiety You Can Do Anywhere micro-resets-for-anxiety-anywhere Short, science-backed movements and breaths you can use anytime rising anxiety starts to take over. When anxiety spikes in the middle of your day, you rarely have time for a full class. In this guide, you’ll learn three micro-resets that take less than two minutes each: a grounding foot scan you can do at your desk, a box-breathing variation that fits into a meeting break, and a simple shoulder and neck release you can practice while waiting for your coffee. Each reset is designed to interrupt the anxiety spiral, bring attention back into the body, and signal safety to your nervous system... tips anxiety stress_relief micro-practices desk_break grounding Jordan Alvarez art_anxiety_body_scan Evening Body Scan to Ease Anxiety Before Bed evening-body-scan-to-ease-anxiety Use this 10-minute body scan routine to unwind lingering anxiety and prepare your mind for rest. Anxiety rarely clocks out when your workday ends. In fact, many people notice their thoughts speeding up as soon as things get quiet. This guided body scan sequence is designed to meet you at that moment. Over 10 minutes, you’ll move through gentle stretches, stillness, and breath cues that help your nervous system downshift from alert to at-ease. We’ll start at the feet, work slowly up to the jaw and eyes, and finish with a simple breathing pattern you can repeat in bed... guides evening body_scan sleep gentle_movement Samira Chen art_sleep_wind_down_stretch Sleep Rituals: A 10-Minute Wind-Down Stretch sleep-rituals-10-minute-wind-down-stretch A gentle floor-based routine to signal to your body that it’s safe to fall asleep. Creating a consistent wind-down ritual trains your body to recognize when it’s time to rest. This guide walks you through a 10-minute, low-effort stretching sequence you can do beside your bed. We’ll start with a supported child’s pose, move into a reclining twist, and finish with legs-up-the-wall for nervous system regulation. You’ll also learn how to pair each shape with simple breath counts to deepen the relaxation response... stretch_restore bedtime rituals Maya Lee art_sleep_calm_morning Waking Up Calm: Gentle Movement for Better Sleep Cycles waking-up-calm-gentle-movement-for-better-sleep What you do in the first 15 minutes of your day might matter more for sleep than what you do at night. Improving sleep isn’t only about winding down. Morning light exposure, gentle movement, and a consistent wake time all support your circadian rhythm. In this article, we explore a short, 15-minute mindful movement routine you can practice after waking. The sequence combines breath-led stretches, slow joint circles, and a brief standing balance that cue alertness without overstimulation... morning circadian_rhythm Nadia Patel art_anxiety_breath_cycle A 4-4-6 Breath Cycle to Steady Anxiety 4-4-6-breath-cycle-to-steady-anxiety Learn a simple breathing pattern that you can pa

pages.dataKeywords: event_1 Thursday Evening In-Person Parkinson’s Support Meeting Free in-person evening support meeting for people living with Parkinson’s and their care partners. Join our Thursday evening Parkinson’s support meeting for people living with Parkinson’s and their care partners. We share updates, coping strategies, and local resources. Light refreshments are provided. This meeting is facilitated by a licensed social worker and is open to all stages and ages. support_meeting in_person both all_stages all_ages emotional_support local_resources peer_support biweekly Schenectady Community Health Center 123 Wellness Way Schenectady Free parking in the rear lot. Wheelchair accessible entrance on the east side. event_2 Downtown Evening Parkinson’s Support Meeting Monthly free in-person support meeting in downtown Schenectady. This downtown support meeting offers a welcoming space to talk about daily life with Parkinson’s, medications, and local services. Family members and care partners are welcome. The group is facilitated by volunteer peer leaders. community medications monthly Downtown Senior Center 50 Main St Room 204 Use side entrance after 5pm; elevator access available. event_3 Tuesday Balance & Gait Exercise Class In-person balance and gait training class focused on fall prevention. A small-group exercise class tailored for people with Parkinson’s, focusing on balance, gait, and posture to help reduce fall risk. Led by a physical therapist with Parkinson’s expertise. Please wear comfortable clothing and supportive shoes. exercise_class exercise people_with_parkinsons early_stage balance fall_prevention weekly Parkinson’s Movement Studio 200 Movement Ln Suite B Studio is on the second floor; elevator available. Please arrive 10 minutes early. event_4 Living Well with Parkinson’s: Evening Education Workshop Online workshop on staying active, staying connected, and planning ahead. In this interactive online workshop, a neurologist and social worker discuss practical ways to live well with Parkinson’s, including exercise, social engagement, mood, and planning ahead. There will be time for Q&A. Ideal for people within the first 5 years of diagnosis and their care partners. workshop education_workshop online education planning emotional_health none Online via Zoom Zoom link will be sent by email after registration. event_5 Monday Morning Parkinson’s Yoga Gentle chair and standing yoga for Parkinson’s, Monday mornings. A gentle yoga class designed specifically for people living with Parkinson’s. The class includes breath work, seated and supported standing poses, and a relaxation segment. Modifications are provided for different mobility levels. hybrid flexibility mind_body Riverfront Wellness Studio 18 Riverfront Dr Masks optional; mats and chairs provided. Zoom option available. event_6 Wednesday Tai Chi for Balance Weekly Wednesday morning Tai Chi for balance and coordination. Slow, flowing movements to support balance, flexibility, and relaxation. pages.dataKeywords: prepare_doctor_questions Prepare questions for your next doctor visit Make a list of questions and concerns to discuss at your next oncology appointment, including treatment options, side effects, and logistics. medical_preparation newly_diagnosed_patient caregiver understanding_treatment_options managing_side_effects planning_logistics attend_support_group_meeting Attend a support group meeting Join a cancer support group meeting—either in person or online—to connect with others who understand what you’re going through. support_connection long_term_survivor emotional_support reducing_isolation take_20_minute_walk Take a 20-minute walk Engage in light physical activity, such as a 20-minute walk, to support your mood, sleep, and overall wellbeing. Adjust duration as needed. physical_activity in_treatment_patient stress_management organize_medication_schedule Organize your medication schedule Create or update a simple schedule for your medications, including doses, times, and special instructions. planning_paperwork treatment_adherence practice_relaxation_breathing Practice 10 minutes of relaxation breathing Use a guided breathing exercise or timer to practice slow, deep breathing to reduce anxiety and tension. self_care review_insurance_and_bills Review recent medical bills and insurance Set aside time to review recent bills and insurance explanations of benefits, noting any questions for a financial counselor. financial_planning reducing_anxiety phone_helpline_main phone_helpline Phone Helpline Talk with an oncology social worker or trained counselor by phone for emotional support, coping strategies, and resource referrals. Real-time conversation with a trained professional Helpful if you prefer to talk rather than type Accessible from any phone, including landlines May have limited hours based on time zone Not ideal if you need to keep conversations private in shared spaces online_chat_counseling online_chat Online Chat Counseling Connect with a counselor through secure text-based chat for brief emotional support and practical guidance. Discreet and can be used from work or shared spaces Easy to save or screenshot messages for later reference No phone required Typing may feel slower than talking for some people Requires a stable internet connection email_support_general email_support Email Support Send non-urgent questions and receive a written response from a support specialist within 2–3 business days. Write at your own pace Useful for complex questions that need detailed answers Creates a written record you can refer back to Not suitable for crisis or urgent support Response times vary based on volume in_person_counseling_center in_person_counseling In-Person Counseling Meet face-to-face with an oncology counselor at our main support center for deeper, ongoing support. Enables more in-depth conversations Can include family members or caregivers Supports non-verbal connection and presence Available only in select locations Requires transportat

BandDad rev_003 Reliable live mixer No issues after six months of weekly use. Faders still feel smooth and all channels work perfectly. Zero hiss through our powered speakers. pages.dataKeywords:
react-native-performance-2026-practical-guide React Native Performance in 2026: A Practical Tuning Guide A step-by-step guide to profiling and optimizing React Native apps in 2026, from startup time to navigation and list rendering. Marta Nowak react native performance mobile profiling optimization react-native-performance-fintech-2024 Scaling React Native Performance for High-Traffic Fintech Apps How we reduced interaction latency by 42% in a production React Native fintech app handling millions of API calls per day. David Kim fintech case study react-native-startup-time-2023 Improving React Native Startup Time in Large-Scale Apps improving-react-native-startup-time-large-scale-apps A deep dive into reducing cold start time in React Native apps with code splitting, lazy initialization, and smarter bundling. Cold start time is one of the most visible performance metrics for users. In this article, we explain how we reduced cold start time from 2.8s to 1.4s on mid-range Android devices in a large React Native app.

We cover code splitting strategies, lazy loading non-critical features, optimizing image loading, and removing unnecessary polyfills. We show how to measure improvements using custom markers in performance traces and how to keep startup time under control as the app grows. Priya Desai startup time react-native-architecture-2022 Choosing the Right Architecture for Your React Native App choosing-react-native-architecture MVC, MVVM, clean architecture, and modularization patterns for React Native teams at different scales. React Native offers a lot of flexibility in how you structure your app. In this article, we compare several architectural styles and show how to evolve from a small prototype to a modular, scalable codebase. While performance is not the main focus, we also highlight how certain decisions can help or hurt runtime characteristics. Liam O'Connor architecture scaling aws-kubernetes-cost-optimization-2025 Cost-Optimizing AWS + Kubernetes for SaaS Platforms cost-optimizing-aws-kubernetes-saas Practical techniques for reducing cloud spend in containerized B2B SaaS platforms without sacrificing reliability. For many SaaS companies, AWS and Kubernetes costs are among the top line items. We discuss right-sizing nodes, implementing autoscaling, and choosing the right storage and networking options. We also show how we saved 32% of monthly cloud costs for a client while improving reliability. Jonas Müller kubernetes devops saas b2b-saas-product-discovery-2024 A 3-Phase Product Discovery Playbook for B2B SaaS product-discovery-playbook-b2b-saas How we structure product discovery workshops for complex B2B SaaS platforms. We outline our 3-phase product discovery approach—understand, explore, and define—and show how it applies to B2B SaaS initiatives. We share agendas, exercises, and artifacts for each phase, including stakeholder maps, opportunity solution trees, and prioritized roadmaps. Sara Lindholm product discovery b2b saas worksh

ba_math_anxiety_preschool_strategies Easing Math Anxiety in Preschoolers: 7 Playful Strategies easing-math-anxiety-in-preschoolers-7-playful-strategies How to spot early signs of math anxiety in preschool and use games, stories, and routines to help young children feel calm and confident with numbers. Dr. Lina Chen math_anxiety preschool young_children parent_support parent math anxiety young children fear of numbers parent tips confidence published ba_math_anxiety_young_children_explained When Numbers Feel Scary: Understanding Math Anxiety in Young Children when-numbers-feel-scary-understanding-math-anxiety-in-young-children A research-informed overview of math anxiety in young children, with simple checklists for parents and preschool teachers. Sam Patel educator_tips mixed preschool anxiety teacher strategies parent guide ba_math_anxiety_classroom_prek_k Designing Pre-K & Kindergarten Math Centers That Reduce Anxiety designing-prek-kindergarten-math-centers-that-reduce-anxiety Practical tips for preschool and kindergarten teachers to design math centers that feel safe for anxious learners. kindergarten classroom_environment educator math centers pre-k anxiety classroom design ba_counting_confidence_ages_3_4 Building Counting Confidence at Home for Ages 3–4 building-counting-confidence-at-home-ages-3-4 Simple, low-prep routines that help 3- and 4-year-olds count, compare, and enjoy playing with numbers. You don’t need fancy materials to build strong counting skills. In this article, we show you how to turn snack time, bath time, and walks around the block into rich counting practice.

We focus on three big ideas: saying number words in order, matching numbers to objects (one-to-one correspondence), and comparing small quantities. Each idea includes quick games, example questions, and tips for adjusting the challenge level based on your child’s comfort. Jordan Rivera counting ages_3_4 parent_tips age 3 age 4 parent activities ba_playful_patterns_preschool Playful Patterns: Why Repeating Designs Matter in Preschool Math playful-patterns-why-repeating-designs-matter-in-preschool-math Discover how simple pattern games with blocks, beads, and sounds build a foundation for algebraic thinking. Patterns are more than pretty designs—they’re an early form of algebra. When children notice and extend patterns, they’re practicing prediction, reasoning, and flexible thinking.

We share a progression of pattern activities from toddlerhood through kindergarten, ideas for pattern talk, and ways to support children who struggle to see “what comes next.” patterns number_sense preschool math algebraic thinking ba_everyday_math_talk_parents Everyday Math Talk: A Parent’s Guide for Ages 3–6 everyday-math-talk-a-parents-guide-ages-3-6 How to weave rich math conversations into routines like cooking, laundry, and playtime—without turning life into a worksheet. pages.dataKeywords: first-audition-beginner-tips Your First Audition: 12 Beginner-Friendly Tips your-first-audition-12-beginner-friendly-tips Stepping into the audition room for the first time can be intimidating. These 12 beginner-friendly tips will help you feel prepared, grounded, and ready to play. beginner new_to_acting auditions audition_tips Sam Rivera audition-room-etiquette-new-actors Audition Room Etiquette for New Actors audition-room-etiquette-for-new-actors From signing in to saying thank you, learn the unspoken rules of audition room etiquette so you can make a professional first impression. Casting teams notice more than just your performance. They notice how you enter the room, how you listen, and how you treat the staff. This article walks you through audition room etiquette step by step: arriving early without hovering, using your phone respectfully, greeting the reader and casting director, handling your sides, and making a clean exit. We’ll also cover Zoom and self-tape etiquette for on-camera auditions. Riley James overcoming-audition-nerves Overcoming Audition Nerves: A Coach’s 5-Minute Reset Use this five-minute reset before any audition—stage or on-camera—to turn nervous energy into usable focus. Even experienced actors get nervous before auditions. The goal isn’t to eliminate nerves, but to channel them. In this article, on-camera coach Riley James shares a five-minute reset you can do in the hallway, your car, or even a bathroom stall. You’ll learn a simple breathing pattern, a physical shake-out, a grounding visualization, and one question that will bring you back to playable action every time. mindset on_camera beginner-acting-foundation-steps 6 Practical Steps to Build Your Acting Foundation 6-practical-steps-to-build-your-acting-foundation Not sure where to start as a new actor? These six steps will help you build a solid foundation without getting overwhelmed. Starting an acting journey can feel like drinking from a firehose. Do you focus on scene study, improv, voice, or movement first? In this guide, we break down six practical steps for new actors: grounding your breath and body, learning to listen, understanding objectives and actions, building a weekly practice, filming yourself regularly, and seeking feedback in a structured way. acting_foundations Amira Lee voice-warmups-for-auditions Quick Voice Warm-Ups You Can Do in the Waiting Room quick-voice-warmups-waiting-room No piano, no privacy, no problem. These discreet vocal warm-ups will get your voice ready for the audition without drawing attention. You arrive early to your audition—but there’s nowhere private to warm up. Rather than skipping your voice work, use these discreet exercises. We’ll cover silent yawns, breath-focused humming, tongue and lip trills under your breath, and light articulation drills you can do behind a mask or in a hallway. voice Sofia Martin what-is-scene-study What Is Scene Study and When Should You Start? what-is-scene-study-and-when-to-start Scene st

pages.dataKeywords: ja_oldkingdom_2019 Reassessing Old Kingdom Tomb Architecture at Giza Spatial Organization and Elite Memory This article reexamines the architecture of Old Kingdom mastaba tombs at Giza with a focus on spatial organization, access control, and the role of decorated spaces in the construction of elite memory. Using newly digitized survey data and 3D models, it demonstrates that tomb layout correlates closely with kinship networks and evolving mortuary cult practices. Miriam El-Sayed Thomas P. Hendrickson Journal of Egyptian Archaeology 145-182 Old Kingdom tombs Giza mastaba mortuary cult elite burial funerary architecture open_access articles 10.1234/jea.2019.105.2.145 ja_oldkingdom_2021 New Discoveries in Old Kingdom Tombs at Saqqara Results of the 2017–2019 Seasons Presenting the results of three excavation seasons at North Saqqara, this study reports on newly uncovered Old Kingdom tombs belonging to middle-ranking officials. The article details their architectural features, inscriptions, and material culture, and discusses implications for understanding provincial administration and mortuary practice. Hassan Mahmoud Linda K. Roberts Omar Abdel Rahman Egyptian Archaeological Reports 1-64 Saqqara archaeological reports provincial administration funerary inscriptions 10.5678/ear.2021.7.1.1 ja_oldkingdom_2023 Iconography and Identity in Old Kingdom Tomb Chapels A Quantitative Study of Tomb Decoration Programs Using a database of 220 decorated Old Kingdom tomb chapels, this article applies quantitative methods to examine correlations between iconographic themes, titles, and tomb locations. It argues that visual programs were carefully calibrated to communicate social identity and local ritual expectations. Sarah J. Klein Studies in Ancient Egyptian Art 201-246 iconography tomb chapels social identity art history 10.9012/saea.2023.12.3.201 ja_2018_oldkingdom_tombs Rock-Cut Tombs of the Late Old Kingdom in Upper Egypt  This contribution surveys late Old Kingdom rock-cut tombs in Upper Egypt, focusing on regional variation and local workshop practices. Architectural documentation and stylistic analysis support a revised chronological framework for these monuments. Youssef Farag Claudia Meier Egyptian Archaeology Review 55-102 rock-cut tombs Upper Egypt chronology subscription 10.2222/ear.2018.29.1.55 ja_2017_middle_egyptian_grammar Recent Approaches to Teaching Middle Egyptian Grammar Blended Learning in the Egyptology Classroom This article evaluates blended learning strategies for teaching Middle Egyptian grammar at the university level. Based on classroom experiments over three semesters, it assesses the effectiveness of digital exercises and automated feedback in improving student outcomes. Daniel R. Collins Journal of Egyptological Pedagogy 23-52 Middle Egyptian grammar language teaching digital humanities pedagogy 10.3344/jep.2017.3.1.23 ja_2020_coptic_language_corpus Building a Digital Corpus for Coptic Language Studies Methodological Reflections

pages.dataKeywords: cg_restaurant_fire_safety Restaurant Fire Safety & Hood Suppression Compliance Guide restaurant-fire-safety-hood-suppression-compliance-guide Step-by-step guidance to help restaurants meet local fire code, hood suppression, and egress requirements. restaurant published cg_commercial_kitchen_hood Commercial Kitchen Hood & Fire Suppression Code Checklist commercial-kitchen-hood-fire-suppression-code-checklist Detailed checklist for commercial kitchen hood systems, suppression equipment, and interlocks. Commercial kitchens must comply with stringent codes governing exhaust hoods, ducts, and fire suppression systems. This guide walks through UL 300 and NFPA 96 related requirements in plain language, including nozzle coverage over appliances, manual pull station placement, gas and power shutoff interlocks, and semi-annual inspection scheduling. It also covers documentation of service tags and what to expect during an AHJ (Authority Having Jurisdiction) inspection. commercial_kitchen cg_warehouse_storage Warehouse Fire Protection & Storage Configuration Guide warehouse-fire-protection-storage-configuration-guide Fire safety guidance for warehouses, including sprinklers, detection, and high-piled storage layouts. Warehouses face unique risks due to high fuel loads and varying rack configurations. This guide explains how fire alarm and sprinkler design interacts with storage heights and commodities. Topics include detector spacing in open warehouse areas, manual pull station locations at exits, notification appliance placement in noisy environments, and special considerations for high-piled and rack storage. A worksheet is included to document square footage by hazard area. warehouse cg_office_code_compliance Office Fire Alarm & Emergency Egress Compliance Guide office-fire-alarm-emergency-egress-compliance-guide Overview of fire alarm, notification, and egress requirements for office buildings and suites. This guide covers typical code expectations for office occupancies, including where fire alarm systems are required, minimum device coverage for corridors and open office areas, and spacing for notification appliances. It explains exit sign visibility requirements, emergency lighting illumination levels, and stairwell signage. Templates for occupant emergency plans and annual fire drill records are provided. office cg_apartment_life_safety Apartment Building Fire & Life Safety Compliance Overview apartment-building-fire-life-safety-compliance-overview Fire and life safety requirements for multi-story apartment buildings, including detection and notification. Multi-family apartment buildings must balance individual unit protection with building-wide systems. This guide explains requirements for smoke alarms in sleeping rooms and common areas, the difference between single-station detectors and system-connected detectors, and where carbon monoxide detection may be required. It reviews corridor and stairwell notification, fire door signage, and recordkeep

pages.dataKeywords: art_pocus_ed_masterclass POCUS in the ED: A 60-Minute Masterclass for Rapid Decision Making pocus-ed-60-minute-masterclass A structured approach to using point-of-care ultrasound in the emergency department for triage, shock, and procedural guidance. This masterclass article walks through core emergency POCUS applications including RUSH exam, eFAST, basic cardiac views, and procedural guidance. It includes image examples, probe positioning tips, and checklists that can be applied immediately in clinical practice. POCUS Emergency Ultrasound RUSH eFAST art_pocus_abdominal_beginner Beginner’s Guide to Abdominal POCUS for Acute Abdominal Pain beginner-abdominal-pocus-acute-pain Step-by-step guidance for using POCUS to evaluate patients with acute abdominal pain in urgent and emergency settings. This article outlines probe selection, patient positioning, and a reproducible abdominal POCUS scanning sequence. It covers biliary, renal, AAA, and free-fluid assessment with tips on avoiding common pitfalls. Abdominal Ultrasound Emergency Medicine art_pocus_critical_care_rounds POCUS on ICU Rounds: Integrating Ultrasound into Daily Practice pocus-icu-rounds-integration How to structure daily ICU rounds around focused cardiac, lung, and abdominal ultrasound exams. Focusing on workflow, this article shows how intensivists can embed POCUS into morning rounds to assess hemodynamics, volume status, lung aeration, and abdominal pathology, with documentation templates for the EMR. Critical Care Lung Ultrasound Cardiac Ultrasound art_pocus_mistakes Ten Common POCUS Mistakes and How to Avoid Them ten-common-pocus-mistakes A practical review of the most frequent interpretation and acquisition errors in POCUS and strategies to reduce them. From gain settings to off-axis cardiac views and misinterpreting reverberation artifacts, this article pairs real-image examples with corrective techniques and teaching pearls. Quality Assurance Education art_pocus_renal_ed Point-of-Care Renal Ultrasound for ED Clinicians pocus-renal-ultrasound-ed Protocol-based approach to renal POCUS for hydronephrosis and urinary retention. Covers scanning planes, grading hydronephrosis, differentiating cysts from solid masses, and integrating findings into ED decision-making. Renal art_abdominal_biliary_pearls Biliary Ultrasound Pearls for the On-Call Resident biliary-ultrasound-pearls-on-call Quick pearls to improve detection of cholelithiasis and cholecystitis on ultrasound. Highlights sonographic Murphy sign, wall thickness cutoffs, and differentiating sludge from stones using multiple patient cases. Gallbladder Residency art_cardiac_intermediate_views Intermediate Cardiac Ultrasound: Beyond the Four Basic Views intermediate-cardiac-ultrasound-views A guide to RV-focused, LVOT, and advanced subcostal views for shock and dyspnea. Discusses when advanced cardiac windows add value, including RV strain, LVOT VTI, and pericardial effusion characterization. Advanced art_msk_ultrasound_basics Musculo
